For more complex manufacturing environments, you can use job card terminals on the production floor or the Time and attendance module in Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management, providing a comprehensive overview of their work hours and production completed. Supervisors can then efficiently review and approve timesheets, ensuring accuracy and compliance.
 When you use Dynamics 365 Field Service or Project Operations, you can record time against specific work orders or projects for detailed time reporting that can be easily allocated to the right financial resources for billing, cost recognition, and revenue recognition. The flexibility of the Dynamics 365 platform also allows for easy adjustments and corrections to timesheets, ensuring that the time and attendance data remains accurate and reflective of the dynamic nature of business operations.
 Determining the high-level architecture of your solution early in the implementation for your time and attendance business processes is important, but you can leave the configuration and setup to the later stages of your project.

l2Processes.title: Manage compensation and benefits
l2Processes.description:
In today's competitive business landscape, attracting, retaining, and developing a top-performing workforce is essential for organizational success. Effective management of worker compensation, benefits, and payroll is a critical component of this strategy. It ensures that employees are compensated fairly and receive the benefits they deserve while also streamlining administrative processes and reducing costs. Dynamics 365 provides a comprehensive set of tools to streamline and enhance various aspects of worker compensation, benefits, and payroll management, from creating customized compensation plans including employee's performance rating and managing benefit enrollments to processing payroll accurately and efficiently.
 Managing worker compensation, benefits, and payroll processes serves two crucial purposes in any organization. Firstly, they ensure the accurate and timely payment of employee wages, benefits, and compensation, maintaining employee satisfaction and reducing financial risks. Secondly, they help organizations comply with labor laws and regulations, minimize legal liabilities, and promote a transparent and compliant workplace culture. Dynamics 365 implementations streamline and enhance these processes, providing a centralized platform for managing employee compensation, benefits, and payroll data securely and efficiently.

l2Processes.title: Manage warehouse operations
l2Processes.description:
The inventory to deliver business process involves managing the flow of goods from receipt into a warehouse or distribution center through to delivery to the customer. This end-to-end process includes receipt of goods, quality management, inventory management, inventory costing, order fulfillment, and shipping.
 Important: The process defined here doesn't include the procurement process, which involves purchasing raw materials or finished goods from suppliers. It only includes the receipt of goods in the warehouse. The process also doesn't include production, which involves converting raw materials into sub-assemblies or finished goods. This process does include the issuing of raw materials into production and the receipt of finished goods into inventory.
 Every organization has variations to the inventory to deliver process. Here, we define the basic outline for any organization looking to implement a technology solution to support their inventory to deliver business processes.

l2Processes.title: Maintain inventory levels
l2Processes.description:
Maintaining inventory levels in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps covers
tracking on-hand quantities, setting up min/max thresholds, automatic
replenishment, inventory journals for adjustments, and supporting cycle and
physical counts. It enables real-time visibility and control over stock,
supports ABC classification, and provides tools for inventory valuation and
reconciliation.

l2Processes.title: Manage accounts payable

apqc.description: Process accounts payable and expense reimbursements

l2Processes.description:
A vendor invoice is generated upon receiving products or services as per a purchase order. It consists of a header and item or service lines, marking the last step in the purchase process. Some invoices may not correspond to purchase orders, like ongoing service bills. There are various methods to enter vendor invoices, including, vendor invoice approval journal, vendor invoice journal for non-purchase order invoices, and using the vendor invoice pool for expense accrual.
 Additionally, you can create vendor invoices based on confirmed purchase orders using the Open vendor invoices and Pending vendor invoices pages.
 The vendor invoices business process area should be defined and incorporated into the overall implementation. This phase typically occurs at the beginning of a project's lifecycle and involves establishing project goals, defining project scope, and developing a detailed project plan. The setup and configuration of your products and base pricing along with the general procurement process should be in place before the vendor invoice process.

l2Processes.title: Process inbound goods
l2Processes.description:
Processing inbound goods in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps includes
purchase order receipt, advanced ship notice (ASN) handling, receiving against
packing lists, inspection, quarantine, put-away, and handling discrepancies
(short/over/damaged). It supports receiving by PO, transfer order, or direct
journal, and can automate workflows for quality checks and vendor scorecarding.

l2Processes.title: Process outbound goods
l2Processes.description:
Processing outbound goods in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps covers
sales order picking, packing, shipping, transfer orders, and crew requisitions.
It supports batch and order picking, packing station management, carrier
integration, and real-time inventory deduction. It can automate backorder
management and provide shipment tracking.

l2Processes.title: Manage inventory quality
l2Processes.description:
Managing inventory quality in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps includes
quality order creation, inspection, quarantine, batch/serial tracking,
expiration management, and handling returns. It supports automated quality
checks, batch disposition, and integration with vendor scorecards and compliance
reporting.

l2Processes.title: Manage freight and transportation
l2Processes.description:
The Manage freight and transportation business process area encompasses the activities that are done in an organization's transportation management process. All distribution organizations must be able to track and manage transportation costs and operations so that they can optimize this critical part of the supply chain. Meeting this goal requires cross-organization collaboration among departments such as Logistics, Procurement, Planning, Sales, and Operations.
 The exact process for the Manage freight and transportation area varies from business to business and depends on the industry. However, the basic steps are the same: The need for transportation is identified. Identification of this need triggers planning activities, including rating and routing operations. Dock appointments can then be scheduled for pickup and/or delivery, and necessary logistics paperwork can be generated. After transportation is completed, freight invoices can be processed and reconciled against estimates. The definition of these steps is done as part of the Inventory to deliver end-to-end business processes and refined in the Define and manage warehouse operations business process area.
 It's essential that you define and establish the business process for Managing freight and transportation when you first begin to implement Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management. This process is a fundamental element for any distribution company, because it forms the backbone of efficient supply chain operations.

l2Processes.title: Analyze warehouse operations
l2Processes.description:
Analyzing warehouse operations in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
includes reporting and analytics on inventory levels, movement, picking/put-away
performance, cycle count accuracy, order fulfillment, and warehouse
productivity. It leverages embedded Power BI, configurable dashboards, and
supports custom KPIs.

l2Processes.title: Develop sales policies
l2Processes.description: The process area refers to the structured definition and governance of rules, strategies, and procedures that guide how an organization sells its products or services.  Core Components of Developing Sales Policies Sales Strategy Definition: This includes setting the overall direction for sales efforts such as defining target markets, value propositions, and competitive positioning. It ensures that sales activities align with business growth objectives. Sales Channels and Teams: Organizations define which channels (e.g., direct, partner, digital) they will use and how sales teams are structured. This includes assigning roles, territories, and responsibilities. Sales Targets and Incentives: Policies are established to determine sales quotas, performance metrics, and compensation structures. These are often aligned with financial planning and HR systems to ensure consistency and motivation. Sales Process Governance: This involves defining the steps from lead qualification to quote generation and order fulfillment. It ensures consistency in how opportunities are pursued and closed Compliance and Risk Management: Sales policies also address regulatory compliance, pricing controls, discounting rules, and approval workflows to reduce risk and ensure accountability. Implementation in Dynamics 365 Microsoft's guidance outlines how Dynamics 365 supports this process area through modules like Sales, Customer Insights, and Finance: Process Flow Mapping: The Define a sales strategy process flow includes subprocesses such as: Define sales channels Define sales team Determine sales targets Establish commission and incentive structures Define sales process Cross-Functional Integration: The process connects with other business areas like: Hire to Retire (for workforce planning) Forecast to Plan (for demand planning) Record to Report (for budgeting and financial alignment) Benefits of Implementation: Strategic alignment between sales execution and business goals Increased productivity through specialization Motivated teams via rewards and recognition

l2Processes.title: Manage sales orders
l2Processes.description: Before you start selling your products and services to customers, it's critical that you establish your business pricing and contracts. Depending on the types of customers you sell to, the process steps and stakeholders vary. For example, in a business-to-business (B2B) model, sales managers may be responsible for establishing and communicating your prices. In a business-to-consumer (B2C) model, pricing might be defined by merchandising, sales leadership, or company policy and published on your website. After you establish your prices and contracts, you can start recording your sales in Dynamics 365 Sales, Dynamics 365 Commerce, or Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management, depending on your business requirements. The Dynamics 365 product family supports many sales models or channels, such as brick-and-mortar, e-commerce, call center, B2B, B2C, and more. An organization may be geographically dispersed or have more than one line of business. For example, a retail organization may have a manufacturing plant to produce some items and distribution centers that combine manufactured and purchased items. An organization may offer services and sell products to support them or sell products and offer after-sales services. An organization may also trade products, services, and resources across companies. Dynamics 365 supports all these scenarios. Learn about intercompany planning in Supply Chain Management or how to manage intercompany transactions in Business Central.

l2Processes.title: Manage accounts receivable
l2Processes.description: The 65.30 Manage Accounts Receivable process area encompasses all activities related to customer billing, revenue recognition, and financial settlement. It supports organizations in maintaining accurate financial records, ensuring timely payments, and managing customer-related financial transactions. This process excludes credit and collections, which are handled separately. Core Subprocesses Issue Sales Invoices Generate invoices from traditional sales orders, free text invoices, service orders, and project-based billing. Support integration with external systems for automated invoice distribution and compliance [1].   Issue Customer Credits Handle credits for returns, overbilling, service issues, and price matching. Credits may be tied to sales orders, free text invoices, or issued as standalone memos [2].   Subscription Billing Manage recurring billing cycles for subscriptions, including contract terms, renewals, and adjustments.   Revenue Recognition Apply recognition schedules for deferred revenue across products, services, and long-term contracts. Support stubbed entries and historical migration for in-flight contracts [4].   Process Payments Record incoming payments from customers across channels. Apply payments to open invoices and manage payment journals.   Handle Prepayments Accept and apply customer prepayments to future invoices or orders.   Issue Refunds Process refunds for overpayments, returns, or service cancellations.   Settle Transactions Match payments to invoices, apply adjustments, and reconcile accounts.   Write Off Bad Debt Identify and write off uncollectible receivables in compliance with financial policies.   Process Customer Rebates Apply approved incentive transactions and validate rebate claims. Ensure accurate financial reflection of rebates.   Manage Trade Allowances Define and apply bill-backs, off-invoice discounts, promotional funds, lump sums, and traditional rebates.   Manage Sales Commissions Calculate and distribute commissions based on invoiced sales, returns, and adjustments.   Manage Bills of Exchange Handle promissory notes and other instruments used for customer payments.    Dynamics 365 Support Finance & Business CentralCore invoicing, credit issuance, payment processing, and revenue recognition.   Project OperationsProject-based billing and service-related credits.   CommercePOS integration for refunds, credits, and invoice generation.   Electronic InvoicingIntegration with external systems for regulatory compliance and automation.

l2Processes.title: Manage credit and collections
l2Processes.description:
In many organizations, not least organizations with a business-to-business sales model, setting credit limits is an important process that helps organizations remain competitive. Credit limits can also help an organization minimize bad debt and the need to write off unpaid balances. When you manage the collection process carefully, it helps you get customers pay on time, increases the cash flow, and helps make the cash flow of the organization more predictable.
 The process for monitoring customer credit and collections often splits into two main focuses in a Dynamics 365 project: The first is around the master data configuration of your customers and the establishment of the credit limits, payment terms, and so on, for your customers. The second is the monitoring of orders and invoices for your customers to ensure that customers remain within their credit limits and rules your business has established to ensure that you can reduce the risk for fraudulent orders and accounts.   Dynamics 365 Finance includes functionality to help you manage your customer master data including establishing credit limits, credit scores, and so on. The Finance app can also help you monitor orders for fraudulent activity, reconcile the credit limits to the customers' balances, and place orders or customers on hold based on your business rules.
 Large retailers, not least retailers that primarily do business online with consumers, typically have credit or fraud departments that monitor sales for fraudulent activity. Dynamics 365 Fraud Protection includes functionality to help customers manage fraud in three key pillars including account protection, loss prevention, and purchase protection. Fraud Protection uses artificial intelligence (AI) to help protect your business from fraud.

l2Processes.title: Analyze sales performance
l2Processes.description:
Analyze sales performance provides a disciplined, data‑driven cadence for measuring sales outcomes and diagnosing the drivers across accounts receivable health, cash flow, customer risk, revenue, and the effectiveness of order management processes.
 Scope and objectives Establish a single view of sales performance across channels, regions, products, and customer segments. Translate operational signals (orders, fulfillment, returns, cancellations, holds) into financial impact (revenue, margins, DSO, cash position). Detect emerging risk in AR and customer credit, and surface proactive actions for collections, pricing, and policy changes. Provide an executive and operational rhythm for continuous improvement of order management processes.  Key analysis areas Sales performance: bookings vs. billings, revenue recognition, margin mix, product/channel/region performance, promo impact. Accounts receivable: aging, DSO, promise‑to‑pay effectiveness, write‑off trends, dispute root causes, concentration risk. Cash flow: collections velocity, forecasted cash, variance vs. plan, impact of payment terms and early‑pay discounts. Customer risk: credit utilization, delinquency, fraud signals, chargebacks/NSF, exposure by tier/segment. Order management health: order cycle times, hold rates and reasons, change/cancel rates, returns/RMA drivers, fulfillment SLAs.  High - level process Define metrics and targetsStandardize KPIs, thresholds, and definitions (e.g., revenue vs. gross sales, DSO formula, “on‑time fulfillment”).   Assemble the data foundationLand sales, order, AR, cash, pricing, and policy data; ensure harmonized calendars, currencies, and dimensions.   Produce the executive and operational dashboardsBuild scorecards for leaders and diagnostic views for sales ops, AR, and order management teams.   Profile performance and variancesCompare actuals vs. plan and prior periods; isolate variance drivers (volume, mix, price, returns, timing).   Diagnose AR and cash driversAttribute DSO changes to disputes, promises, aging buckets, terms shifts, and customer/segment mix.   Evaluate customer riskRefresh risk scores, credit utilization, and delinquency trends; flag concentrations and escalating exposure.   Inspect order management processesAnalyze holds, lead times, changes/cancels, backorders, and returns; connect bottlenecks to revenue and cash impact.   Recommend and track actionsGenerate prioritized actions (collections sequences, term changes, pricing/promo adjustments, policy updates), assign owners, and set due dates.   Close the loopMeasure action effectiveness, update targets, and incorporate learning into the next review cycle.    Roles and responsibilities Sales leadership: Own sales outcomes, approve corrective actions, and set targets. Sales operations/RevOps: Maintain metrics, perform variance analysis, drive pipeline and pricing insights. Finance/AR: Lead AR, cash, and revenue analytics; partner with collections on promises, disputes, and write‑offs. Order management/SCM operations: Improve order flow, reduce holds and returns, align fulfillment with demand. Risk and credit management: Monitor exposure, adjust limits/policies, and coordinate with collections. Data & analytics: Curate data models, dashboards, and advanced analytics; ensure data quality and governance.  Dynamics 365 support Finance: AR aging, DSO, cash forecasting, revenue recognition, collection letters, interest notes, disputes, business events. Sales: Opportunity/pipeline to bookings, price lists/discounts, forecast accuracy, seller productivity insights. Supply Chain Management & Commerce: Order lifecycle analytics, hold reasons, returns/exchanges, channel performance, promo impact. Customer Insights (Data & Journeys): Segmentation, lifetime value, churn/propensity, campaign attribution for sales. Microsoft Fabric/Power BI: Unified semantic models, scorecards, drill‑through diagnostics, scheduled refresh, row‑level security. Copilot/agents: Narrative summaries of performance, anomaly detection, suggested next‑best actions (e.g., target customers for outreach, policy exceptions to review).  Data and telemetry foundations Master dimensions: calendar, product, customer, region/route‑to‑market, channel, sales org. Conformed measures: revenue, gross margin, returns, AR buckets, write‑offs, DSO, cash collected, order cycle time. Event streams: order created/changed/canceled, hold applied/released, invoice posted, collection letter issued, payment posted, interest note posted, dispute opened/closed.  Operational controls and governance Metric definitions and change control to prevent KPI drift. Segregation of duties for pricing changes, credit limit adjustments, write‑offs, and term overrides. Audit trails for policy changes and manual adjustments. Privacy and retention controls for customer financial data.  Inputs and outputs Inputs: sales orders/invoices, returns, price and discount tables, AR transactions, collections activities, credit limits and exposure, cash receipts, inventory/fulfillment status. Outputs: executive scorecards, variance narratives, prioritized action lists with owners and due dates, refreshed targets, and a closed‑loop action effectiveness log.  KPIs (illustrative) Sales and margin: revenue vs. plan, gross margin %, mix impact, promo ROI, average selling price trends. AR and cash: DSO, current vs. >90‑day balances, promise‑kept rate, dispute rate and time‑to‑resolve, cash forecast accuracy. Customer risk: credit utilization %, high‑risk balance share, delinquency transition rates, chargeback/NSF rate. Order management: order‑to‑invoice cycle time, hold rate and top reasons, change/cancel rate, return rate and top root causes. Process effectiveness: action closure rate, impact per action (revenue/cash), time‑to‑detect/time‑to‑correct anomalies.  Cadence and operating rhythm Weekly operational reviews at team level for actions and blockers. Monthly business reviews for performance vs. plan and policy decisions. Quarterly planning to refine targets, adjust incentives, and align investment to the most material improvement opportunities.  Implementation tips Start with a minimal KPI set tied to decisions, then expand. Favor conformed dimensions and a single semantic model to avoid conflicting truths. Pair diagnostic dashboards with Copilot narratives to accelerate insight consumption. Treat exceptions as productized workflows (e.g., credit limit review, returns spike playbook) with standard owner/SLAs.

l2Processes.title: Develop production strategies

apqc.description: Plan for and align supply chain resources

l2Processes.description:
This process area includes the processes that establish how the organization intends to run, measure, and account for production. By taking the time to map and analyze production processes, the organization can make better decisions based on real data.
 This process area should be implemented whenever any other production processes are implemented. For implementations that include Dynamics 365 Finance and Supply Chain Management, production and other supply chain processes are often part of a second phase. Alternatively, all processes are implemented at once.

l2Processes.title: Plan production operations
l2Processes.description:
Planning production operations picks up where the plan supply and replenishment process area finishes. Production orders are either firmed from the generated planned orders or they're manually created. Then, the orders are organized into a production schedule that often includes sequencing or otherwise organizing production based on efficiency, due dates, and material and machinery availability. Once the production schedule is complete for the next time period, the information is sent to the shop floor, and the run production operations process area can begin.
 This business process area should be included as part of the initial implementation, unless the business is only implementing financial business processes in the first phase. Additionally, some organizations choose to continue with a manual production planning and scheduling process and implement the functionality in Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management in a later phase. The production planning functionality is flexible and can be adapted as the business changes over time.

l2Processes.title: Run production operations
l2Processes.description:
The run production operations business process area covers the activities in an organization's internal production process. Any manufacturing organization finds it critical to track and control production operations. The goal is to ensure consistent product quality and minimize production costs. Running production operations also requires coordination and communication between many departments, such as engineering, operations, quality, sales, planning, and procurement.
 The process for executing production operations varies from business to business, depending on industry and product, but it follows the same basic steps. At the beginning of production, materials are consumed into the process. A combination of people and tools or machines then run the specific production steps. Finally, the sub-assembly or finished good is created as the output of the process. The definition of these steps is done as part of the Design to retire end-to-end business processes and refined further in the define production strategies business process area.
 Define this business process at the beginning of an implementation of Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management. It's a foundational process for any manufacturing company.

l2Processes.title: Control production quality
l2Processes.description:
This process area is highly related to the Manage inventory quality process area in the Inventory to deliver end-to-end process. Controlling production quality covers the processes from inspecting manufactured goods to implementing corrective and preventive actions to improve product quality.
 For regulated industries, this business process area should be considered critical. It should be addressed initially and continuously referenced throughout the implementation to ensure compliance. In other organizations, this process area may be implemented alongside the Run production operations process area. Also, some companies run production quality processes externally to their systems and onboard to Dynamics 365 at a later date. If an organization has requirements to implement the control production quality process area, it's important to identify that as early as possible because it can increase implementation scope. It's also important to recognize that these processes are often executed in "best of breed" specialized systems that are unique by industry. In that case, integrations to such systems may be required as part of the Dynamics 365 implementation.

l2Processes.title: Analyze production operations
l2Processes.description:
Activity is part of the broader effort to optimize and monitor manufacturing
performance.

l2Processes.title: Develop procurement and sourcing strategy
l2Processes.description:
Sourcing and procurement strategies are outlined for both materials and services that are needed within your organization. During this process, the sourcing or procurement teams collaborate with several different departments focusing on three primary areas: price/cost, lead time, and quality.
 This business process area mainly covers:
 Defining procurement process to outline overall procurement strategy for both material and services that are needed within your organization Defining procurement and sourcing policies to reduce financial risk that organization is taking and ensure compliance requirements are met and proper approvals are completed before any procurement transactions can be processed Creating and updating pricing contracts after vendor evaluation and selection, and analyzing product proposals.   Developing procurement and sourcing strategies is a critical step at the beginning of your implementation. However, this will continue to be refined over time as your business and operations change and evolve to support your organization's goals and objectives. It's important to continually evaluate all procurement and sourcing strategies to ensure you're receiving the best pricing, lead time, and quality for your products and services.

l2Processes.title: Manage supplier relationships
l2Processes.description:
This article explains how to use the manage vendor relationships business process in Dynamics 365 Commerce, Finance, Project Operations, and Supply Chain Management. This process is part of the procure to pay end-to-end process. It helps you find, select, and evaluate vendors for your business needs.
 The manage vendor relationships business process area is an essential part of the procure to pay process in Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management. Dynamics 365 includes a comprehensive set of capabilities to help you streamline vendor management. You can use it to:
 Search for potential vendors based on product type, location, price, and other criteria. Approve qualified vendors and create vendor records with all the relevant information. Update vendor records as needed, such as changing bank accounts or payment terms. Monitor vendor performance using various metrics, such as delivery time, quality, and compliance. Collaborate with vendors through a centralized platform that shows purchase orders, invoices, and consignment stock.   The manage vendor relationships business process area helps you optimize your supply chain operations and achieve better business outcomes. You should implement it at the start of your Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management implementation. It's a foundational process for any organization.

l2Processes.title: Source and contract goods and services
l2Processes.description: This process governs the strategic and operational activities involved in identifying, engaging, evaluating, and contracting suppliers to fulfill business needs. It includes issuing requests (RFI, RFQ, RFP), evaluating supplier bids, managing procurement spend, and formalizing contracts for goods and services. The process ensures alignment with sourcing strategies, budget controls, compliance requirements, and performance expectations. Scope & Outcomes Scope Includes: Market exploration and supplier discovery  Issuance of sourcing requests (RFI, RFQ, RFP)  Bid evaluation and supplier selection  Spend governance and escalation workflows  Contracting for goods and services using Microsoft Syntex and Dynamics 365 SCM   Outcomes Include: Qualified supplier pool  Competitive and compliant sourcing decisions  Controlled procurement spend  Digitally managed contracts with searchable metadata  Strategic and transactional supplier relationships  Technology Support Microsoft Syntex: Automates contract classification, metadata extraction, and compliance tagging  Dynamics 365 SCM: Manages purchase agreements, trade terms, and supplier performance  Supplier Collaboration Portal: Facilitates bid submissions, contract uploads, and profile maintenance  Power BI: Provides dashboards for spend analysis, bid scoring, and contract performance  Roles & Responsibilities Procurement Managers: Own sourcing strategy, issue requests, evaluate bids, and approve contracts  Legal & Compliance: Review contract terms, ensure regulatory alignment  Finance/AP: Monitor spend, validate pricing, enforce budget controls  Business Stakeholders: Define requirements, participate in evaluations, and provide feedback  Supplier Onboarding Operations: Manage portal workflows and contract intake

l2Processes.title: Procure goods and services
l2Processes.description:
This process governs the operational execution of purchasing goods and services, including creation and management of purchase requisitions, purchase orders, blanket orders, planned purchases, and returns. It ensures that procurement activities align with approved budgets, sourcing strategies, and supplier contracts. The process supports consolidation of requisitions, automated PO generation, and integration with financial and inventory systems. Scope & Outcomes  Initiates procurement through requisitions and planned purchases  Converts approved requisitions into purchase orders or blanket orders  Manages vendor-specific pricing, delivery terms, and order confirmations  Supports returns and adjustments for non-conforming goods  Consolidates requisitions across departments or cost centers  Ensures compliance with budget limits and approval workflows  Outcomes include accurate PO records, timely deliveries, validated returns, and optimized procurement cycles  Key Components  Purchase Requisitions: Internal requests for goods/services, routed through approval workflows. Can be consolidated across business units or cost centers.  Purchase Orders (POs): Formal orders issued to suppliers, generated manually or automatically from approved requisitions or planned purchases.  Blanket Orders: Long-term agreements with suppliers for recurring purchases, supporting release schedules and quantity tracking.  Planned Purchases: Forecast-driven procurement based on demand planning, linked to production or project schedules.  Returns: Managed through return orders or credit memos for defective, excess, or incorrect goods.  Requisition Consolidation: Combines multiple requests into a single PO to optimize supplier engagement and reduce administrative overhead.  Technology Support  Dynamics 365 SCM
Purchase requisition and PO workflows  Blanket order management  Planned purchase integration with MRP and demand forecasting  Return order processing and inventory adjustments  Budget control and approval routing    Dynamics 365 Business Central
Simplified PO creation and vendor management  Purchase invoice matching and payment processing  Return handling and credit memo generation  Integration with financial dimensions and cost centers    Roles & Responsibilities  Requesters: Initiate requisitions, provide specifications, confirm receipt  Procurement Managers: Review requisitions, issue POs, manage supplier communication  Finance/AP: Validate budget availability, process payments, manage returns  Warehouse/Receiving: Confirm delivery, inspect goods, initiate returns  Category Managers: Oversee blanket orders, planned purchases, and supplier performance  Deliverables  Approved purchase requisitions  Issued purchase orders and blanket agreements  Consolidated requisition reports  Return order documentation  Spend and delivery performance dashboards

l2Processes.title: Develop project strategy

apqc.description: Establish service delivery governance and strategies

l2Processes.description:
The govern projects business process area encompasses the procedures, policies, and structures that are used to manage recurring activities that deliver a particular product or service. Effective management of these processes is crucial for maintaining efficiency, improving productivity, and driving growth. In this context, Dynamics 365 plays a pivotal role. As a comprehensive business solution, it provides robust tools for project governance, so that businesses can streamline their processes, enhance collaboration, and make data-driven decisions.
 The process of defining, scoping, and implementing this business process area in an overall implementation with business apps is a strategic endeavor. Ideally, it should occur during the initial stages of system planning. In this way, you gain a holistic view of the organization's processes and increase the likelihood that the solution is tailored to meet the organization's unique needs.
 Important: Project governance is an iterative process. As the organization evolves, so should its use of Dynamics 365. Conduct regular reviews and updates to ensure that the implementation continues to provide value and meet the changing needs of the business.

l2Processes.title: Manage project contracts
l2Processes.description:
Project contracts serve as a legally binding agreement between a project provider and a client in the project sales process. It outlines the scope, deliverables, timelines, costs, and terms of the project. This document ensures both parties' expectations are aligned, mitigates potential conflicts, and provides a framework for successful project execution. It acts as a safeguard, promoting transparency and accountability throughout the project's lifecycle.
 Effective project contract management faces multifaceted challenges. Navigating complex legal frameworks, ensuring compliance with evolving regulations, and managing diverse stakeholder expectations strains seamless execution. Ambiguities in contract terms and obligations often lead to disputes, demanding meticulous communication and documentation. Integrating digital tools for efficient tracking while fostering clear vendor-client collaboration remains vital. Addressing these challenges fosters successful project delivery, minimizing risks and optimizing outcomes.
 Modern trends in project contract management include a shift towards digitalization and automation, with the integration of contract lifecycle management (CLM) software. The adoption of AI-powered tools for contract analysis and risk assessment, increased emphasis on data security and privacy compliance within contracts, growing integration of blockchain for transparent and tamper-proof contract records, and the utilization of collaborative cloud platforms for real-time updates and communication. These trends aim to enhance efficiency, accuracy, and collaboration while mitigating legal and operational risks in project contract management.
 In conclusion, the project contract stands as the cornerstone of the project sales process, encapsulating terms, deliverables, timelines, and expectations. It safeguards interests, ensuring a clear understanding between parties. Its meticulous formulation cultivates trust, minimizes disputes, and paves the way for successful project execution, fostering enduring client relationships.

l2Processes.title: Plan projects
l2Processes.description:
Resources and the schedule are critical during the project lifecycle. The ability to track these two types of information helps ensure that your project is in a healthy state. To get started, define a baseline at the beginning of your project. Then, during the project's lifecycle, refine that baseline through estimation to complete by adjusting the resources and schedule.
 As a project manager, you should first collect requirements, define the project scope, and plan the project, including all required costs and generated revenue. Once you have all these elements, you can create and define the first budget baseline to follow. During the project lifecycle, you should periodically reassess the resources and schedule to ensure they reflect changes, and validate that your project is profitable according to the baseline.
 This business process should be defined and incorporated into the overall implementation during the project planning phase, which is typically the first step of your project.

l2Processes.title: Manage project delivery
l2Processes.description:
The manage project delivery business process area is key to tracking the work that is delivered on a project. It helps ensure that the final outcomes are provided to stakeholders in a diligent and timely manner. During this phase, all the tasks, activities, and milestones that are outlined in the project plan are performed and completed. Depending on the nature of the project, work might include various activities, such as the examples in the following list:Design Development Testing Documentation Quality assurance   We recommend that you use a structured approach to track, record, and manage the effort that team members and resources invest in these activities. In this way, you gain valuable insights into how the project is progressing and how project objectives are being met.
 This business process area also covers the evaluation of project performance, estimation of task completion times, and identification of potential bottlenecks. In addition, it facilitates the assessment of resource allocation, and therefore helps optimize workforce utilization and enhance project efficiency. Through the systematic capture of project time, organizations enhance their ability to deliver projects on schedule, allocate resources effectively, and boost overall productivity.
 Ideally, the structure of the manage project delivery business process is defined during the early stages of project implementation. By outlining the manage project delivery process as project parameters, timelines, and resource allocation are being established, you ensure that accurate data collection begins from the project's inception. Although the process might naturally evolve as the project progresses, it's important to lay its foundation before the project work begins.

l2Processes.title: Manage project financials
l2Processes.description:
The manage project financials business process area focuses on managing the financial data that is associated with project-related activities. It involves generating, processing, and reviewing invoices according to contractual arrangements. It also involves tracking payments, managing project budgets, adjusting project transactions, and recognizing project revenue. The manage project financials business process ensures the accuracy of the financial data that is linked to the project. In this way, it also ensures a healthy cash flow for your organization.
 Manage project financials is an essential step in the project to profit end-to-end business process flow. Maintaining a structured and well-defined approach to project financial management is critical for preventing financial risks and payment delays. It also helps ensure control and successful completion of project tasks in your organization.
 The manage project financials business process should be outlined and incorporated into the overall implementation during the planning phase. The planning phase typically occurs at the project's launch. It involves setting project goals, defining project scope, and developing a detailed project plan.

l2Processes.title: Develop service strategy
l2Processes.description:
Service to cash is a set of integrated capabilities in Dynamics 365 that helps
organizations streamline their service delivery and invoicing processes. It
enables businesses to manage their customer service interactions and turn them
into revenue-generating opportunities.
With service to cash, organizations can automate their service request handling,
create service agreements with customers, manage their resources, track their
time and expenses, and generate invoices for services rendered. This automation
helps them reduce manual effort, minimize errors, and improve billing accuracy.
Some of the key features of service to cash for Dynamics 365 include:
 * Service Management: Provides tools to manage service requests, create service
   agreements, and track work orders.
 * Resource Management: Helps organizations assign the right resources to
   service requests, manage their schedules, and track their time and expenses.
 * Invoicing: Enables organizations to generate invoices based on service
   agreements and track payments.
 * Analytics: Provides insights into service performance and profitability to
   help organizations make informed decisions.
Overall, service to cash for Dynamics 365 can help organizations improve their
service delivery, increase customer satisfaction, and generate more revenue.
Every organization has variations of the service to cash process. Here, we
define the basic outline for any organization that is looking to implement a
technological solution to support the service to cash business process.

l2Processes.title: Analyze project performance
l2Processes.description:
The monitor and analyze project performance business process area focuses on systematically monitoring, assessing, and evaluating various project elements, including the following list:How much of the project scope is already delivered? What remains? What are the project costs and revenue? Are there any risks? Which issues prevent the project from progressing as planned and meeting its objectives?   By monitoring project performance and conducting in-depth analysis, project managers and stakeholders can gain valuable insights, make informed decisions, and take any corrective action that is required.
 The process of monitoring and analyzing project performance should be established at the onset of the project. As soon as the project objectives, scope, and resources are defined, it's imperative to set up a monitoring and analysis mechanism. In this way, you ensure that a system for gauging progress, measuring performance, and implementing course corrections is in place from the earliest stages. Early incorporation of this business process area also ensures that data collection is consistent throughout the project, so that insights for analysis are more accurate.

l2Processes.title: Manage customer relationships
l2Processes.description:
Strong customer relationship management requires collaboration across Sales, Service, Marketing, and Operations to maximize customer lifetime value.
 Effective customer relationship management starts when Sales classifies and documents new customer accounts. Prospects can be nurtured so that they become clients. Sales is also responsible for structuring customer hierarchies to represent organizational relationships. Account managers and cross-functional teams are assigned to manage key accounts and develop growth strategies.
 As customers begin engaging, Customer Service provides ongoing support across channels such as email, chat, phone, and in person. Service agents have access to the full customer history and can therefore deliver personalized experiences. Marketing creates segmented customer journeys to guide customers through educational touchpoints. Operations fulfills orders and returns.
 Throughout the customer lifecycle, teams track satisfaction metrics and loyalty through surveys and interaction analysis. This data helps identify upsell opportunities and prevent churn. Dynamics 365 empowers teams to work together seamlessly and gain unified visibility into all customer interactions. Proactive relationship management is vital for maximizing lifetime value, profitability, and traceability.

l2Processes.title: Define accounting policies
l2Processes.description:
In a Dynamics 365 implementation, definition of the financial structure and definition of organizational policies are crucial steps that significantly affect the success of the implementation and the overall efficiency of the business processes. In Dynamics 365 Finance, the chart of accounts, financial dimensions, and many General ledger parameters and configurations are used in downstream processes. Because Dynamics 365 Finance is a double-entry system that integrates directly with Dynamics 365 Commerce, Project Operations, and Supply Chain Management, all entries that are posted to a subledger are also posted to the general ledger.
 By defining the financial structure early and clearly in your implementation, you help ensure accurate financial reporting, compliance, data consistency, and cost allocations. The financial structure in a Dynamics 365 Finance implementation includes the chart of accounts, cost centers, profit centers, financial reporting structures, tax codes, and more.
 Organizational accounting policies include things such as the legal entity or company structure, elimination and consolidation rules, and intercompany accounting configuration, including centralized payments. They also define how you handle currency conversion and revaluation of your financial data throughout the system. In addition, if you use the Fixed assets or Asset leasing module, it covers the asset-related company policies. If you use the Cash and bank management module, it covers the banking policies and configurations. It's important to design your processes so that they are streamlined and efficient, to help reduce manual intervention and minimize errors. When you define your organizational accounting policies, you should consider standardizing them across companies and departments. By implementing consistent business processes across the organization, you help ensure uniformity and reduce confusion among employees. This standardization is especially important when different geographies, departments, or companies are involved. Although Dynamics 365 Finance supports flexible configuration across legal entities, the more processes you can standardize, the smoother your implementation is.
 Many other areas rely on or are affected by the decisions that are made in the define financial structure and organizational accounting policies area. Therefore, the business processes in this area should be defined as early as possible in the overall implementation project.

l2Processes.title: Manage budgets
l2Processes.description:
Budget management is a cornerstone of financial planning and control, and holds profound significance for businesses across industries. It empowers organizations to allocate resources efficiently, make informed financial decisions, and chart a course towards sustainable growth. In an era marked by economic volatility and shifting market dynamics, a robust budget management strategy underpins not only fiscal responsibility but also the ability to adapt and thrive in changing circumstances. By leveraging Dynamics 365 to streamline this critical business process, enterprises can harness the power of data-driven insights, automation, and real-time visibility, ultimately optimizing their financial performance and enhancing their competitive edge.
The budgeting features of Dynamics 365 Finance can be added at any point in your project or post go-live; however, we recommend that you consider including the basic budgeting features in your project at a minimum to harness the full flexibility and data visibility into budgets versus actuals. If Dynamics 365 Finance is not your system of record for financials, we recommend that you integrate your actual or the totals of your financial transactions into your system of record for financials where you do manage your budget. As part of a holistic ERP implementation, budget management should be integrated into your organization's operations, enabling real-time insights, collaboration, and data-driven decision-making.

l2Processes.title: Record financial transactions
l2Processes.description:
Efficiently recording financial transactions is a cornerstone of organizational success, serving as the bedrock for informed decision-making, compliance, and financial stability. In the realm of Dynamics 365, this process gains an extra layer of precision and agility, transforming the way businesses handle their financial data. The significance of recording financial transactions lies in its ability to provide a real-time, accurate snapshot of an organization's fiscal health. Dynamics 365 Finance and Business Central enhance this importance by streamlining the entire transaction recording process, offering robust tools and features that contribute to better financial management.
Implementing Dynamics 365 Finance involves navigating a series of crucial business processes to ensure seamless financial transaction recording. The overall processes include defining policies, setting approval workflows, managing ledger postings, recording entries, correcting vouchers, handling accruals, managing deferrals, and processing allocations. These steps collectively ensure accurate, real-time financial data. In a Dynamics 365 implementation, start with clear policies and early approval workflows, progressively addressing ledger management, journal entries, and voucher corrections. Strategic integration of accruals, deferrals, and allocations at different project stages maximizes the system's potential, optimizing financial transaction recording.
 When embarking on a Dynamics 365 Finance implementation, addressing the aforementioned processes strategically is paramount. Begin by defining clear policies and procedures, setting the stage for a robust financial transaction recording system. Early in the project, establish the approval process for journals to ensure a streamlined workflow. As the implementation progresses, focus on managing ledger postings, recording journal entries, and correcting vouchers. Accruals, deferrals, and allocations should be methodically integrated into the system to enhance the accuracy and completeness of financial data. By approaching these processes thoughtfully at different stages of the project, organizations can unlock the full potential of Dynamics 365 Finance in revolutionizing their financial transaction recording practices.

l2Processes.title: Analyze financial performance
l2Processes.description:
Effectively reporting and analyzing financials and cash flow is at the heart of informed decision-making for organizations. In this dynamic landscape, Dynamics 365 offers a comprehensive suite of tools and functionalities to streamline this critical process. From configuring cash flow forecasting to running financial statements, the platform empowers finance professionals to delve deep into financial data, derive meaningful insights, and make data-driven decisions. This journey encompasses a series of strategic steps, including configuring cash flow forecasting, refining prediction models, analyzing cash position, predicting customer payments, scrutinizing financial trends, detecting anomalies, analyzing budget to actuals, designing financial statements, running reports, and distributing financial insights. This holistic approach ensures that organizations not only have a clear understanding of their current financial health but also gain valuable foresight to navigate future financial landscapes effectively.
Reporting and analyzing the financials and cash flow within Dynamics 365 is a strategic process. Beginning with the configuration of cash flow forecasting, organizations can refine and improve prediction models to anticipate future financial scenarios. The platform facilitates real-time analysis of the cash position, enabling proactive liquidity management. Utilizing advanced prediction tools, Dynamics 365 helps forecast customer payments, optimize cash inflows, and support effective credit management. The system's analytical capabilities come into play to identify trends in financial data, while anomaly detection features help promptly address irregularities. With a focus on budget-to-actual analysis, Dynamics 365 enables comparisons that yield insights for strategic adjustments. The platform's versatility extends to the design and generation of comprehensive financial statements, providing accurate and timely reports. Throughout this process, Dynamics 365 empowers finance professionals to view, analyze, and distribute financial statements, ensuring a comprehensive approach to financial reporting and analysis that goes beyond compliance to enable proactive decision-making.
 In this dynamic process, Dynamics 365 acts as a catalyst, transforming financial data into actionable insights. From configuring cash flow forecasting to dissecting individual transactions, the platform equips organizations to navigate the complexities of financial reporting and analysis with precision and agility. This journey not only ensures compliance with reporting standards but also empowers finance professionals to proactively shape the financial trajectory of their organizations. The reporting and analyzing configurations and business processes are typically left until later phases of a Dynamics 365 implementation to allow other configurations and data to be stabilized to ensure the reporting and testing is accurate.

l2Processes.title: Implement solutions
l2Processes.description:
Administer to operate serves as the linchpin for the sustained success of
Dynamics 365 within organizational landscapes. Focused on the ongoing
administration and optimization of existing solutions, this end-to-end business
process entails a comprehensive approach to ensure the seamless operation of
Dynamics 365. It's important to note that administer to operate primarily
concentrates on post-implementation processes, with the Dynamics 365
Implementation Guide serving as a dedicated resource for initial deployment and
installation guidance.
Within the administer to operate framework, various critical processes are
orchestrated to maintain the health and efficiency of Dynamics 365 solutions.
Encompassing a range of critical processes, this end-to-end business process
involves defining business continuity plans, managing licensing and
entitlements, administering system features, ensuring system access and
security, training users for increased adoption, monitoring systems and
capacity, managing background jobs and notifications, adopting software
releases, overseeing data synchronization, ensuring system compliance, and
providing responsive support.
While primarily addressing post-implementation needs, administer to operate
plays a pivotal role in maintaining the efficiency, compliance, and adaptability
of Dynamics 365 solutions, ensuring they continually align with evolving
business processes and user requirements. For initial deployment and
installation guidance, organizations can refer to the Dynamics 365
Implementation Guide, recognizing administer to operate as the cornerstone for
ongoing optimization and resilience.
In summary, administer to operate encompasses a multifaceted approach to
sustainably manage, optimize, and support Dynamics 365 solutions throughout
their lifecycle, ensuring they remain aligned with organizational goals and
responsive to evolving business dynamics.
Every organization has variations to the administer to operate processes. Here,
we define the basic outline for any organization looking to implement a
technology solution to support the administer to operate process with Dynamics
365.

l2Processes.title: Define business continuity plan
l2Processes.description:
Business continuity planning stands as a cornerstone in the realm of organizational resilience, ensuring the seamless operation of critical business functions in the face of unforeseen disruptions. At its core, business continuity planning encompasses a comprehensive framework designed to identify potential risks, devise preventive measures, and establish protocols for swift recovery. In the dynamic landscape of today's business environment, where digital transformation is the norm, a robust business continuity plan becomes not just a best practice but a strategic imperative.
 When it comes to cloud solutions, such as Dynamics 365, the stakes are higher, and the need for a well-defined business continuity plan becomes even more pronounced. Cloud solutions are the backbone of modern businesses and power essential processes and workflows. Any downtime or data loss can have far-reaching consequences, affecting not only operational efficiency but also customer trust. All of that makes it paramount to understand that business continuity means everything during an implementation project, particularly in the implementation of cloud solutions such as Dynamics 365. Business continuity is essential for safeguarding the integrity of critical business operations.
 In the realm of Dynamics 365, a successful business continuity plan is more than a safety net; it's a strategic investment. Navigating the nuances of this powerful platform requires a tailored approach to risk mitigation and recovery. As Dynamics 365 becomes an integral part of your project, considerations for data backup, system monitoring, and failover mechanisms become pivotal. While Microsoft protects and manages your SaaS components that are managed Microsoft tenant, it's still important to consider other components that connect with or are used alongside your Dynamics 365 components.
 In this article, we'll explore the types of tools and options that organizations should weigh when crafting a business continuity plan specific to Dynamics 365 implementations. From Azure-based solutions to integration with third-party tools, we delve into the key considerations that elevate your project's resilience and ensure the uninterrupted flow of business processes.
 The processes we have defined in our business process catalog for business continuity planning encompasses a strategic framework with defined processes to ensure organizational resilience. Initiatives begin with the establishment of clear objectives, followed by a comprehensive Business Impact Analysis to assess potential disruptions and prioritize recovery efforts. The identification of risks, development of a detailed plan, and its subsequent implementation form the core of the strategy. Regular testing and validation refine the plan's effectiveness, while ongoing maintenance ensures its relevance through updates reflecting changes in technology, processes, and risks. Concurrently, critical systems and data are identified and prioritized.
 A Disaster Risk Assessment informs recovery objectives, leading to the formulation and implementation of a comprehensive Disaster Recovery Plan. Testing, validation, and continuous updates enhance the plan's adaptability to evolving threats and technologies, ultimately ensuring an organization's preparedness and resilience in the face of unforeseen disruptions.
 Integrating business continuity planning into a Dynamics 365 implementation project is crucial for fostering a resilient operational environment. Ideally, consideration for business continuity planning should commence during the initial stages of project planning. As project objectives are defined and critical business processes identified, it becomes opportune to evaluate potential risks and plan for disruptions. Throughout the implementation lifecycle, continuous assessment and refinement of the business continuity plan should occur alongside the development and deployment of Dynamics 365 solutions.
 By weaving business continuity planning into the fabric of the implementation process from the outset, organizations can proactively address risks, safeguard critical systems, and ensure a robust framework for continuity, thereby fortifying the success and sustainability of their Dynamics 365 project.

l3Processes.title: Classify assets

apqc.description: Develop property strategy and long term vision

l3Processes.description:
Classifying assets means organizing and categorizing assets based on specific criteria such as type, usage, value, location, or lifecycle stage. This helps organizations manage, track, and report on their assets more effectively. Common asset classifications include:Tangible vs. Intangible (e.g., machinery vs. patents) Current vs. Non-current (e.g., inventory vs. buildings) IT assets, Facilities, Vehicles, etc. Depreciation categories for accounting purposes  How Dynamics 365 Supports Asset Classification Microsoft Dynamics 365, particularly in modules like Dynamics 365 Finance and Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management, provides robust tools for asset classification: 
 Asset Types and Categories:
 You can define asset types (e.g., equipment, vehicles) and assign them to categories. Categories can include depreciation profiles, useful life, and accounting rules. Fixed Asset Management: Allows classification by asset group, location, department, and responsible employee. Supports tagging assets with custom attributes for more granular classification. Hierarchical Structures: Assets can be grouped into parent-child relationships (e.g., a vehicle fleet with individual cars). Lifecycle Tracking: Tracks assets from acquisition to disposal, with classification updates at each stage. Reporting and Analytics: Enables filtering and reporting based on asset classifications for audits, budgeting, and compliance.

l3Processes.title: Define leasing policies

apqc.description: Determine build, buy, or lease decision

l3Processes.description:
Leasing policies in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps govern the
acquisition, management, and accounting of leased assets. The system supports
both finance and operating leases, allowing configuration of lease terms,
payment schedules, and accounting treatments in compliance with standards like
IFRS 16 and ASC 842. Policies define approval workflows, lease classification,
recognition of right-of-use assets, and automated posting of lease liabilities
and expenses.

l3Processes.title: Define asset accounting books

apqc.description: Develop property strategy and long term vision

l3Processes.description: Only used for configurations

l3Processes.title: Define depreciation and amortization policies

apqc.description: Develop property strategy and long term vision

l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps supports multiple depreciation and
amortization methods, including straight-line, declining balance,
sum-of-years-digits, usage-based, and bonus depreciation. Schedules are
configured per asset or asset group, with automated calculation and posting. The
system handles revaluations, accelerated depreciation, and compliance with tax
and statutory requirements.

l3Processes.title: Develop maintenance strategy
l3Processes.description:
Maintenance strategy in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
defining preventive and corrective maintenance plans for assets. The system
supports scheduling, work order management, maintenance history tracking, and
integration with asset records. Maintenance strategies can be tailored by asset
type, usage, and criticality, supporting compliance and asset longevity.

l3Processes.title: Plan fixed assets
l3Processes.description:
Asset data is often maintained in worksheets and is typically configured after
the general ledger setup is completed in Dynamics 365. A lot of organizations
start late with data migration of data that is related to fixed asset or asset
management. They often realize that there are gaps when the balance or
transaction type of data is brought into the business solution. We recommend
that you start the fixed asset migration early in the project to pressure-test
the data and the processes that the business team follows.
With asset leasing in Dynamics 365 Finance, organizations can access and use
assets without the need for large upfront investments. Instead of purchasing
assets outright, organizations can lease them from leasing companies for a
predetermined period. With the asset leasing module in Dynamics 365 Finance, the
lessee to create legal contracts between the lessor and lessee.
Here are some common methods to acquire assets in Dynamics 365 Finance and
Supply Chain Management:
 1. Procurement and sourcing: With procurement and sourcing in Dynamics 365
    Supply Chain Management, organizations can source assets from suppliers.
    This supports a supplier bidding process for supplier selection,
    negotiation, and purchase agreement creation. Users can use these features
    to acquire assets from approved suppliers through:
     1. Purchase orders
     2. Purchase requisition
 2. Fixed assets: With the fixed assets module, organizations can manage their
    fixed assets throughout their lifecycle, which includes acquiring assets
    through one of the following means:
     1. Asset creation
     2. Asset acquisition journal
     3. Asset transfer

l3Processes.title: Plan asset leases

apqc.description: Determine build, buy, or lease decision

l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows users to plan asset leases by
forecasting lease requirements, evaluating lease versus buy scenarios, and
aligning lease schedules with operational and financial strategies. The system
supports lease budgeting, tracks lease terms, and integrates with fixed asset
and financial modules for compliance and reporting.

l3Processes.title: Budget fixed assets
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps supports budgeting for fixed asset
acquisitions by allowing users to allocate funds, track budget consumption, and
monitor variances. The system integrates with capital budgeting, procurement,
and project management modules, ensuring alignment between asset plans and
financial controls. Budgeting tools enable users to set approval limits, track
expenditures, and generate reports for compliance and analysis.

l3Processes.title: Budget asset leases
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enables budgeting for asset leases,
including forecasting lease payments, tracking commitments, and integrating with
overall capital and operating budgets. The system provides visibility into lease
obligations, supports compliance with accounting standards, and allows users to
monitor lease-related expenditures against budget.

l3Processes.title: Reallocate asset budgets
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows users to reallocate asset
budgets in response to changing business needs, project delays, or cost
overruns. The system supports budget transfers, tracks approvals, and maintains
audit trails for compliance. Users can adjust allocations between projects,
departments, or asset categories as required.

l3Processes.title: Source assets
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps supports sourcing assets by enabling
users to identify suppliers, request quotes, evaluate bids, and select vendors.
The system integrates with procurement and supply chain modules to streamline
sourcing and ensure compliance with purchasing policies. Users can manage vendor
relationships, track sourcing activities, and document procurement decisions.

l3Processes.title: Define research and development approach
l3Processes.description: Defining the research and development approach involves outlining the methodology and strategies for conducting research and development. It includes setting objectives, identifying resources, and planning the steps needed to innovate and improve offerings.

l3Processes.title: Purchase assets
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 Finance and Operations enables organizations to purchase assets
through integrated procurement processes. Users can create purchase
requisitions, route them for approval, generate purchase orders, and manage
vendor relationships. The system supports tracking of asset-related
expenditures, automates invoice matching, and ensures compliance with financial
controls. Asset records are created upon receipt, linking procurement and
financial modules for seamless asset lifecycle management.

l3Processes.title: Produce assets
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps supports asset production through
manufacturing and project management modules. Users can initiate production
orders, manage bills of materials (BOM), track labor and material costs, and
record asset completion. The system integrates with inventory and fixed asset
modules, allowing produced assets to be capitalized and tracked throughout their
lifecycle.

l3Processes.title: Lease assets
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enables organizations to lease assets
by managing lease agreements, tracking lease terms, and automating payment
schedules. The system supports both finance and operating leases, integrates
with fixed asset and financial modules, and ensures compliance with accounting
standards. Users can record lease acquisitions, monitor lease obligations, and
manage end-of-lease activities.

l3Processes.title: Install and commission assets
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps supports installation and commissioning
of assets by enabling users to track installation activities, manage
commissioning checklists, and record asset readiness. The system integrates with
project management and maintenance modules, ensuring assets are properly
installed, tested, and ready for use. Commissioning status is updated in the
asset record, supporting compliance and operational readiness.

l3Processes.title: Record fixed asset acquisitions
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows users to record fixed asset
acquisitions by capturing asset details, linking to purchase or production
orders, and assigning asset numbers. The system supports capitalization,
depreciation setup, and integration with financial reporting. Asset records are
enriched with attributes such as acquisition date, cost, and location,
supporting lifecycle management and compliance.

l3Processes.title: Record asset lease right-of-use
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps supports recording asset lease
right-of-use by capturing lease details, calculating right-of-use asset values,
and integrating with financial reporting for compliance with accounting
standards (e.g., IFRS 16, ASC 842). The system automates recognition of lease
liabilities, amortization schedules, and updates asset records accordingly.

l3Processes.title: Maintain fixed assets
l3Processes.description:
Organization may have certain assets that are large items in the balance sheet.
Based on the location and area where the assets are installed or deployed, there
may be extra tax component associated with the asset. These assets may have
insurance associated with them as well. In some cases, assets may be leased out
to a lessee. Hence, it becomes important for companies to optimally manage and
report asset information.
Fixed assets are reported on the balance sheet. Activities related to
investments are captured in a company's cash flow statement. The acquisition or
disposal of an asset is also recorded in the cash flow statement.
When you use a general ledger together with fixed assets, you can view the
current value of all fixed assets. The way in which fixed assets are handled
must correspond to both international accounting standards and the accounting
legislation in each country/region.
Companies start off by evaluating the need for assets and budget/plan it up in
advance. The process then involves either acquiring an asset or leasing an asset
depending on multiple parameters. In case the asset is acquired, it goes through
a high-level process that could be a mix of more asset related information
adjustment, depreciation, disposal. Companies can define rules to record
acquisition method, depreciation and disposal, write-ups and write-downs.
The management of assets and its finance is an ongoing operational process for
an organization. It becomes more important when audits are executed to review
financial information.
In Dynamics 365, you find multiple reports to assess fixed asset information.
One such example is the Fixed assets roll forward report (Finance).

l3Processes.title: Maintain asset leases
l3Processes.description:
This process covers the ongoing management of asset lease records, including
updating lease terms, tracking payment schedules, handling modifications, and
managing lease renewals or terminations. The system supports compliance with
accounting standards and provides audit trails for all lease-related changes.

l3Processes.title: Make payments on asset leases
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 automates and tracks lease payment schedules, generating payment
proposals and integrating with accounts payable for processing. The system posts
payments, updates lease liabilities, and provides reporting on outstanding and
completed payments.

l3Processes.title: Revalue and adjust assets

apqc.description: Process and record fixed-asset adjustments, enhancements, revaluations, and transfers

l3Processes.description:
This process allows users to revalue fixed assets based on market value,
impairment, or other adjustments. Dynamics 365 supports revaluation journals,
impairment postings, and adjustment tracking, ensuring compliance with
accounting standards and accurate financial reporting.

l3Processes.title: Depreciate assets
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 automates asset depreciation based on configured schedules,
methods, and useful lives. The system posts periodic depreciation entries to the
general ledger, updates asset book values, and provides detailed reporting for
compliance and analysis.

l3Processes.title: Insure assets
l3Processes.description:
This process involves tracking insurance coverage for fixed assets, including
policy details, coverage periods, and renewal dates. Dynamics 365 allows users
to attach insurance documents, set reminders for renewals, and report on
coverage status.

l3Processes.title: Transfer assets

apqc.description: Process and record fixed-asset adjustments, enhancements, revaluations, and transfers

l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 supports asset transfers between locations, departments, or legal
entities. The system manages transfer approvals, updates asset records, and
posts necessary financial entries to reflect the transfer.

l3Processes.title: Improve assets

apqc.description: Process and record fixed-asset adjustments, enhancements, revaluations, and transfers

l3Processes.description:
This process allows users to capitalize improvements or major repairs to
existing assets. Dynamics 365 supports adding value to assets, updating
depreciation schedules, and tracking improvement history for compliance and
analysis.

l3Processes.title: Budget asset maintenance
l3Processes.description:
In many organizations, the terms maintenance and repair are used
interchangeably. However, they might refer to different areas in the asset
management process. Maintenance includes activities that can be scheduled and/or
performed at a specific time to help prevent assets from breaking down and
increase their life. Repairs help restore the functionality of assets when they
are damaged or inoperable from a process standpoint.
There are three main types of maintenance tasks:
 * Reactive: Reactive maintenance involves repairing an asset that has broken
   down or experienced a failure, typically one that isn't critical for
   business. If the maintenance strategy isn't effectively planned for the
   appropriate assets, reactive maintenance can lead to unexpected downtime and
   higher costs.
 * Preventive: Preventive maintenance involves performing periodic planned tasks
   to review the state of an asset and apply corrective procedures to help
   prevent it from failing. These tasks can help extend the life of the asset
   and can also help the organization avoid unplanned repair and restoration
   costs.
 * Predictive: Predictive maintenance involves using signals from machines and
   equipment on the production floor to sense deviations or irregularities in
   day-to-day operations. By using sensor intelligence mechanisms and/or smart
   technology, the organization can predict failures or outages before they
   occur. This proactive approach helps ensure that maintenance can be performed
   in a timely manner to mitigate potential delays.
