l3Processes.title: Analyze knowledge base usage

apqc.description: Manage customer service problems, requests, and inquiries

l3Processes.description: As a Knowledge Manager, I want to analyze how agents and customers use the Knowledge Base,So that I can improve content quality and ensure articles help resolve cases. Steps to Analyze Knowledge Base Usage in D365 Customer Service Analyzing Knowledge Base (KB) usage in Microsoft Dynamics 365 Customer Service helps organizations evaluate how effectively agents and customers are using articles to resolve cases. This guide outlines the steps to track, review, and optimize KB usage. 1. Why Analyze Knowledge Base Usage? Identify the most frequently used articles.Assess whether KB articles are resolving cases.Determine gaps in knowledge content.Improve article quality and relevance. 2. Steps to Analyze Knowledge Base Usage in D365 Step 1: Access Knowledge Base Insights Navigate to Customer Service Hub or Customer Service Workspace. Click on Knowledge Articles in the left-hand navigation panel. Select Knowledge Search Analytics under Insights (if enabled).  Step 2: Review Knowledge Search Analytics Metrics to Analyze: Top-searched keywords: Identify what agents and customers search for. Most-viewed articles: See which articles are frequently accessed. Article success rate: Review how often an article is linked to resolved cases. Unused or low-performing articles: Identify articles that need updates or removal.  How to View Data: Open Dashboards > Knowledge Search Analytics for graphical reports. Use Power BI Reports if custom analytics are set up.  Step 3: Track Knowledge Base Usage in Cases Open Service > Cases. Use the Knowledge Search panel to see linked articles. Filter cases by Knowledge Article Associations to check:How often articles are attached to cases. Whether articles contribute to case resolution. Feedback on article effectiveness.    Step 4: Analyze Agent Engagement with KB Articles Navigate to Settings > Service Management > Knowledge Search Analytics.Review: Which agents use KB articles the most. Article attachment trends per agent/team. Cases where no KB article was used (indicating potential content gaps).  Step 5: Gather Customer Feedback on KB Articles Enable Knowledge Article Ratings & Feedback Go to Settings > Service Management > Knowledge Settings. Enable Ratings & Comments for KB articles.   Review Customer Feedback Open Knowledge Articles > Select an article. View Ratings, Comments, and Helpful/Not Helpful votes. Identify articles with poor feedback and update them.    Step 6: Use Reports & Dashboards for KB Insights Standard Reports: Open Reports > Knowledge Base Usage Report. Export data to Excel for further analysis.  Power BI Dashboards (If Enabled): Track Knowledge Base adoption trends. Visualize article effectiveness per category or product.

l3Processes.title: Review call center performance

apqc.description: Manage customer service problems, requests, and inquiries

l3Processes.description: As a Call Center Manager, I want to analyze case type trends, individual agent performance, and SLA violations,So that I can optimize service efficiency and reduce escalations. Steps to Review Call Center Performance by Case Type, Analyze Individual Performance, and Analyze SLA Violations in D365 Customer Service Tracking call center performance, individual agent performance, and SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance ensures efficient customer service operations. This guide outlines how to review and analyze these metrics using Microsoft Dynamics 365 Customer Service Historical Analytics. 1. Reviewing Call Center Performance by Case Type Step 1: Access Customer Service Historical Analytics Navigate to Customer Service Workspace or Customer Service Hub. Click on Customer Service Historical Analytics from the left-hand navigation panel. Open the Case Insights Dashboard.  Step 2: Filter Cases by Type Locate the Case Type field in the analytics dashboard. Use filters to categorize cases by type (e.g., Technical Issue, Billing, Warranty, Product Inquiry). Apply date filters to analyze performance over a specific period.  Step 3: Analyze Case Trends Key Metrics to Review: Total number of cases per type (Identify the most common issues). Average resolution time by case type (Compare how quickly different case types are resolved). First Contact Resolution (FCR) rate (How often cases are resolved in the first interaction). Escalation rates by case type (Which types require managerial intervention).  2. Analyzing Individual Agent Performance Step 1: Open Agent Performance Dashboard In Customer Service Historical Analytics, go to Agent Insights. Select the Agent Performance dashboard.  Step 2: Review Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)  Total Cases Handled: Number of cases an agent has worked on. Average Handling Time (AHT): The time taken to resolve cases. Resolution Rate: Percentage of cases resolved by the agent. Customer Satisfaction Score (CSAT): Based on customer feedback and surveys. Escalation Rate: Percentage of cases escalated to supervisors.   Step 3: Compare Agent Performance Filter by agent name to compare performance. Identify top-performing agents vs. those needing training. Check if agents handle certain case types more efficiently.  Best Practice: Use Power Automate to send automated alerts if an agent’s performance drops below a set threshold. 3. Analyzing SLA Violations Step 1: Access SLA Compliance Dashboard In Customer Service Historical Analytics, click on SLA Insights. Open the SLA Compliance dashboard.  Step 2: Identify SLA Breaches Key Metrics to Analyze: Total SLA violations (Number of missed SLAs). Cases with SLA breaches (Filter cases that exceeded response/resolution time). Agents with the highest SLA violations (Identify recurring issues). SLA compliance percentage (Measure overall adherence).  Step 3: Drill Down into SLA Violations Filter by Case Type to see which case types frequently exceed SLA limits. Filter by Agent/Team to identify who is missing SLAs. Review SLA breach reasons (e.g., long wait times, agent workload).  Best Practice: Automate SLA alerts in Power Automate to notify managers when an SLA is about to breach.  4. Best Practices for Call Center Performance Management Use AI-powered insights (Copilot for Customer Service) for real-time analytics. Enable SLA breach notifications in Power Automate. Regularly review performance dashboards to address gaps proactively. Use Power BI for deeper analytics beyond built-in reports.

l3Processes.title: Review case loads and rebalance case loads

apqc.description: Manage customer service problems, requests, and inquiries

l3Processes.description: As a Customer Service Manager, I want to analyze and redistribute case loads across my team,So that no agent is overwhelmed and cases are resolved efficiently. Steps to Review and Rebalance Case Loads in D365 Customer Service Effectively reviewing and rebalancing case loads ensures that agents are not overwhelmed and that cases are resolved efficiently. This guide outlines how to analyze case distribution and redistribute cases using Microsoft Dynamics 365 Customer Service Historical Analytics. 1. Reviewing Case Loads in D365 Customer Service Step 1: Access Customer Service Historical Analytics Navigate to Customer Service Workspace or Customer Service Hub. Click on Customer Service Historical Analytics from the left-hand navigation panel. Open the Agent Insights Dashboard or Case Insights Dashboard.  Step 2: Review Current Case Load Distribution Key Metrics to Analyze: Total Cases Assigned Per Agent: Identify overloaded agents. Active vs. Resolved Cases: Determine backlog levels. Case Aging Report: Find cases that have been open too long. Escalation Rate Per Agent: Identify performance gaps. SLA Status by Agent: Check if any agents are at risk of breaching SLAs.  Step 3: Identify Imbalances in Case Loads Compare case volumes across different agents and teams. Look for agents with a significantly higher number of open cases. Identify unassigned or overdue cases that require reassignment.  2. Rebalancing Case Loads in D365 Customer Service Step 1: Reassign Cases Manually Navigate to Customer Service Workspace and open Cases. Use filters to identify:Overloaded agents. Cases that need reassignment based on priority. Unassigned cases that need a responsible owner.   Select cases that need reassignment. Click Assign, then select the appropriate agent or team. Click Save to confirm the reassignment.  Best Practice: Leave an internal Case Note explaining why the case was reassigned. Step 2: Automate Case Balancing with Queues & Routing Rules Go to Service Management > Queues. Review the existing queues and ensure cases are evenly distributed. Navigate to Service Management > Routing Rules. Set up Auto-Assignment Rules based on:Agent workload. Case priority. Case type and required expertise.    Best Practice: Use Omnichannel for Customer Service with Workstreams for automatic case distribution. Step 3: Set Up Real-Time Alerts for Case Load Monitoring  Use Power Automate to notify managers when: An agent has more than a predefined number of active cases. A case has been unassigned for too long. An agent is consistently missing SLAs.   3. Best Practices for Case Load Balancing Regularly review workload distribution to avoid agent burnout. Use AI-based routing in Omnichannel for Customer Service for smarter case assignments. Enable workload-based case reassignment in Power Automate. Monitor SLA trends to prevent bottlenecks in case resolution.

l3Processes.title: Define product policies
l3Processes.description:
All organizations group and categorize or segment their products into logical
grouping for various reasons. Dynamics 365 includes several capabilities that
you can consider implementing together at the beginning of a project. The terms
categorization, merchandising, and segmentation of products and services are all
related concepts. Although all three terms are often used interchangeably, they
do have distinct meanings and functions in the system as well. The following
list outlines the differences:- Categorization refers to the process for
grouping products or services based on shared characteristics.
 * Segmentation refers to the process of dividing a market (of typically
   customers) into smaller groups (or segments) based on shared characteristics
   or interests.
 * Merchandising refers to the process of presenting products or services in an
   appealing and attractive way to the customers.
It's important for an organization to consider carefully how it categorizes,
segments, and merchandises the products and services it offers. During the
implementation of a business solution, an organization often already has a way
of doing this already. It's important to consider if the current mechanism for
categorizing products and services meets all the business requirements, because
the implementation of a new technology solution is often a good time to consider
making a change. The organization might want to consider questions such as the
following list:
 * How do you drive downstream business processes and automation?
 * How do you report and analyze your data including operational reporting and
   financial reporting?
 * How has your business grown since the original mechanism of categorization,
   segmentation, or merchandising was developed?
 * How do you anticipate your business will grow in the future?
The answers to these questions should help inform your process for defining the
way you implement a technology solution to support the categorization of
products and services. This business process area should always be considered
early in a Dynamics 365 implementation project. Also consider the number of
changes that might be happening to the data in this area as well. This can make
integrations, data migration, and other aspects of your project more complex.
When an organization starts a Dynamics 365 implementation, it's important to
carefully consider how you define the product lines in the system. This includes
tasks like setting up item groups and product categories in Dynamics 365 Supply
Chain Management and product families in Dynamics 365 Sales. The business
process areas for categorizing either products or services also include the
definition of product policies. We recommend that you group similar items
together based on the expected behaviors they need in the system. This includes
settings like item model groups, reservations, storage, and tracking dimensions,
and more in Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management.
Also, if your organization offers multiple types of products or services, you
should define the rules and configurations for each type of product or service
that you offer. For example, in the chemical or food and beverage industry, you
might use catch weight items. In some industries, consigned inventory is common
and requires more setup, configuration, and considerations. Similarly, the
service industry has considerations to make for the services that they offer,
including warranties, installation, and so on. Many organizations also charge
extra fees or service charges for tangible items that are sold which might
require more considerations. Dynamics 356 supports various flexible
configurations to support many types of products, services, and extra fees and
charges that might be charges to customers or imposed by suppliers.
Once you have a basic definition for organizing products or services, you can
categorize and build hierarchical structures and catalogs or assortments for the
downstream processes. Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management and Commerce support
multiple flexible hierarchies for procurement, sales, channel management, and
more. In Dynamics 365 Sales, you can create hierarchies of products or services
with product families. Dynamics 365 Commerce uses assortments to define product
mix for a retail channel such as the call center, retail store, or online store.
One or more assortments are then combined and assigned to a catalog for each
retail channel. This process often uses product attributes that further describe
the product. However, attributes can describe categories or retail channels as
well, for example.
When you group common or similar items together into categories, you can more
easily manage the attributes. Attributes serve an important function in
merchandising and for customers to find products that meet their requirements.
Attributes can be easily added at any time, but starting with a good baseline
for your project to help you meet reporting requirements is critical to
downstream analytics. With tools to mass update attribute values, you can easily
manage the values at any time and adapt to the changing customer needs and
supply chain.
With Dynamics 365 Customer Service, you can create and manage subjects to manage
complex hierarchies that can represent products, brands, or types of issues that
are reported by your customers, for example. With Dynamics 365 Customer
Insights, segments help you organize customers into groups based on their buying
habits and one common segment is typically related to the products customers
purchase. However, organizations can define many segments based on many
different attributes of the products that are purchased.

l3Processes.title: Develop merchandise and assortment plans
l3Processes.description:
Allowing organizations to plan which products are offered in specific stores or

l3Processes.title: Research new products
l3Processes.description:
The ability for an organization to create new products in their offerings plays
a pivotal role in the growth and innovation of the organization. The business
process area involves strategic development, launch and integration of fresh
offerings into the market, ensuring that the organization remains competitive
and responsive to the evolving customers' needs.
The process of introducing new offerings into the market can vary drastically
from industry to industry and geography to geography, among other factors.
However, one thing that is certain among all organizations, is that the process
can be costly, time consuming, and has a direct impact on the organization's
bottom line. For this reason, having strong technology solutions like Dynamics
365, Microsoft 365, and the Power Platform are essential to ensure that business
decision makers have the right information to make the right decisions.
Organizations can diversify their portfolio, tap into new revenue streams, and
strengthen market presence by introducing new products. The Dynamics 365 product
suite offers a variety of tools and products to help support this process and
products across multiple industries.
For example, Dynamics 365 Customer Service offers capabilities to support
tangible products that are offered to customers. Dynamics 365 Commerce offers
the capabilities to track sales in the point of sale, online, in a call center,
or with integrations from Dynamics 365 Sales or other third-party solutions that
help you manage your sales cycle. Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management supports
the product lifecycle of manufactured products, distributed products, and much
more.
With complex support for various types of products across the portfolio, it's
important to select the right technologies to support your business needs. When
introducing new products, it's important to consider that careful execution of
the market research, ideation, rigorous testing, and seamless coordination
across departments is executed well.
Dynamics 365 Marketing offers tools to help with many steps, and when used
alongside our productivity tools like Microsoft Teams, Planner, and Outlook, and
Dynamics 365 Customer Voice, as a few examples, organization can successfully
implement solutions to support the process leading to increased customer
satisfaction, increased brand reputation, and sustainable growth.
When organizations are looking to implement technological solutions to support
the introduction of new products business process, we recommend that these
activities are prioritized and planned early in the process. The product data
that is captured as part of the business process is critical to many of the
downstream processes and poor implementation can cause data quality issues which
can lead to other issues later in the project and in the overall business cycle.
Keep in mind that product data is considered master data and should be carefully
planned and integrated into all downstream business processes.
If you are planning to use a third-party solution for product master data, you
can integrate your solution in the first phases of your product and you can look
to replace the third-party solution in a later phase. However, careful
considerations for the functionality needed should be taken early in the project
to avoid reworking later.

l3Processes.title: Develop new products

l3Processes.title: Set product prices

apqc.description: Establish guidelines for applying pricing and discounting of products/services

l3Processes.description:
Managing product pricing is a pivotal aspect of organizational success, influencing profitability and competitive positioning. In today's dynamic business landscape, effective product pricing requires a comprehensive approach, and when using the suite of Dynamics 365 products organizations can achieve these goals. From Dynamics 365 Commerce to Customer Service, Field Service, Project Operations, Sales, and Supply Chain Management, these interconnected tools provide a robust framework for optimizing product pricing strategies.
 Dynamics 365 enables organizations to seamlessly align pricing structures with market demands and competitive landscapes, fostering transparent relationships with clients. The suite facilitates agile responses to dynamic market changes, ensuring adaptability and strategic refinement to stay ahead. Specifically, in Dynamics 365 Commerce, businesses can apply advanced pricing models for both business to business (B2B) and business to consumer (B2C) scenarios, across channels such as online, in store, and in the back office, enhancing their merchandising strategies and delivering personalized pricing experiences to diverse customer segments.
 The process of managing product pricing involves a series of interconnected business processes crucial for organizational efficiency. Beginning with the definition of a robust product catalog and strategy, organizations can introduce new products seamlessly, strategically positioning them in the market. The management of product pricing involves meticulous determination of prices and eligibility for discounts, ensuring offerings align with market demands.
 Simultaneously, defining product costing becomes paramount for maintaining profitability. The holistic approach extends to managing the entire product lifecycle, from introduction to retirement, ensuring continuous refinement and strategic adaptation. This integrated process within Dynamics 365 empowers organizations to navigate the complexities of product pricing with agility and foresight, contributing to sustained success.
 In an organization focused on products, especially when downstream processes such as procure to pay, prospect to quote, order to cash, plan to produce, project to profit, case to resolution, service to cash, and/or record to report are part of the Dynamics 365 implementation, early consideration of business processes related to managing product pricing is essential.
 The data and output of these processes impact various downstream operations and defining them early in the project ensures overall project success. If Dynamics 365 won't serve as the system of record for product data, integrations to the designated system should be considered early in the planning phase.

l3Processes.description:

l3Processes.title: Launch new products
l3Processes.description:

catalogs, and communicating availability to stakeholders. Dynamics 365 finance
and operations apps supports product release, catalog updates, and integration
with sales and inventory.

l3Processes.title: Process change requests
l3Processes.description:
Management of the product lifecycle within an organization is of paramount
importance for businesses that deal with tangible products, whether they are
involved in buying, selling, distributing, or manufacturing. Strategic
orchestration of the product lifecycle ensures optimal efficiency,
cost-effectiveness, and customer satisfaction.
When organizations use Dynamics 365 to maintain product information, the system
plays a pivotal role by seamlessly integrating various products, such as
Dynamics 365 Customer Service, Sales, Field Service, Project Operations, and
Supply Chain Management. This comprehensive integration provides a unified
approach and enables organizations to streamline operations, enhance
collaboration, and make informed decisions throughout the entire product
lifecycle.
The process of managing a product's lifecycle involves several critical business
processes, each of which contributes to the overarching success of the data and
downstream process. These business processes include analyzing product margins
of products that are sold or produced. Many signals can come from upstream
processes in the case-to-resolution process. These processes send signals that
inventory should be removed, or that item usage should be restricted in sales,
procurement, production, or servicing operations.
For organizations that manufacture products, it's critical to ensure that you:
 * Define and document clear engineering policies.
 * Maintain accurate product versions and details.
 * Process the requested changes with rigor to ensure compliance with
   established policies and regulations.
Furthermore, the capability to seamlessly request, process, and retire a product
underscores the agility and adaptability that Dynamics 365 affords.
Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management includes robust product information
management capabilities that seamlessly integrate with other products in the
suite. Therefore, organizations can navigate the complexities of the product
lifecycle with precision and ensure sustained success and adaptability in the
dynamic business landscape.
The business processes for managing the product lifecycle in a Dynamics 365
implementation should be addressed in the early to mid-design and configuration
stages of the project.

l3Processes.title: Process change orders
l3Processes.description:

l3Processes.title: Monitor product performance

l3Processes.title: Analyze product quality data

l3Processes.title: Analyze product profitability

l3Processes.title: Conduct current state analysis
l3Processes.description:
In Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps, conducting a current state analysis
is the foundational step in the Forecast to Plan process. The system enables
organizations to capture and visualize their existing operational landscape,
including inventory levels, supply chain flows, procurement practices, and
financial health. Through integrated dashboards and reporting, users can assess
baseline performance, identify bottlenecks, and understand resource utilization.
This analysis informs strategic planning by providing a clear, data-driven
picture of where the organization stands, setting the stage for targeted
improvements and future growth.

l3Processes.title: Set strategic goals and incentives
l3Processes.description:
Within Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps, identifying strategic
initiatives involves leveraging analytics and scenario modeling to pinpoint
projects and programs that advance organizational priorities. The system
facilitates initiative tracking, resource allocation, and progress monitoring,
ensuring that each initiative is aligned with overall business strategy and
delivers measurable value.

l3Processes.title: Identify strategic initiatives
l3Processes.description:
Within Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps, identifying strategic
initiatives involves leveraging analytics and scenario modeling to pinpoint
projects and programs that advance organizational priorities. The system
facilitates initiative tracking, resource allocation, and progress monitoring,
ensuring that each initiative is aligned with overall business strategy and
delivers measurable value.

l3Processes.title: Develop long-range plan
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps supports long-range planning by
integrating financial, operational, and supply chain data into multi-year
forecasts and scenario analyses. Users can model growth trajectories, investment
needs, and market shifts, ensuring that the organization is prepared for future
challenges and opportunities.

l3Processes.title: Analyze and mitigate risks
l3Processes.description:
Risk analysis in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps leverages real-time
data and predictive analytics to identify vulnerabilities across the supply
chain, financial operations, and market environment. The system enables users to
assess risk exposure, model mitigation strategies, and monitor risk indicators,
supporting proactive decision-making.

l3Processes.title: Develop scenario and contingency plans
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps provides tools for scenario planning
and contingency management, allowing organizations to simulate disruptions,
evaluate alternative strategies, and prepare response plans. These capabilities
ensure resilience and agility in the face of uncertainty.

l3Processes.title: Manage environmental, social, and governance (ESG) plan
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enables organizations to manage ESG
initiatives by tracking compliance, sustainability metrics, and social impact.
The system supports reporting, goal-setting, and performance monitoring,
ensuring alignment with regulatory and stakeholder expectations.

l3Processes.title: Develop product strategy
l3Processes.description:
Product strategy in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves analyzing
market trends, customer needs, and competitive positioning to guide product
development and lifecycle management. The system supports portfolio analysis,
innovation tracking, and go-to-market planning.

l3Processes.title: Develop sales strategy
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps supports sales strategy development
through integrated CRM, sales analytics, and pipeline management. Users can
segment markets, forecast sales, and optimize pricing and promotions to drive
revenue growth.

l3Processes.title: Develop marketing strategy
l3Processes.description:
Marketing strategy in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps leverages
customer insights, campaign analytics, and market segmentation to plan and
execute targeted marketing initiatives. The system supports campaign management,
ROI tracking, and lead generation.

l3Processes.title: Develop budgets
l3Processes.description:
In Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations (Dynamics 365 finance and
operations apps), developing budgets is a foundational process that enables
organizations to define, manage, and monitor their financial plans across fiscal
periods. The budgeting module allows users to create budget models, allocate
funds to various accounts, and set up budget cycles that align with
organizational goals. Budgets can be developed collaboratively, leveraging
workflow approvals, versioning, and historical comparisons. Dynamics 365 finance
and operations apps supports both manual entry and data import, enabling
seamless integration with external tools like Excel. The system provides robust
reporting and analysis capabilities, allowing stakeholders to track budget vs.
actuals, perform variance analysis, and adjust plans dynamically. Budget
controls can be configured to enforce spending limits and trigger alerts when
thresholds are exceeded, ensuring fiscal discipline and transparency throughout
the organization.

l3Processes.title: Forecast revenue
l3Processes.description:
Forecasting revenue in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
projecting future income based on historical data, market trends, and business
drivers. The system supports multiple forecasting methodologies, including
statistical models, manual adjustments, and scenario planning. Users can create
revenue forecasts at various levels—by product, customer, region, or business
unit—and link them to budget cycles for comprehensive financial planning.
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enables real-time visibility into
forecast accuracy, variance analysis, and the impact of changing assumptions,
supporting agile decision-making and proactive risk management.

l3Processes.title: Plan capital allocation and investments
l3Processes.description:
Capital allocation and investment planning in Dynamics 365 finance and
operations apps enables organizations to strategically distribute financial
resources across projects, assets, and initiatives. The system supports capital
budgeting, tracking planned vs. actual investments, and evaluating ROI. Users
can set up approval workflows, monitor capital expenditures, and integrate
investment plans with overall financial forecasts and budgets. Dynamics 365
finance and operations apps provides tools for scenario analysis, enabling
organizations to assess the impact of different investment strategies on
financial health and growth objectives.

l3Processes.title: Plan operational allocation and investments
l3Processes.description:
Operational allocation and investment planning in Dynamics 365 finance and
operations apps involves distributing resources to support day-to-day business
activities, such as procurement, production, and logistics. The system allows
users to allocate budgets to operational units, track expenditures, and analyze
the effectiveness of resource deployment. Integration with inventory management
and procurement modules ensures alignment between financial plans and
operational execution, supporting cost control and process optimization.

l3Processes.title: Manage financial risks
l3Processes.description:
Risk management in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps encompasses
identifying, assessing, and mitigating financial risks that could impact
organizational objectives. The system supports risk tracking, scenario analysis,
and the implementation of controls to minimize exposure. Users can link risk
management activities to financial planning, ensuring that budgets and forecasts
account for potential uncertainties. Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
provides audit trails, compliance monitoring, and reporting tools to support
proactive risk management and regulatory adherence.

l3Processes.title: Plan workforce development
l3Processes.description:
Workforce development planning in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
involves forecasting staffing needs, budgeting for personnel costs, and aligning
workforce strategies with business goals. The system supports headcount
planning, salary budgeting, and integration with HR modules for talent
management. Users can analyze workforce trends, identify skill gaps, and plan
training investments to support organizational growth.

l3Processes.title: Set operational targets
l3Processes.description:
Setting operational targets in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
defining key performance indicators (KPIs), establishing benchmarks, and
aligning operational goals with financial plans. The system enables users to set
targets for revenue, cost, inventory levels, and other metrics, providing
real-time tracking and variance analysis. Operational targets can be linked to
budget cycles and monitored through dashboards and reports, supporting
continuous improvement and accountability.

l3Processes.title: React to supply chain signals
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enables organizations to respond
rapidly to supply chain signals such as stockouts, delays, or demand spikes. The
system supports real-time monitoring, alerting, and automated response workflows
to maintain operational continuity.

l3Processes.title: Allocate headcount to business units
l3Processes.description:
Headcount allocation in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enables
organizations to distribute personnel resources across business units, track
staffing levels, and budget for labor costs. The system supports integration
with HR modules, enabling seamless management of employee assignments, payroll,
and benefits. Users can analyze headcount trends, optimize workforce
distribution, and ensure alignment with strategic objectives.

l3Processes.title: Plan marketing campaigns
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enables organizations to plan, execute,
and analyze marketing campaigns, integrating customer data, channel performance,
and campaign ROI. The system supports campaign scheduling, budget allocation,
and performance measurement.

l3Processes.title: Forecast sales
l3Processes.description: This process involves estimating future sales revenue based on historical sales data, market trends, and other relevant inputs. It typically includes analyzing past sales performance, considering current market conditions, and projecting future sales figures to inform business decisions. Dynamics 365 Sales Insights empowers sellers by integrating relevant sales intelligence from Dynamics 365, Microsoft Teams, and Microsoft 365 into one platform. This solution uses artificial intelligence to generate automated insights, helping sellers build better customer relationships and win more deals. It includes features like Sales Accelerator and Assistant Studio, which enhance the efficiency of the sales process by providing predictive scoring and relationship insights. Additionally, Dynamics 365 Project Operations offers budget management and time-phased forecasting, which integrates sales, resourcing, project management, and finance teams into a single application.

l3Processes.title: Forecast demand
l3Processes.description: This process focuses on predicting future customer demand for products or services. It involves gathering historical demand data, using statistical models and algorithms to generate demand forecasts, and adjusting these forecasts based on real-time data and changing market conditions. The goal is to ensure that the business can meet customer demand efficiently. Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management (SCM) includes advanced demand planning capabilities. It leverages AI-driven integrated planning and insights across finance, sales, demand, and supply planning. This integration allows businesses to manage their global supply chain with real-time visibility, optimize forecasts, and make quick decisions. The demand planning app uses historical transactions and machine learning models to calculate future forecasts, ensuring accurate demand predictions. The SCM Demand & Supply Planning Newsletter highlights the seamless integration of demand planning with Dynamics 365 Finance and Supply Chain, leveraging Power Query and Azure Data Explorer for efficient data management.

l3Processes.title: Plan demand consensus
l3Processes.description: This process involves aligning demand forecasts across different departments and stakeholders within the organization. It includes reviewing and validating demand forecasts, aggregating them into a consensus plan, and ensuring that all parties agree on the projected demand figures. This collaborative approach helps to create a unified demand plan that can be used for strategic planning and resource allocation. Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management facilitates real-time collaboration across departments using Microsoft Teams. This helps quickly gain consensus on demand plans, ensuring that all stakeholders are aligned. The platform supports continuous planning and faster replenishment cycles, allowing planners to react to demand changes in near-real time. It also offers AI-powered predictions and historical data to improve forecast accuracy. The demand planning feature in Dynamics 365 SCM allows for consensus demand planning, integrating marketing, sales, and demand planning to create a unified plan.

l3Processes.title: Measure business performance
l3Processes.description:
Business performance measurement in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
provides real-time visibility into key metrics across financial, operational,
and supply chain domains. The system supports customizable dashboards, KPI
tracking, and exception reporting.

l3Processes.title: Plan aggregate supply
l3Processes.description: This process focuses on planning the overall supply to meet the aggregated demand forecast. It involves determining the total supply requirements, considering factors such as inventory levels, production capacity, and supplier capabilities. The goal is to ensure that the business can fulfill the demand efficiently and effectively, while optimizing resources and minimizing costs. Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management includes tools for planning aggregate supply to meet demand forecasts. It offers features like Planning Optimization, which enhances supply planning capabilities. The platform supports efficient data transfers and transparent logistics management, ensuring that businesses can fulfill demand efficiently. It also includes functionalities for managing inventory, production capacity, and supplier capabilities. The Supply Chain Management capabilities in Dynamics 365 help modernize supply chain operations, streamline procurement, and optimize fulfillment.

l3Processes.title: Plan workforce capacity
l3Processes.description:
Workforce capacity planning in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps analyzes
labor requirements, skill gaps, and scheduling needs to ensure operational
readiness. The system supports resource allocation, shift planning, and
productivity tracking.

l3Processes.title: Plan physical capacity
l3Processes.description:
Physical capacity planning in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps evaluates
facility constraints, equipment utilization, and storage needs to optimize
operational throughput. The system supports layout planning, capacity modeling,
and bottleneck analysis.

l3Processes.title: Balance supply and demand
l3Processes.description:
Balancing supply and demand in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
leverages integrated planning tools to align inventory levels, production
schedules, and customer orders. The system supports real-time adjustments,
exception management, and fulfillment optimization.

l3Processes.title: Plan risks and opportunities
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps supports risk and opportunity planning
by integrating market intelligence, operational data, and scenario modeling.
Users can assess potential disruptions, identify growth opportunities, and
develop mitigation strategies.

l3Processes.title: Project inventory levels
l3Processes.description:
Inventory projection in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps utilizes demand
forecasts, supply plans, and historical trends to estimate future inventory
positions. The system supports scenario analysis, safety stock optimization, and
replenishment planning.

l3Processes.title: Plan procurement
l3Processes.description:
In Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps, procurement planning is a core
supply chain function that ensures timely and cost-effective sourcing of goods
and materials. The system enables users to define procurement policies, manage
supplier relationships, and automate purchase order creation. Integrated
analytics provide visibility into supplier performance, lead times, and cost
trends, supporting informed decision-making and efficient replenishment.

l3Processes.title: Plan production
l3Processes.description:
Production planning in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps coordinates
manufacturing schedules, resource allocation, and material requirements to
ensure efficient fulfillment of demand. The system supports production order
management, capacity planning, and shop floor control, integrating with
inventory and procurement modules for seamless operations.

l3Processes.title: Plan logistics and distribution
l3Processes.description:
Logistics and distribution planning in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
optimizes the movement of goods from suppliers to warehouses and customers. The
system supports transportation management, route optimization, and carrier
integration, ensuring timely and cost-effective delivery.

l3Processes.title: Plan projects
l3Processes.description:
Project planning in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps integrates resource
management, scheduling, and cost tracking to support project-based operations.
The system enables users to define project milestones, allocate resources, and
monitor progress.

l3Processes.title: Plan service operations
l3Processes.description:
Service operations planning in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
coordinates field service activities, resource scheduling, and service order
management. The system supports dispatching, work order tracking, and service
analytics.

l3Processes.title: Allocate inventory
l3Processes.description:
Inventory allocation in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps ensures that
available stock is distributed optimally across locations and orders. The system
supports allocation rules, reservation management, and exception handling to
maximize fulfillment efficiency.

l3Processes.title: Load goods for shipping

apqc.description: Plan, transport, and deliver outbound product

l3Processes.description:
Loading in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves moving packed goods
to the dock, assigning them to shipments, and updating shipment status.

l3Processes.title: Report on and analyze trends
l3Processes.description:
Trend analysis in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps leverages historical
data and predictive analytics to identify patterns, forecast outcomes, and
inform decision-making. The system supports trend reporting, variance analysis,
and scenario modeling.

l3Processes.title: Develop continuous improvement initiatives
l3Processes.description:
In Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations, developing continuous improvement
initiatives is a core capability that empowers organizations to drive
operational excellence through data-driven insights. The system enables users to
identify inefficiencies, monitor key performance indicators, and launch targeted
improvement projects across supply chain, financial, and operational domains.
Leveraging integrated analytics, Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
supports root cause analysis, action tracking, and progress measurement,
ensuring that improvement efforts are aligned with strategic objectives and
deliver measurable results. Continuous improvement is embedded in the Forecast
to Plan cycle, fostering a culture of innovation and adaptability.

l3Processes.title: Conduct business performance reviews
l3Processes.description:
Business performance reviews in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps provide
a structured framework for evaluating operational and financial outcomes against
established targets. The system supports periodic review cycles, enabling users
to assess progress, identify variances, and recommend corrective actions.
Integrated reporting and analytics facilitate comprehensive reviews across
departments, ensuring transparency and accountability. Performance reviews are a
critical component of the Forecast to Plan process, driving informed
decision-making and continuous improvement.

l3Processes.title: Measure goal achievement
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enables organizations to measure goal
achievement by tracking progress against defined KPIs and operational targets.
The system provides real-time dashboards, automated alerts, and variance
analysis, ensuring that users can monitor achievement levels and respond
proactively to deviations. Goal measurement is integral to the Forecast to Plan
cycle, supporting continuous alignment between strategy and execution.

l3Processes.title: Analyze and segment customers and markets
l3Processes.description:
Customer and market segmentation in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
involves analyzing customer data, identifying market trends, and grouping
customers based on attributes such as purchasing behavior, geography, or
industry. The system supports advanced analytics, enabling organizations to
tailor financial plans, marketing strategies, and resource allocation to
specific segments. Integration with CRM and sales modules enhances visibility
and supports targeted decision-making.

l3Processes.title: Cross dock produced goods

apqc.description: Plan, transport, and deliver outbound product

l3Processes.description:
Cross-docking in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows produced goods
to be directly allocated to outbound orders, bypassing storage.

l3Processes.title: Analyze and segment goods and services
l3Processes.description:
Goods and services segmentation in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
enables organizations to categorize products and services, analyze
profitability, and optimize resource allocation. The system supports grouping by
product lines, cost centers, or other attributes, providing insights into
performance and supporting strategic planning. Integration with inventory and
sales modules ensures alignment between financial analysis and operational
execution.

l3Processes.title: Measure plan adherence
l3Processes.description:
Planning variance analysis in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps provides
deep insights into discrepancies between planned and actual outcomes. The system
supports root cause analysis, exception reporting, and corrective action
tracking, enabling organizations to refine planning processes and improve
forecast accuracy. Variance analysis is a vital feedback loop in the Forecast to
Plan process, driving continuous improvement and operational resilience.

l3Processes.title: Monitor financial performance
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps’s financial performance monitoring
capabilities provide real-time visibility into cost, expense, and budget
adherence across the enterprise. The system integrates financial data from
procurement, inventory, and operations, enabling users to track spending,
identify cost drivers, and ensure fiscal discipline. Financial monitoring is a
foundational element of the Forecast to Plan process, supporting informed
resource allocation and investment decisions.

l3Processes.title: Monitor operational performance
l3Processes.description:
Operational performance monitoring in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
delivers actionable insights into process efficiency, resource utilization, and
service levels. The system supports real-time tracking of operational metrics,
exception management, and continuous improvement initiatives. Operational
monitoring is embedded in the Forecast to Plan cycle, ensuring that
organizations can respond quickly to emerging issues and optimize performance.

l3Processes.title: Define organizational structure
l3Processes.description:
Defining the organizational structure in Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations
involves establishing the hierarchy of legal entities, operating units,
departments, and positions. This structure underpins all HR, financial, and
operational processes, enabling organizations to manage reporting relationships,
approval workflows, and resource allocation. The system supports flexible
modeling of business units, allowing for clear delineation of responsibilities,
reporting lines, and integration with other modules such as payroll, expense
management, and security roles.

l3Processes.title: Define operating hours and schedule
l3Processes.description:
Defining operating hours and schedules in Dynamics 365 finance and operations
apps allows organizations to set standard working times, shifts, and calendars
for departments and positions. This configuration supports time tracking,
payroll calculations, and resource planning. The system enables assignment of
schedules to workers and positions, facilitating compliance with labor
regulations and efficient workforce management.

l3Processes.title: Define human resources policies
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps supports the documentation and
enforcement of HR policies, including leave, attendance, benefits, and
compliance requirements. Policies can be linked to workflows, approval
processes, and employee records, ensuring consistent application across the
organization.

l3Processes.title: Develop code of conduct
l3Processes.description:
Organizations can record and communicate codes of conduct in Dynamics 365
finance and operations apps, linking them to onboarding, compliance, and
disciplinary processes. The system can track acknowledgments and integrate with
HR workflows for policy violations.

l3Processes.title: Define expense policies
l3Processes.description:
Initial review in progress. 
 55.05.050 Define Expense Policies.docx

l3Processes.title: Plan workforce
l3Processes.description: PG review to be done : 55.05.060 Plan workforce.docx

l3Processes.title: Develop communication strategy
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps can support communication strategies by
enabling automated notifications, workflow alerts, and integration with
collaboration tools. The system can be configured to send onboarding,
offboarding, and compliance communications to relevant stakeholders.

l3Processes.title: Budget workforce
l3Processes.description:
Planning and recruiting represent a foundational business process area that is
upstream of most business processes across a business. It's important that you
define department structures, and define and manage jobs and positions, so that
you can assign workers to tasks. In addition, before you can record time and
attendance, and pay employees, you must create and manage shifts, and define
work calendars and schedules. You should plan and recruit your workforce early
in the implementation, because the associated configurations are required to
support day-to-day operations and business processes.
Input from various stakeholders is required to complete these steps. The list
includes but isn't limited to the following roles:
 * Finance is required to define the relevant financial dimensions.
 * HR and Administrative stakeholders are required to define organizational
   details.
 * IT Operations might be required to import data.

l3Processes.title: List open positions
l3Processes.description:
Listing open positions in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows
organizations to maintain a catalog of vacancies, including job descriptions,
requirements, and status. The system supports integration with recruitment
modules, enabling HR to track open positions, manage approvals, and monitor
progress toward filling roles. Positions can be linked to organizational
structure and budget, providing a comprehensive view of workforce needs.

l3Processes.title: Recruit new talent
l3Processes.description:
Recruiting new talent in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
managing the end-to-end recruitment process, from posting vacancies to tracking
applicants and coordinating interviews. The system supports integration with
applicant tracking systems, enabling seamless data flow between recruitment and
HR modules. Dynamics 365 can automate workflows for approvals, candidate
communications, and onboarding preparation.

l3Processes.title: Hire for open positions
l3Processes.description:
Hiring for open positions in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps supports
the transition from candidate selection to employee onboarding. The system
enables HR to create worker records, assign positions, and initiate onboarding
workflows. Integration with payroll and compliance modules ensures new hires are
properly set up for compensation and regulatory requirements.

l3Processes.title: Onboard new employees
l3Processes.description:
Onboarding new employees in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
creating worker records, assigning roles and positions, and initiating
compliance and provisioning workflows. The system can automate notifications,
checklist completion, and integration with IT and payroll systems to ensure a
smooth onboarding experience.

l3Processes.title: Onboard new contractors
l3Processes.description:
Onboarding new contractors in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
creating contractor records, assigning roles, and managing compliance
requirements. The system supports integration with payroll and financial
modules, enabling organizations to track contractor status, payments, and
project assignments.

l3Processes.title: Identify workplace hazards

apqc.description: Develop and implement workforce strategy and policies

l3Processes.description:
In today's dynamic business landscape, the implementation of occupational health
and safety processes is essential for maintaining a safe and productive work
environment. With Dynamics 365, organizations can streamline and enhance their
safety management processes. The defined processes can cover areas such as the
following list:
 * Hazard identification
 * Safety protocol documentation
 * Safety condition analysis
 * Safety audits
 * Staff training
 * Injury or illness reporting
 * Treatment seeking
 * Cost-of-care determination
 * Safety communication
These processes collectively contribute to a safer and healthier workplace. When
you use Dynamics 365 with Microsoft 365 products, such as Viva Learning or Viva
Engage, you can streamline business processes and improve your overall health
and safety metrics.
These processes serve a twofold purpose in any organization. First, they ensure
the well-being of employees, reducing the risk of workplace accidents and
health-related issues. Second, they help organizations meet legal and regulatory
requirements, avoid potential liabilities, and enhance their reputation as
responsible corporate citizens. Dynamics 365 implementations facilitate the
systematic execution of these processes, providing an efficient and centralized
platform for managing safety-related data, incidents, and compliance.
One notable advantage of implementing occupational health and safety processes
within Dynamics 365 and Microsoft 365 is their flexibility. None of these
processes are mandatory prerequisites for the system's operation, allowing
organizations to tailor their safety management to their specific needs and
timelines. These processes can be easily integrated into the system at any stage
of its operation or even incrementally added as the organization evolves. This
adaptability ensures that companies can respond to changing safety requirements,
growth, and new regulations without major disruptions to their operations. This
way, Dynamics 365 becomes a valuable tool for long-term safety and compliance
management.

l3Processes.title: Document safety protocols

apqc.description: Develop and implement workforce strategy and policies

l3Processes.description:
Documenting safety protocols in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows
organizations to maintain a repository of safety policies, procedures, and
compliance requirements. The system can link protocols to training records,
incident management, and audit processes, ensuring that employees have access to
up-to-date safety information.

l3Processes.title: Audit workplace for safety

apqc.description: Develop and implement workforce strategy and policies

l3Processes.description:
Auditing workplace safety in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
scheduling and tracking safety inspections, recording findings, and managing
corrective actions. The system can automate audit workflows, generate compliance
reports, and support regulatory submissions.

l3Processes.title: Report an injury or illness

apqc.description: Develop and implement workforce strategy and policies

l3Processes.description:
Reporting injuries or illnesses in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
enables organizations to log incidents, track case management, and ensure
regulatory compliance. The system supports workflows for notification,
investigation, and resolution, integrating with HR and safety modules.

l3Processes.title: Verify employment

apqc.description: Develop and implement workforce strategy and policies

l3Processes.description:
Verifying employment in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
confirming worker status, employment dates, and job assignments. The system can
generate employment verification letters, support compliance checks, and provide
audit trails for regulatory purposes.

l3Processes.title: Recognize employees
l3Processes.description:
Recognizing employees in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enables
organizations to record awards, achievements, and acknowledgments. The system
can automate recognition workflows, link to compensation changes, and support
reporting for engagement programs.

l3Processes.title: Update worker information

apqc.description: Develop and implement workforce strategy and policies

l3Processes.description:
Updating worker information in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows
HR teams to maintain accurate employee records, including personal details, job
assignments, and status changes. The system supports integration with payroll,
benefits, and compliance modules, ensuring that updates are reflected across all
relevant processes.

l3Processes.title: Manage cases and requests

apqc.description: Develop and implement workforce strategy and policies

l3Processes.description:
Managing cases and requests in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enables
organizations to track HR, compliance, and safety cases, assign tasks, and
monitor resolution. The system supports workflows for case creation, escalation,
and closure, integrating with HR and compliance modules.

l3Processes.title: Promote employees
l3Processes.description:
Managing employee performance and growth is integral to the success of any
organization, serving as a cornerstone for fostering a motivated and skilled
workforce. In today's dynamic business landscape, Dynamics 365 plays a pivotal
role in streamlining this process.
Specifically, Dynamics 365 Human Resources and Customer Voice synergize to
provide a comprehensive solution. Dynamics 365 Human Resources facilitates the
definition of performance and review processes, competency requirements, career
paths, and learning plans. Meanwhile, Customer Voice helps in gathering valuable
feedback, contributing to a holistic view of employee performance. This
integration empowers organizations to align their workforce strategies with
real-time insights, ensuring a more effective approach to employee development.
When you use Dynamics 365 Human Resources together with Dynamics 365 Project
Operations, Supply Chain Management, or Field Service, you can manage the skills
required to perform work including project work, production work, or field work.
When you use Dynamics 365 Human Resources together with Dynamics 365 Commerce,
you can manage the shifts and training for the frontline workforce in a brick
and mortar or call center stores. Adding in Dynamics 365 Guides provides
augmented reality to help train and upskill employees while using Dynamics 365
Remote Assist can be used to assist field workers in their work to monitor or
upskill. While Dynamics 365 Customer Service includes features and ability to
integrate with telephony system to monitor calls and monitor the customer
service that is being delivered to customers.
Now, delving into the intricacies of managing employee performance and growth, a
series of interconnected business processes come into play. These processes
encompass defining performance and review procedures including the goal setting
process and documenting employee performance. Skill management is broken up into
several business process areas including establishing required competencies for
roles, maintaining a repository of skills and competencies, analyzing gaps
between skills required for the jobs to be done and the skills of the workforce.
The employee growth process including learning, and succession planning
containing process areas such managing course offerings, creating learning
plans, assigning courses, recording employee progress of assigned learning, and
analyzing/reporting on employee learning. These processes also include outlining
employee career paths and formulating succession plans. This comprehensive list
of processes is supported within Dynamics 365 and help organizations not only
automating but also optimizing the entire employee management lifecycle,
promoting efficiency and strategic decision-making for organizations aiming to
cultivate a high-performance culture.
The features and functionality for managing employee performance and growth
aren't mandatory to run Dynamics 365 Human Resources; any of the features can be
added at any time. Some downstream features such as skills competencies may be
required for downstream scheduling processes, for example, in Dynamics 365 Field
Service, Project Operations, and Supply Chain Management. In these cases, the
skill definition and management processes should be planned earlier in the
project to define the flow of data for the downstream processes.

l3Processes.title: Define employee career paths

apqc.description: Develop employee career plans and career paths

l3Processes.description:
Defining employee career paths in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
allows organizations to map progression opportunities, link positions to
competencies, and support succession planning. The system can track career
development plans, training requirements, and readiness for advancement.

l3Processes.title: Track skills and competencies
l3Processes.description: Dynamics 365 Human Resources enables organizations to track and manage skills and competencies by maintaining a centralized repository of employee capabilities, certifications, and education. It supports skill-gap analysis, competency mapping, and career development planning, allowing HR teams to compare job requirements with workforce skills, identify gaps, and assign targeted learning plans. Integrated analytics and course management tools help monitor progress and align employee development with organizational goals

l3Processes.title: Set employee growth goals
l3Processes.description: In Dynamics 365 Human Resources, you can set up Employee Growth Goals on the Performance Goals page. Each goal includes a name, description, start and end dates, and optional categories for organization. You can add detailed topics, comments, measurable targets, and mark stretch goals. Attach supporting documents, link performance journals, and create reusable templates for future goals. Goals help track progress across periods and reviews, but they’re not mandatory for performance evaluations.

l3Processes.title: Perform a skill gap analysis
l3Processes.description: In Dynamics 365 Human Resources You can create a skill profile analysis to view a list of a person's competencies. You can create a skill gap analysis to compare a person’s skills and the skills required for a job. Skill-mapping searches return results for criteria you enter by looking through the following information: Skills, Education, Certificates, Positions and Project experience

l3Processes.title: Asses worker performance
l3Processes.description: In Dynamics 365 Human Resource, performance reviews (called discussions) provide a flexible way to assess employee performance through one-on-one meetings or detailed reviews. Reviews can include goals, comments, performance journal activities, and competency ratings. You can use templates for consistency, capture measurable results, and store snapshots for historical tracking. This process supports continuous feedback, development planning, and formal evaluations aligned with organizational standard

l3Processes.title: Define learning paths
l3Processes.description: In Dynamics 365 Human Resources, "learning paths" refer to either the course-based training programs (virtual or in-person) that HR professionals can create and manage. HR professionals can create, assign, and manage participants for these courses, which can include setting up registration deadlines and participant limits.

l3Processes.title: Train employees
l3Processes.description:
Training employees in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
scheduling courses, tracking attendance, and managing certifications. The system
can automate training workflows, generate compliance reports, and integrate with
HR and payroll modules.

l3Processes.title: Transfer workers
l3Processes.description:
Transferring workers in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
updating worker records to reflect new positions, departments, or locations. The
system supports workflow-driven approvals, automatic updates to payroll and
benefits, and integration with organizational hierarchy.

l3Processes.title: Develop leave and absence policies

apqc.description: Develop and implement workforce strategy and policies

l3Processes.description:
Managing employee time and attendance is a critical aspect of organizational
efficiency, playing a pivotal role in ensuring optimal workforce productivity
and compliance. In today's dynamic business environment, the use of Dynamics 365
products, such as Human Resources, Project Operations, Field Service, and Supply
Chain Management, can help organizations streamline these crucial processes.
These tools enable an interconnected approach to time and attendance management
including leave and absence management, fostering a more cohesive and efficient
work environment.
The process of managing employee time and attendance encompasses several key
business processes that are supported by Dynamics 365. Organizations typically
start by defining and managing time and attendance policies to align with their
specific requirements. Dynamics 365 Human Resources supports flexible
configuration for leave and absence policies and plans including sick time, paid
time off, selling or borrowing leave, and the leave accrual process.
In addition, you can use Dynamics 365 Human Resources to record employee
absences and facilitate requests for time off, all seamlessly integrated into
the employee self-service experience. In addition, Dynamics 365 Commerce
supports the ability to create and managing shifts.
Depending on your industry and time-reporting needs, Dynamics 365 allows
employees to create and submit timesheets. This includes time clocks
functionality with Dynamics 365 Commerce, Human Resources, and Supply Chain
Management. For more complex manufacturing environments, you can use job card
terminals on the production floor or the Time and attendance module in Dynamics
365 Supply Chain Management, providing a comprehensive overview of their work
hours and production completed. Supervisors can then efficiently review and
approve timesheets, ensuring accuracy and compliance.
When you use Dynamics 365 Field Service or Project Operations, you can record
time against specific work orders or projects for detailed time reporting that
can be easily allocated to the right financial resources for billing, cost
recognition, and revenue recognition. The flexibility of the Dynamics 365
platform also allows for easy adjustments and corrections to timesheets,
ensuring that the time and attendance data remains accurate and reflective of
the dynamic nature of business operations.
Determining the high-level architecture of your solution early in the
implementation for your time and attendance business processes is important, but
you can leave the configuration and setup to the later stages of your project.

l3Processes.title: Record employee absences

apqc.description: Develop and implement workforce strategy and policies

l3Processes.description:
In base Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations, recording employee absences enables
organizations to track leave, sick days, and other types of absences. The system
supports integration with HR modules, allowing for automated accrual
calculations, absence approvals, and reporting. Absence data can be used for
payroll processing, compliance, and workforce planning.

l3Processes.title: Manage shifts

apqc.description: Develop and implement workforce strategy and policies

l3Processes.description:
Managing shifts in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows organizations
to define work schedules, assign shifts to employees, and track attendance. The
system supports shift planning, overtime calculations, and integration with
payroll and compliance modules.

l3Processes.title: Request time off

apqc.description: Develop and implement workforce strategy and policies

l3Processes.description:
Requesting time off in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enables
employees to submit leave requests, which can be routed through approval
workflows. The system tracks leave balances, integrates with payroll, and
supports compliance with company policies.

l3Processes.title: Record employee time

apqc.description: Develop and implement workforce strategy and policies

l3Processes.description:
Recording employee time in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows
organizations to capture hours worked, overtime, and project allocations. The
system supports integration with payroll, project accounting, and compliance
modules, enabling accurate compensation and reporting.

l3Processes.title: Request travel

apqc.description: Develop and implement workforce strategy and policies
