l3Processes.title: Manage employee travel

apqc.description: Develop and implement workforce strategy and policies

l3Processes.description:
Managing employee travel in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
tracking travel arrangements, expenses, and compliance with company policies.
The system supports integration with expense management and payroll modules.

l3Processes.title: Define compensation policies
l3Processes.description:
In today's competitive business landscape, attracting, retaining, and developing
a top-performing workforce is essential for organizational success. Effective
management of worker compensation, benefits, and payroll is a critical component
of this strategy. It ensures that employees are compensated fairly and receive
the benefits they deserve while also streamlining administrative processes and
reducing costs. Dynamics 365 provides a comprehensive set of tools to streamline
and enhance various aspects of worker compensation, benefits, and payroll
management, from creating customized compensation plans including employee's
performance rating and managing benefit enrollments to processing payroll
accurately and efficiently.
Managing worker compensation, benefits, and payroll processes serves two crucial
purposes in any organization. Firstly, they ensure the accurate and timely
payment of employee wages, benefits, and compensation, maintaining employee
satisfaction and reducing financial risks. Secondly, they help organizations
comply with labor laws and regulations, minimize legal liabilities, and promote
a transparent and compliant workplace culture. Dynamics 365 implementations
streamline and enhance these processes, providing a centralized platform for
managing employee compensation, benefits, and payroll data securely and
efficiently.

l3Processes.title: Define benefit offerings
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps supports the definition of benefit
offerings, including health, retirement, and other employee benefits. The system
enables HR to configure benefit plans, eligibility rules, and enrollment
periods, integrating with payroll and compliance modules.

l3Processes.title: Manage benefits enrollment
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enables organizations to manage
benefits enrollment, allowing employees to select benefit plans, update
coverage, and track enrollment status. The system supports automated workflows,
eligibility checks, and integration with payroll for deductions.

l3Processes.title: Reimburse workers for expenses
l3Processes.description:
Initial review in progress. 
 Document link : 55.70.045 Reimburse Workers for expenses.docx

l3Processes.title: Pay employees
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps supports payroll processing by
integrating with HR modules to calculate pay, generate payroll journals, and
process payments. The system enables organizations to manage payroll schedules,
deductions, and compliance reporting, integrating with financial modules for
accounting and reconciliation.

l3Processes.title: Return goods to vendor

apqc.description: Plan, transport, and deliver outbound product

l3Processes.description:
Returning goods to vendor in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
creating return orders, processing shipments back to vendors, and updating
inventory and financial records.

l3Processes.title: Record life events

apqc.description: Manage human resource information systems HRIS

l3Processes.description:
Recording life events in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows HR to
track changes such as marriage, birth, or other qualifying events that impact
benefits and compensation. The system supports automated workflows for updating
records, adjusting benefits, and ensuring compliance.

l3Processes.title: Manage compensation changes
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enables organizations to manage
compensation changes, including salary adjustments, bonuses, and promotions. The
system supports approval workflows, audit trails, and integration with payroll
and financial modules to ensure accurate processing.

l3Processes.title: Conduct succession planning
l3Processes.description:
Succession planning in Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations enables organizations
to identify key roles, assess talent pipelines, and prepare for future
leadership transitions. The system supports tracking of critical positions,
readiness assessments, and development plans, integrating with HR records and
organizational hierarchies to ensure business continuity.

l3Processes.title: Conduct exit interviews
l3Processes.description:
Exit interviews in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allow organizations
to capture feedback from departing employees, analyze trends, and identify
opportunities for improvement. The system can automate interview scheduling,
collect responses, and generate reports for HR review.

l3Processes.title: Plan worker retirement
l3Processes.description:
Planning worker retirement in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
tracking retirement eligibility, managing retirement dates, and coordinating
benefits and offboarding activities. The system supports automated workflows for
notifications, benefits adjustments, and compliance reporting.

l3Processes.title: Terminate workers
l3Processes.description:
Terminating workers in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
updating worker status to inactive, removing system access, and processing final
payroll and benefits. The system supports automated workflows for approvals,
asset collection, and compliance reporting, ensuring a smooth and secure
offboarding process.

l3Processes.title: Furlough workers
l3Processes.description:
Furloughing workers in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
updating worker status to inactive or furloughed, adjusting payroll and
benefits, and managing system access. The system supports workflows for
notifications, asset collection, and compliance, ensuring accurate tracking and
reporting.

l3Processes.title: Track employee learning
l3Processes.description:
Tracking employee learning in Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations enables
organizations to record training activities, certifications, and development
plans. The system supports integration with learning management systems,
allowing HR to monitor course completion, compliance training, and skill
development across the workforce.

l3Processes.title: Monitor employee satisfaction
l3Processes.description:
Monitoring employee satisfaction in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
involves collecting feedback through surveys, analyzing engagement metrics, and
generating reports for HR leadership. The system can automate survey
distribution, aggregate responses, and support action planning for workforce
improvement.

l3Processes.title: Analyze worker performance
l3Processes.description:
Analyzing worker performance in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows
organizations to track goals, appraisals, and productivity metrics. The system
supports performance review workflows, integrates with compensation modules, and
provides dashboards for management insight.

l3Processes.title: Analyze safety achievement
l3Processes.description:
Analyzing safety achievement in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
involves tracking safety incidents, compliance training, and achievement of
safety goals. The system can generate reports, support regulatory submissions,
and integrate with incident management modules.

l3Processes.title: Monitor human capital expenses
l3Processes.description:
Monitoring human capital expenses in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
enables organizations to track payroll, benefits, and other employee-related
costs. The system provides financial reporting, budget analysis, and integration
with HR and accounting modules for comprehensive expense management.

l3Processes.title: Analyze and reconcile compensation and benefits
l3Processes.description:
Analyzing and reconciling compensation and benefits in Dynamics 365 finance and
operations apps involves reviewing payroll, benefits costs, and compensation
changes. The system supports financial reconciliation, audit trails, and
integration with HR and accounting modules for accurate reporting.

l3Processes.title: Define warehouse processes
l3Processes.description:
Defining warehouse processes in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
involves establishing the operational procedures for receiving, put-away,
picking, packing, shipping, inventory movements, and cycle counting. This
includes configuring work templates, location directives, and process flows that
dictate how goods move through the warehouse, ensuring efficiency and compliance
with business requirements.

l3Processes.title: Design warehouse layout
l3Processes.description:
Designing warehouse layout in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
setting up the physical and logical structure of the warehouse, including zones,
aisles, bins, shelves, and locations. It requires configuring location profiles,
location formats, and mapping the warehouse structure in the system to support
efficient storage and retrieval of goods.

l3Processes.title: Document warehouse policies
l3Processes.description:
Documenting warehouse policies in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps means
formalizing rules and procedures for inventory handling, quality checks, safety,
compliance, and operational standards. These policies are reflected in system
configurations, user roles, approval workflows, and audit trails to ensure
consistent and compliant warehouse operations.

l3Processes.title: Manage work assignments
l3Processes.description:
Managing work assignments in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
allocating tasks such as picking, put-away, cycle counting, and replenishment to
warehouse workers. The system supports work creation, assignment, tracking, and
completion, with options for manual or automated assignment based on workload,
location, or skill.

l3Processes.title: Receive goods
l3Processes.description:
Receiving goods in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves registering
the arrival of products against purchase orders, verifying quantities, and
updating inventory records. The process can include inspection, handling
discrepancies, and posting product receipts.

l3Processes.title: Track supplier managed and consignment inventory
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps supports supplier managed and
consignment inventory by allowing organizations to track inventory owned by
suppliers but stored at their own locations. The system enables visibility of
consigned stock, triggers ownership transfer upon consumption, and supports
supplier reconciliation and reporting.

l3Processes.title: Track customer managed inventory and consigned inventory

apqc.description: Track third-party logistics storage and shipping performance

l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows tracking of customer managed and
consigned inventory, where inventory is owned by the customer but stored at the
organization's location. The system supports visibility, usage tracking, and
reconciliation for customer-owned stock.

l3Processes.title: Process inventory movements
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps supports inventory movements such as
transfers between locations, warehouses, or legal entities. Movements can be
tracked via transfer orders, journals, or automated workflows, ensuring accurate
inventory records and financial postings.

l3Processes.title: Stage inventory
l3Processes.description:
Staging inventory in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
temporarily holding inventory in a designated area before final put-away,
picking, or shipment. Staging supports efficient warehouse operations and can be
tracked by location and status.

l3Processes.title: Count inventory
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps supports inventory counting through
physical inventory counts and cycle counts. The system enables count scheduling,
variance reporting, and adjustment posting, supporting compliance and inventory
accuracy.

l3Processes.title: Adjust inventory levels
l3Processes.description:
Inventory adjustments in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allow users to
correct inventory balances due to damage, loss, overages, or other
discrepancies. Adjustments are posted via inventory journals and can be tracked
for audit and compliance.

l3Processes.title: Put away received goods
l3Processes.description:
Put-away in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves moving received
goods from the receiving area to storage locations, often using system-directed
logic based on product/location setup, and can include scanning for
confirmation.

l3Processes.title: Cross dock received goods to outbound orders
l3Processes.description:
Cross-docking in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows received goods
to be directly allocated to outbound orders, bypassing storage, and is typically
system-driven based on order demand.

l3Processes.title: Quarantine received goods
l3Processes.description:
Quarantine in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves placing received
goods in a quality hold location for inspection or disposition before they are
available for use or sale.

l3Processes.title: Allocate goods

apqc.description: Plan, transport, and deliver outbound product

l3Processes.description:
Allocation in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps reserves inventory for
outbound orders, ensuring goods are available for picking. The system can
allocate based on order priority, location, and inventory status.

l3Processes.title: Release goods for picking

apqc.description: Plan, transport, and deliver outbound product

l3Processes.description:
Releasing goods for picking in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps moves
orders to a status where pick tickets are generated, and warehouse staff are
instructed to pick items.

l3Processes.title: Pick goods

apqc.description: Plan, transport, and deliver outbound product

l3Processes.description:
Picking in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves warehouse staff
retrieving items from storage locations, often using system-directed logic and
scanning for confirmation.

l3Processes.title: Pack goods

apqc.description: Plan, transport, and deliver outbound product

l3Processes.description:
Packing in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves consolidating
picked items into containers, generating packing slips, and preparing goods for
shipment.

l3Processes.title: Build a quality plan for a product
Scrap defective inventory
l3Processes.description:
In base Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations, scrapping defective inventory is
performed using an inventory adjustment journal. Warehouse staff identify items
that are damaged, expired, or otherwise unfit for use. They create an inventory
journal entry, select the relevant items and quantities, and specify a reason
code (such as "scrap" or "defective"). Posting the journal reduces on-hand
inventory and updates financial records, ensuring accurate stock and cost
accounting. The process may include capturing supporting notes, attaching
documentation, and, if configured, triggering workflow approvals for audit
purposes.

l3Processes.title: Plan loads

apqc.description: Plan, transport, and deliver outbound product

l3Processes.description:
The Manage freight and transportation business process area encompasses the
activities that are done in an organization's transportation management process.
All distribution organizations must be able to track and manage transportation
costs and operations so that they can optimize this critical part of the supply
chain. Meeting this goal requires cross-organization collaboration among
departments such as Logistics, Procurement, Planning, Sales, and Operations.
The exact process for the Manage freight and transportation area varies from
business to business and depends on the industry. However, the basic steps are
the same: The need for transportation is identified. Identification of this need
triggers planning activities, including rating and routing operations. Dock
appointments can then be scheduled for pickup and/or delivery, and necessary
logistics paperwork can be generated. After transportation is completed, freight
invoices can be processed and reconciled against estimates. The definition of
these steps is done as part of the Inventory to deliver end-to-end business
processes and refined in the Define and manage warehouse operations business
process area.
It's essential that you define and establish the business process for Managing
freight and transportation when you first begin to implement Dynamics 365 Supply
Chain Management. This process is a fundamental element for any distribution
company, because it forms the backbone of efficient supply chain operations.

apqc.description: Plan, transport, and deliver outbound product

l3Processes.title: Route loads

apqc.description: Plan, transport, and deliver outbound product

l3Processes.description:
Routing loads in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves determining
the optimal path for shipments, assigning carriers, and sequencing stops for
multi-drop loads. The system can automate routing based on rules and carrier
contracts.

l3Processes.title: Schedule dock appointments

apqc.description: Plan, transport, and deliver outbound product

l3Processes.description:
Scheduling dock appointments in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows
coordination of inbound and outbound shipments, reserving dock time slots, and
managing yard activities.

l3Processes.title: Print shipping documentation

apqc.description: Plan, transport, and deliver outbound product

l3Processes.description:
Printing shipping documentation in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
includes generating packing slips, bills of lading, and shipping labels as part
of the outbound process.

l3Processes.title: Process freight invoices

apqc.description: Process and audit carrier invoices and documents

l3Processes.description:
Processing freight invoices in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
matching carrier invoices to shipments, validating charges, and posting to
accounts payable.

l3Processes.title: Reconcile freight

apqc.description: Process and audit carrier invoices and documents

l3Processes.description:
Freight reconciliation in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps compares
planned versus actual freight costs, identifies discrepancies, and supports cost
allocation

l3Processes.title: Define warehouse management KPIs
l3Processes.description:
In base Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps, users can define warehouse
management KPIs (Key Performance Indicators) such as inventory accuracy, order
picking accuracy, put-away efficiency, and cycle count compliance. These KPIs
are typically configured using Power BI, embedded analytics, or custom reports,
leveraging transactional data from warehouse operations.

l3Processes.title: Measure warehouse performance
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps provides tools to measure warehouse
performance, including standard and custom reports on picking rates, put-away
times, inventory movements, and worker productivity. These can be visualized
through dashboards or exported for further analysis.

l3Processes.title: Analyze inventory levels
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps offers real-time inventory visibility,
allowing users to analyze inventory levels by location, product, batch, or other
dimensions. Standard reports and inquiries support stock analysis, reorder point
monitoring, and inventory valuation.

l3Processes.title: Report on inventory quality
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps can report on inventory quality using
quality orders, nonconformance tracking, and quarantine management. Reports can
show failed inspections, scrap rates, and quality trends.

l3Processes.title: Map value streams
l3Processes.description:
Mapping value streams involves analyzing and visualizing the flow of materials
and information from order intake to finished goods, identifying bottlenecks and
opportunities for efficiency.

l3Processes.title: Develop order management policies
l3Processes.description: This process is designed to define the rules, conditions, and configurations that govern how sales orders are handled across different channels such as call centers, eCommerce platforms, and traditional sales operations. It ensures consistency, compliance, and efficiency in order processing, fulfillment, and customer service. These scenarios reflect the need to tailor policies based on the operational context whether it's a centralized warehouse, a distributed retail network, or an online storefront.  Functional Elements in Dynamics 365 Microsoft Learn outlines two main types of policies used in Intelligent Order Management:  Filter Policies: These validate data as it flows through orchestration. For example, a filter policy might route orders from a specific region to a designated fulfillment center.  Execution Policies: These update or enrich data during orchestration. For instance, assigning a fulfillment location when an order exceeds a certain quantity.   Each policy includes: Policy Name and Type Criticality (Information vs. Critical) Status (Published or Unpublished) Associated Entity (Order, Order Product, Fulfillment Order, etc.) These policies are configured in the Orchestration > Policies section of Dynamics 365 Intelligent Order Management. Strategic Value Developing clear order management policies: Reduces fulfillment errors and delays Enables automation and orchestration of order flows Supports omnichannel consistency Minimizes customizations by leveraging standard Microsoft capabilities

l3Processes.title: Manage sales channels
l3Processes.description: This process is designed to help organizations define, configure, and optimize the various channels through which they sell products or services such as retail stores, eCommerce platforms, call centers, and partner networks. It ensures that each channel is aligned with the company's sales strategy, pricing models, and fulfillment capabilities. This process is especially important in omnichannel environments where consistency, visibility, and control across all sales touchpoints are critical for customer satisfaction and operational efficiency.  Key Capabilities in Dynamics 365 Dynamics 365 supports this process by enabling:  Retail Channel Management: Configure and manage physical stores, online storefronts, and call centers as distinct sales channels with tailored workflows and policies.  Flexible Pricing Management: Apply different pricing strategies per channel, including discounts, promotions, and trade allowances.  Catalogs and Assortments: Define product assortments and catalogs specific to each channel to ensure relevant offerings. Omnichannel Commerce: Enable seamless customer experiences across channels, including unified order history, inventory visibility, and returns processing.  Strategic Value Managing sales channels effectively leads to: Improved Margin Control: By tailoring pricing and promotions per channel.  Increased Visibility: Through centralized monitoring of sales performance across all channels.  Simplified Operations: By standardizing processes and reducing manual interventions.  Enhanced Customer Experience: With consistent service and product availability regardless of the channel.

l3Processes.title: Define customer classifications
l3Processes.description: The process refers to the structured segmentation of customers into meaningful groups based on shared characteristics, behaviors, or financial attributes. This classification supports targeted sales strategies, credit risk management, and operational efficiency across the customer lifecycle.  Defining customer classifications allows organizations to:  Segment customers by attributes such as industry, geography, size, payment behavior, or strategic value.  Apply differentiated policies for pricing, credit limits, collections, and service levels. Improve reporting and analytics by enabling grouped insights into customer performance, profitability, and risk.  Support automation in workflows like invoicing, dunning, and sales forecasting by applying rules based on classification.    In Dynamics 365 Finance, customer classification is typically managed through:  Customer Groups. These are configured in the system to assign shared financial and operational settings (e.g., posting profiles, payment terms, tax groups) to multiple customers. This is done via: Accounts receivable > Setup > Customer groups Accounts receivable > Setup > Customer posting profiles  Customer Definitions Entity: This data entity supports importing and updating customer records, including classification fields like: CUSTOMERGROUPID (required)PARTYTYPE (Organization or Person) PAYMENTTERMS, SALECURRENCYCODE, SALESTAXGROUP, etc. These fields help define the customer's financial and operational profile. Customer Statistics and Risk Scoring: Classification can also feed into analytics such as customer turnover, aging, and risk scoring

l3Processes.title: Develop sales catalogs

apqc.description: Define offering and customer value proposition

l3Processes.description:
The process focuses on how organizations define, structure, and manage product offerings across various sales channels. The "Develop sales catalogs" process is designed to help businesses:  Classify and group products into catalogs  Configure assortments for different sales channels  Enable efficient product discovery and selection  Support pricing, promotions, and inventory visibility   This process ensures that customers and sales teams can easily find and transact with the right products, tailored to their region, channel, or customer segment.  
 The process includes the following capabilities:  Create Product Catalogs: Organize products into logical groupings (e.g., Electronics, Office Supplies, Accessories).  Configure Product Assortments: Define which products are available in which channels (e.g., online vs. retail).  Use Sales Categories: Track non-inventoried items using categories instead of item numbers.  Build Catalog Hierarchies: Support unlimited levels and multiple hierarchies per item (e.g., Manufacturer > Size > Touchscreen).  Define Attributes: Use structured metadata (e.g., text, currency, Boolean) to enrich product definitions. This structure supports both B2B and B2C scenarios and is especially useful in omnichannel environments.  Strategic Value Developing robust sales catalogs enables: Faster Sales Cycles: By making it easier for customers and reps to find the right products.  Channel-Specific Customization: Tailor offerings to different markets or customer types. Improved Data Governance: Standardized product definitions reduce errors and duplication.  Enhanced Reporting: Hierarchies and attributes support better analytics and forecasting.

l3Processes.title: Develop sales pricing strategy
l3Processes.description: The Develop Sales Pricing Strategy business process defines how an organization sets, manages, and evolves its pricing structures to align with market conditions, business goals, and customer expectations. It is a critical component of the broader Order to Cash cycle and directly influences profitability, competitiveness, and customer satisfaction. Core Components Regular Sales Pricing: Establishing base prices for products and services based on cost, market demand, and strategic positioning. Discounting: Designing and applying volume discounts, promotional offers, and loyalty-based incentives. Freight and Other Charges: Incorporating shipping costs, handling fees, and surcharges into final pricing. Coupons and Promotions: Managing time-bound, customer-specific coupon codes and promotional pricing. Price Adjustments: Revising prices based on market shifts, competitive analysis, and internal profitability reviews.  Process Flow Analyze Market Data: Gather insights on competitors, customer behavior, and cost structures. Evaluate Pricing Strategy: Assess alignment with business goals and financial targets. Implement Pricing: Configure systems to apply pricing rules across channels. Revise Strategy: Monitor performance and adjust pricing as needed.  Stakeholders Involved Pricing Manager: Designs and maintains pricing structures. Sales Operations Manager: Ensures pricing aligns with sales goals and is correctly applied. Finance Controller: Validates pricing for profitability and compliance. E-commerce Manager: Manages digital pricing, coupons, and tiered pricing models.  Dynamics 365 Support Microsoft Dynamics 365 provides robust tools to support this process: Configuration & Automation: Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management: Enables configuration of base prices, discounts, freight charges, and promotional pricing across products and channels. Dynamics 365 Commerce: Supports coupon management, tiered pricing, and loyalty programs for online sales. Dynamics 365 Sales: Integrates pricing strategies into lead generation and opportunity management.   Data-Driven Decision Making: Built-in analytics dashboards help pricing managers evaluate performance and identify opportunities for price adjustments. Integration with Power BI allows deeper insights into pricing trends, customer behavior, and profitability.   Operational Efficiency: Automated workflows ensure consistent application of pricing rules across sales channels. Real-time updates allow agile responses to market changes and promotional campaigns.

apqc.description: Perform testing using the standard testing procedure

l3Processes.title: Define trade allowances

apqc.description: Design and manage customer loyalty program

l3Processes.description:
The Define Trade Allowances business process outlines how organizations create and manage promotional incentives offered to customers in exchange for meeting specific volume or behavioral goals. These allowances are commonly used in industries such as consumer packaged goods (CPG), food and beverage, wholesale distribution, and retail. Core Components Bill-Backs
Retroactive payments made to customers based on actual sales performance. These are calculated after the sale and processed as deductions in accounts receivable.  Off-Invoice Discounts
Immediate reductions applied directly to the invoice at the time of sale. These are simple to administer and provide instant value to the customer.  Promotional Funds
Budgeted amounts allocated to support trade allowance programs. These funds are tracked and reconciled to ensure alignment with financial policies.  Lump Sum Payments
One-time payments made to customers for agreed-upon promotional activities or services, such as in-store displays or advertising.  Traditional Rebates
Periodic payments based on cumulative purchases or performance metrics. These are often used to reward long-term customer loyalty.   Process Flow Identify Allowance Requirements
Define the types of trade allowances needed based on business goals and customer expectations.  Analyze Trade Data
Review historical sales, customer behavior, and market trends to inform allowance design.  Develop Allowance Criteria
Set eligibility rules, performance thresholds, and funding limits.  Evaluate Allowances
Assess the financial impact and strategic alignment of proposed programs.  Implement Allowances
Configure systems and processes to apply allowances across sales channels.  Revise Allowances
Monitor performance and adjust programs as needed to optimize effectiveness.   Stakeholders Trade Promotion Manager
Designs and manages trade allowance programs, including eligibility, funding, and tracking.  Accounts Receivable Manager
Processes customer claims and ensures accurate financial reconciliation.  Sales Director
Negotiates terms with customers and monitors program effectiveness.  Finance Controller
Oversees budgeting and compliance with financial policies.   Dynamics 365 Support Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management
Enables setup and automation of trade allowance agreements, bill-back claims, and promotional fund tracking.  Accounts Receivable Integration
Processes deductions and reconciles short-pays against approved claims.  Analytics and Reporting
Built-in dashboards and Power BI integration support performance analysis and forecasting.

l3Processes.title: Define order risk management strategy

apqc.description: Design and manage customer loyalty program

l3Processes.description:
The Define Risk Management Strategy business process helps organizations identify, assess, and mitigate fraud and operational risks across sales channels and fulfillment processes. It is a critical component of the broader Order to Cash cycle and supports financial integrity, customer trust, and operational resilience. Core Components Order Holds
Apply automated or manual holds on sales orders based on credit exposure, fraud risk, or policy violations.  Fraud Detection
Use rule-based and AI-driven tools to detect suspicious transactions, bot activity, and payment anomalies.  Return Abuse Monitoring
Track patterns of excessive or fraudulent returns and apply exception handling policies.  Payment Risk Scoring
Evaluate transactions using risk scores to determine approval, hold, or escalation paths.  Bot Detection
Identify and block automated attacks or scraping attempts on e-commerce platforms.  Exception Handling
Manage edge cases such as mismatched payments, duplicate orders, or unusual customer behavior.   Process Flow Identify Risk Areas
Define risk categories such as fraud, credit exposure, and operational vulnerabilities.  Analyze Risk Data
Review historical incidents, transaction patterns, and customer behavior.  Develop Risk Rules
Create detection logic, thresholds, and escalation paths.  Implement Controls
Configure systems to enforce risk rules and automate responses.  Monitor and Revise
Continuously evaluate effectiveness and adjust strategies based on performance and emerging threats.   Stakeholders Fraud Risk Manager
Designs detection rules, monitors alerts, and manages escalations.  Finance Controller
Oversees credit policies, order holds, and financial exposure thresholds.  E-commerce Manager
Implements fraud prevention strategies for online channels.  Customer Service Lead
Monitors return abuse and exception handling policies.   Dynamics 365 Support Dynamics 365 Fraud Protection
Provides real-time fraud detection, risk scoring, and bot protection.  Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management
Supports order holds, credit policies, and exception handling.  Dynamics 365 Commerce
Enables fraud detection for online channels, including bot detection and payment risk scoring.

l3Processes.title: Define accounts receivable strategy

apqc.description: Design and manage customer loyalty program

l3Processes.description:
The Define Accounts Receivable Strategy business process outlines how an organization establishes policies and rules for invoicing, payment terms, and cash discounting. This strategy ensures financial transparency, supports efficient collections, and aligns with broader business goals. It does not include the operational execution of invoicing or payment processing. Core Components Cash Discounting Rules
Define discount terms to incentivize early payments. Examples include: 3% discount if paid within 10 days 1% discount if paid within 30 days
Discounts can be configured with or without sales tax, depending on compliance needs.  Invoicing Strategy
Establish invoice formats, approval workflows, and posting rules. This includes: Automated invoice generation Invoice posting workflows Customer account adjustments
These elements ensure timely billing and reduce manual errors.  Payment Rules
Set flexible payment terms based on: Fixed periods (e.g., net 30, net 60) Installment schedules Percentage allocations
Rules should be aligned with customer segmentation and financial policies.  Process Flow Define Strategy Requirements
Identify business goals, customer expectations, and compliance needs.  Design Terms and Discounts
Configure payment terms and discount policies based on historical data and market practices.  Establish Approval Workflows
Implement controls for invoice posting and payment journal approvals.  Integrate with Financial Models
Ensure strategy is embedded in customer and financial data models from the start.  Monitor and Refine
Use analytics to assess performance and adjust strategy as needed.   Stakeholders Accounts Receivable Manager
Oversees invoicing, payment application, and reconciliation.  Finance Controller
Ensures compliance with financial policies and validates discounting and payment rules.  ERP Implementation Lead
Aligns strategy with Dynamics 365 configuration and data models.   Dynamics 365 Support Finance & Business Central
Automates invoice generation, payment term configuration, and discount application.  Workflow Management
Supports approval processes for invoice posting and customer adjustments.  Analytics & Reporting
Dashboards and Power BI integration help monitor AR performance and cash flow.

l3Processes.title: Define credit and collections strategy

apqc.description: Design and manage customer loyalty program

l3Processes.description:
This business process defines how an organization establishes policies and controls to manage customer credit risk and ensure timely collections. It is a critical part of the Order to Cash cycle and supports financial governance, cash flow health, and customer relationship management. Core Components Credit Limits
Set thresholds for customer credit exposure based on risk profiles, historical payment behavior, and financial analysis.  Credit Holds
Automatically or manually block orders that exceed credit limits or violate credit policies.  Interest Notes
Apply interest charges to overdue balances based on predefined rules and rates.  Collection Letters
Generate structured communications to remind customers of overdue payments, escalating in tone and urgency.  Fees and Penalties
Define and apply late payment fees, reinstatement charges, and other penalties as part of the collections process.   Process Flow Define Credit Policies
Establish rules for credit limits, approval workflows, and risk thresholds.  Configure Collections Strategy
Set up interest calculation methods, collection letter sequences, and fee structures.  Automate Monitoring
Use aging snapshots, customer pools, and collection agents to track overdue accounts.  Execute Collections Activities
Generate letters, apply interest, and escalate cases as needed.  Review and Refine
Monitor effectiveness and adjust policies based on performance and business needs.   Stakeholders Credit Manager
Oversees credit limit policies and approval workflows.  Collections Coordinator
Manages overdue accounts, sends collection letters, and tracks customer responses.  Finance Controller
Ensures compliance with financial policies and oversees interest and fee application.  Accounts Receivable Manager
Reconciles payments, applies holds, and manages customer account status.   Dynamics 365 Support Dynamics 365 Finance
Supports credit limit configuration, credit holds, interest notes, and collection letter automation.  Collections Workspace
Provides centralized views of aging balances, payment history, and remaining credit.  AI-Driven Features
Automates email generation and prioritizes collection actions using customer data.  Case Management
Tracks collection activities and escalations using case categories and approval workflows.

l3Processes.title: Identify production resources
l3Processes.description:
This process focuses on determining and managing the people, machines,
materials, and subcontractors required for production

l3Processes.title: Sell product subscriptions
l3Processes.description:
The Sell Product Subscriptions business process defines how organizations offer and manage recurring product sales, including both traditional subscriptions (tangible or intangible) and continuity orders. This process is part of the broader Order to Cash cycle and supports predictable revenue, customer retention, and operational efficiency. Core Components Traditional Subscriptions
Includes recurring sales of tangible goods (e.g., magazines, consumables) and intangible services (e.g., software, memberships). Managed through subscription contracts with defined billing cycles and renewal terms.  Continuity Orders
Automatically generated orders based on a predefined schedule (e.g., monthly shipments of household items). These are integrated into digital storefronts and managed via Intelligent Order Management.  Subscription Frequency
Defined at the product level (e.g., monthly, quarterly, yearly). Determines how often fulfillment jobs are triggered [3].  Contract Management
Subscription contracts include start/end dates, billing terms, and fulfillment rules. Only one product per subscription is currently supported, with multi-product support planned.   Process Flow Identify Customer and Services
Capture customer details and select subscription offerings.  Negotiate Terms
Define pricing, frequency, and renewal policies.  Confirm Order
Validate subscription setup and initiate billing schedule.  Revise Order
Adjust terms or frequency based on customer feedback or operational needs.  Fulfill Subscription
Use Intelligent Order Management to orchestrate deliveries across warehouses and carriers.   Stakeholders Subscription Product Manager
Designs offerings, pricing models, and renewal policies.  Billing Specialist
Manages invoicing, billing schedules, and revenue recognition.  E-commerce Manager
Integrates continuity orders into online sales flows.  Order Fulfillment Lead
Oversees logistics and delivery orchestration.  Finance Controller
Ensures compliance with accounting standards for recurring revenue.   Dynamics 365 Support Dynamics 365 Finance
Supports subscription billing setup, revenue recognition, and deferrals.  Dynamics 365 Commerce
Manages continuity orders and integrates subscription flows into e-commerce platforms.  Dynamics 365 Intelligent Order Management
Automates subscription fulfillment and order orchestration.  Dynamics 365 Business Central
Provides financial and operational support for subscription contracts.

l3Processes.title: Enter sales orders
l3Processes.description: The Enter Sales Orders business process defines how organizations capture and manage sales orders across various channels and scenarios. It supports both traditional and modern sales models and is a core part of the Order to Cash cycle. Supported Sales Channels Manual Entry: Sales orders entered directly by back-office staff using Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management or Business Central. Point of Sale (POS): Store associates enter orders at the register using Dynamics 365 Commerce. Call Centers: Assisted sales and remote order entry. eCommerce (B2B/B2C): Online orders captured automatically via integrated platforms. Quotes to Orders: Sales professionals convert quotes into orders using Dynamics 365 Sales. Agreements to Orders: Orders generated from contractual agreements or recurring commitments.  Supported Sales Types Products: Tangible goods across retail, wholesale, and manufacturing. Services: Consulting, subscriptions, maintenance, and leasing. Gift Cards: Issued and redeemed through POS or eCommerce. Royalty-Based Items: Sales that trigger royalty payouts, common in media and publishing.  Advanced Scenarios Intercompany Trade: Automatically generated orders between legal entities. Brokered Sales: Orders facilitated by third-party brokers with custom integrations. Dual Write: Real-time synchronization between Dynamics 365 apps and Dataverse. EDI Orders: Electronic Data Interchange for automated B2B order processing.  Process Flow Initiate Order: Capture customer intent via POS, eCommerce, call center, or manual entry. Validate Terms: Apply pricing, discounts, and contract terms. Confirm Order: Generate and confirm the sales order in the system. Sync Systems: Use Dual Write or EDI to ensure data consistency across platforms. Monitor & Adjust: Track order status, apply changes, and manage exceptions.  Stakeholders Sales Associate: Enters orders via POS or call center. Sales Manager: Oversees quote conversion and agreement-based orders. Order Fulfillment Lead: Coordinates delivery and logistics. Finance Controller: Ensures compliance with revenue recognition and royalty tracking. IT Integration Lead: Manages Dual Write, EDI, and broker integrations.  Dynamics 365 Support Dynamics 365 Commerce: POS, gift cards, eCommerce, loyalty. Dynamics 365 Sales: Quote-to-order conversion. Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management: Manual entry, intercompany, royalty tracking. Dynamics 365 Business Central: Back-office order management. Dynamics 365 Intelligent Order Management: Orchestration and fulfillment. Dual Write & EDI: Real-time sync and automated B2B processing.

l3Processes.title: Manage blanket sales orders
l3Processes.description:
This business process defines how organizations manage long-term sales commitments with customers using blanket sales orders, also referred to as sales agreements in Dynamics 365. These agreements allow customers to commit to purchasing large quantities of goods or services over time, with predefined pricing, quantities, and delivery schedules. Core Concepts Sales Agreements: Framework contracts that define terms for recurring sales over a period. They include pricing, quantity commitments, and delivery schedules. Release Orders: Individual sales orders created from the original agreement to fulfill specific shipments. These do not affect item availability until released. Types of Agreements:Quantity-based: Customer commits to purchasing a specific quantity. Value-based: Agreement is based on a total monetary value. Schedule-based: Delivery dates are predefined across multiple shipments.    Process Flow Negotiate Contract
Define pricing, quantity, and delivery terms with the customer.  Confirm Contract
Finalize and approve the agreement in the system.  Create Release Orders
Generate individual sales orders from the agreement as needed.  Monitor Contract Performance
Track fulfillment progress, remaining quantities, and revenue recognition.  Revise Contract
Adjust terms or quantities based on customer needs or business changes.  Complete Fulfillment
Close the agreement once all commitments are met.   Stakeholders Sales Agreement Manager
Negotiates and maintains long-term agreements.  Order Fulfillment Coordinator
Manages release orders and ensures timely delivery.  Finance Analyst
Monitors revenue recognition and pricing compliance.  Customer Service Representative
Handles customer inquiries and updates orders.   Dynamics 365 Support Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management
Enables creation and tracking of sales agreements and release orders.  Dynamics 365 Business Central
Supports blanket sales orders with flexible scheduling and conversion to sales orders.  Integration Features
APIs and EDI support allow automated creation and fulfillment of release orders. Agreements can be configured for two-way or three-way match policies.

l3Processes.title: Manage sales order changes
l3Processes.description:
This business process defines how organizations manage updates to sales orders across all channels and order types. It excludes cancellation scenarios, which are covered separately. Scope Sales order changes may include: Quantity adjustments Delivery date changes Shipping address updates Pricing modifications  These changes apply to: Standard sales orders Call center orders eCommerce orders POS orders Intercompany orders Drop shipment orders  Process Flow Initiate Change Request
Triggered by customer service, sales, or fulfillment teams based on customer input or operational needs.  Validate Change
Ensure changes comply with pricing policies, inventory availability, and contractual terms.  Apply Changes
Update the sales order in Dynamics 365. Changes are tracked with audit trails and version history.  Synchronize Related Documents
For intercompany and drop shipment scenarios, changes to the sales order automatically update linked purchase orders.  Notify Stakeholders
Communicate changes to fulfillment, finance, and customer service teams to ensure alignment.   Stakeholders Sales Operations Analyst
Manages updates and ensures changes are communicated across teams.  Order Management Specialist
Applies changes and monitors downstream impacts.  Finance Analyst
Validates pricing and revenue implications.  Customer Service Representative
Coordinates with customers and confirms changes.   Dynamics 365 Support Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management
Supports direct editing of sales orders with audit trails and change tracking.  Dynamics 365 Commerce
Enables call center agents to modify orders placed through assisted channels.  Dynamics 365 Business Central
Allows flexible editing including partial shipments and backorders.  Intercompany & Drop Shipment Sync
Automatically updates related documents across legal entities and vendors.

l3Processes.title: Manage sales order holds
l3Processes.description:
The Manage Sales Order Holds business process defines how organizations temporarily pause order processing across various sales channels due to issues such as payment verification, credit checks, fraud detection, or manual review. This process is part of the broader Order to Cash cycle and ensures operational control and risk mitigation. Core Hold Types Standard Order Holds
Applied manually or automatically based on business rules, such as missing information or pending approvals.  Call Center Holds
Used to pause orders initiated through assisted channels for customer verification or issue resolution.  POS Holds (Suspended Sales)
Transactions can be suspended in the Point of Sale (POS) system to manage customer flow or resolve discrepancies before finalizing the sale.  Fraud Holds
Triggered by fraud detection systems or manual review when suspicious activity is identified.  Check Holds
Applied when payment by check requires verification or clearance before order fulfillment.   Process Flow Identify Order for Hold
Triggered by system rules, manual review, or customer interaction.  Place Order on Hold
Sales order status updated to reflect hold type and reason.  Review Hold Status
Stakeholders assess whether the hold can be cleared.  Clear Hold or Escalate
If approved, the order proceeds; otherwise, escalated for further review.  Update Order Status
Order resumes processing or is flagged for additional action.   Stakeholders Order Management Specialist
Applies and releases holds based on business rules and customer interactions.  Call Center Supervisor
Manages holds on assisted orders and ensures timely resolution.  POS Associate
Suspends and resumes transactions in-store to maintain accuracy and flow.  Fraud Risk Analyst
Reviews fraud holds and determines next steps.  Finance Controller
Oversees check holds and ensures compliance with financial policies.   Dynamics 365 Support Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management
Enables configuration and automation of order holds across standard and intercompany scenarios.  Dynamics 365 Commerce
Supports POS transaction suspension and fraud detection workflows.  Dynamics 365 Business Central
Provides financial controls for check holds and manual order reviews.

l3Processes.title: Manage store operations
l3Processes.description:
The Manage Store Operations business process defines how brick-and-mortar retail stores handle daily operational tasks using Dynamics 365 Commerce. It supports store associates and managers in executing financial transactions, customer interactions, and operational procedures through POS and back-office tools. Core Components Start-of-Day Operations
Open shift, count starting cash, add float to drawer. End-of-Day Operations
Close shift, reconcile cash, generate reports for review and approval. Cash Management  Cash Drops: Reduce drawer cash during the day.  Bank Drops: Deposit funds at end of day.  Add Cash to Drawer: Replenish drawer as needed.  Record Non-Sales Cash Movements: Track petty cash reimbursements, donations, etc.  Customer Pickup
Fulfill online or cross-channel orders in-store.
Verify order and customer identity via POS.
Complete pickup transaction. Price Adjustments
Modify pricing at POS for promotions, corrections, or overrides. Safe Drops
Securely transfer excess cash from drawer to store safe. Process Flow Open Store: Start shift, prepare cash drawer.  Operate Store: Handle transactions, pickups, price changes, and cash movements.  Close Store: End shift, reconcile cash, submit reports.  Stakeholders Store Associate: Executes POS transactions, pickups, and cash handling.  Store Manager: Oversees operations, approves reports, manages safe/bank drops.  Finance Analyst: Reviews reports, reconciles cash, ensures compliance.  Dynamics 365 Support Dynamics 365 Commerce: POS interface for transactions, pickups, and cash operations.  Back-office tools for reporting and reconciliation.  Omnichannel Fulfillment: Supports BOPIS (Buy Online, Pick Up In Store) and cross-channel order management.

l3Processes.title: Process customer returns and exchanges
l3Processes.description:
This business process defines how organizations manage customer returns, exchanges, and related service scenarios across all sales channels. It supports both customer satisfaction and regulatory compliance, and is a critical part of the Order to Cash cycle. Core Scenarios Covered Returns
Customers may return items through POS, eCommerce, or call center channels. Returns can be full or partial, and may involve restocking, scrapping, or refurbishing.  Exchanges
Items can be exchanged for identical or alternative products. Exchanges may be processed immediately or scheduled for later fulfillment.  Credits and Refunds
Refunds may be issued as store credit, gift cards, or direct payment. Price matching adjustments are also supported. Dynamics 365 enforces business rules and approval workflows for exceptions.  Repair and Service
Items not returned may be serviced or repaired. Service orders or technician dispatches can be initiated to resolve issues without requiring a return.  Quality Management and Inspection
Returned items undergo inspection to determine disposition. Dynamics 365 supports quarantine orders, inspection workflows, and disposition decisions to maintain inventory accuracy and product quality.  Product Recalls and Tracing
Recalled items are tracked and processed through specialized workflows. Traceability ensures compliance with safety and regulatory standards.   Process Flow Initiate Return or Exchange
Triggered by customer request via any channel.  Validate Request
Check eligibility, warranty, and return policies.  Process Transaction
Issue refund, credit, or exchange order.  Inspect Returned Item
Perform quality checks and determine disposition.  Service or Repair (if applicable)
Create service orders or dispatch technicians.  Trace and Recall (if applicable)
Execute recall workflows and trace affected inventory.   Stakeholders Customer Service Representative
Initiates returns, exchanges, and service requests.  Store Associate
Handles in-store returns and restocking.  Warehouse Manager
Manages returned inventory and inspections.  Quality Control Analyst
Inspects items and determines disposition.   Dynamics 365 Support Commerce
Manages POS and eCommerce returns, exchanges, and refunds.  Supply Chain Management
Supports inspection, quarantine, and disposition workflows.  Field Service
Enables repair orders and technician dispatches.  Finance
Handles credit issuance, refund processing, and approval workflows.

l3Processes.title: Cancel sales orders
l3Processes.description: This business process defines how organizations cancel sales orders across all channels and order types using Dynamics 365. It supports full and partial cancellations and includes voiding transactions at the Point of Sale (POS). The process ensures accurate inventory, financial reconciliation, and customer satisfaction. Supported Channels and Order Types POS (Point of Sale): Voiding sales directly at the register. eCommerce: Online cancellations initiated by customers or agents. Call Center: Assisted cancellations via customer service. Back Office: Manual cancellations by operations or finance teams. Omnichannel: Orders placed in one channel and canceled in another.  Cancellation Scenarios Full Cancellation: Entire order is voided before fulfillment. Partial Cancellation: Specific lines or quantities are canceled while the rest of the order proceeds. Automated Cancellation: Triggered by payment failures, fraud detection, or inventory shortages. Policy-Based Cancellation: Enforced through business rules such as time limits, restocking fees, or approval workflows.  Process Flow Initiate Cancellation: Triggered manually or automatically based on business logic. Validate Policy: Check against configured rules for eligibility and fees. Apply Cancellation: Update order status, reverse inventory transactions if needed. Reconcile Financials: Adjust revenue, taxes, and payment records. Notify Stakeholders: Inform fulfillment, finance, and customer service teams.  Stakeholders Sales Associate: Initiates cancellations at POS or call center. Order Management Specialist: Manages cancellations across systems. Finance Analyst: Ensures proper financial adjustments. Customer Service Representative: Communicates with customers and confirms cancellations.  Dynamics 365 Support Commerce: POS voids, eCommerce cancellations, omnichannel order tracking. Supply Chain Management: Inventory reversal, partial cancellation handling. Business Central: Back-office cancellation workflows and financial reconciliation.

l3Processes.title: Allocate inventory to sales orders
l3Processes.description:
This business process defines how organizations assign available inventory to sales orders using manual or automatic methods. In Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management, this is referred to as reservations or marking. The goal is to ensure accurate fulfillment, prioritize customer needs, and maintain inventory integrity across channels. Core Concepts Reservation
Inventory is reserved for specific sales orders either manually by users or automatically based on system rules. Reservations ensure that stock is held for committed orders and not consumed by others.  Marking
A method to link supply and demand directly. Marking ties inbound inventory (e.g., purchase orders) to outbound demand (e.g., sales orders), ensuring that specific stock is allocated to specific orders. This is especially useful in cross-docking scenarios.  Manual vs. Automatic Allocation  Manual: Users select inventory to reserve or mark for orders. Automatic: System applies rules to allocate inventory based on availability, priority, and delivery timelines.    Process Flow Identify Inventory
Determine available stock and inbound supply.  Determine Reservation Method
Choose between manual or automatic reservation.  Apply Reservation or Marking
Reserve inventory or mark supply to demand.  Confirm Reservation Records
Validate that inventory is correctly allocated.  Update Inventory Requirements
Adjust planning and fulfillment based on allocations.  Complete Allocation Process
Finalize reservations and proceed with order fulfillment.   Stakeholders Inventory Manager
Oversees inventory availability and ensures accurate allocation.  Sales Operations Analyst
Manages order prioritization and business rule compliance.  Procurement Specialist
Coordinates inbound supply to support allocation needs.  Warehouse Associate
Executes picking and packing based on reserved inventory.   Dynamics 365 Support Supply Chain Management
Supports reservations, marking, and inventory visibility.  Business Central
Enables manual and automated inventory allocation.  Intelligent Order Management
Enhances allocation logic with visibility add-ins and prioritization rules.

l3Processes.title: Issue sales invoices
l3Processes.description:
This business process defines how organizations issue sales invoices across various scenarios using Dynamics 365 Finance, Business Central, and Project Operations. It supports accurate billing, revenue recognition, and customer account management. Supported Invoice Types Sales Order Invoices
Generated from confirmed shipments, reflecting actual goods delivered. These invoices include item details, pricing, taxes, and shipping charges. They can be created manually or in bulk via batch jobs [1].  Free Text Invoices
Used for billing services or miscellaneous charges not tied to a sales order. These can be one-time or recurring (e.g., subscriptions, retainers). Templates and default values streamline creation and ensure consistency [1] [2].  Project Invoices
Created from time, expense, and fee transactions recorded against a project. Billing rules define how and when invoices are generated—such as time and materials, fixed price, or milestone-based billing. These support detailed breakdowns and customer-specific formats.  Service Invoices
Apply to scenarios where services are sold independently or alongside products. These may originate from service orders or manual entry and are integrated with service management workflows [1].   Integration Scenarios Invoices can be: Posted, printed, or emailed directly from Dynamics 365. Synchronized with external systems for further processing. Managed using extensible connectors for electronic invoicing and regulatory compliance.  Process Flow Invoice Source Identification
Determine whether the invoice is based on a sales order, project, service, or free text.  Invoice Creation
Use templates, billing rules, or manual entry to generate the invoice.  Validation and Posting
Apply tax rules, verify pricing, and post the invoice to the ledger.  Distribution and Integration
Send invoices to customers via email or integrate with external systems for processing.  Monitoring and Adjustments
Track invoice status, apply corrections, and manage disputes or adjustments.   Stakeholders Accounts Receivable Specialist
Manages invoice creation, posting, and customer communication.  Project Manager
Oversees project-based billing and ensures alignment with contract terms.  Finance Controller
Ensures compliance with revenue recognition and financial reporting standards.  Service Coordinator
Handles service-related invoicing and supports customer resolution.   Dynamics 365 Support Finance & Business Central
Core invoicing capabilities including free text and sales order invoices.  Project Operations
Project-based billing with configurable rules and formats.  Electronic Invoicing Service
Supports integration with external systems and regulatory compliance.

l3Processes.title: Issue customer credits
l3Processes.description:
The Issue Customer Credits process defines how organizations manage financial adjustments for customers across various sales channels. It supports accurate financial reconciliation, customer satisfaction, and operational efficiency. Core Scenarios Sales Order Credits
Issued when goods are returned or orders are canceled after invoicing. Credits are created from the original invoice or through a return order process. Inventory and financial postings are automatically adjusted.  Free Text Invoice Credits
Used to correct or reverse charges not tied to a sales order. These can be manually created or copied from the original invoice with negative amounts. Recurring invoices can be adjusted using credit notes.  Project-Based Credits
Generated from time, expense, or fee transactions that require reversal. In Dynamics 365 Project Operations, credit notes are linked to billing rules and specific milestones.  Service-Related Credits
Applied when services are not delivered as expected or customer dissatisfaction arises. These credits ensure service quality and customer retention.  POS Credits and Price Matching
In Dynamics 365 Commerce POS, credit memos can be issued directly from return transactions. Price matching adjustments are supported through credit memo operations. Partial redemptions automatically generate new credit memos for remaining balances.   Process Flow Initiate Credit
Triggered by return, cancellation, service issue, or pricing discrepancy.  Validate Eligibility
Confirm policy compliance and customer entitlement.  Create Credit Memo
Generate credit from invoice, free text, or manually.  Apply Credit
Link to customer account, adjust balances, and optionally issue refunds.  Track and Reconcile
Monitor credit usage, partial redemptions, and financial impact.   Stakeholders Accounts Receivable Specialist
Manages credit issuance and reconciliation.  Customer Service Representative
Initiates credits and communicates with customers.  Finance Controller
Ensures compliance with financial policies and reporting.  POS Associate
Issues credit memos and handles price matching at the register.   Dynamics 365 Support Finance & Business Central
Core credit memo creation and posting.  Commerce POS
Credit memo issuance, redemption, and price matching.  Project Operations
Project-based credit note generation.

l3Processes.title: Bill subscriptions
l3Processes.description:
The Bill Subscriptions business process defines how organizations manage recurring billing for subscription-based products and services. It supports both traditional subscription billing and continuity billing models, enabling predictable revenue and streamlined operations. Core Scenarios Traditional Subscription Billing
Recurring charges for services or digital products (e.g., software, memberships) billed monthly, quarterly, or annually.  Managed via billing schedules and contract terms  Supports proration, usage-based billing, and multi-element arrangements  Revenue recognition aligned with ASC 606 and IFRS 15 standards  Continuity Billing
Recurring shipments of physical goods (e.g., consumables, curated boxes) fulfilled through continuity orders.  Automatically generated based on customer preferences  Integrated with fulfillment and inventory systems  Billing Concepts Frequency: Monthly, quarterly, annually, or custom intervals  Pricing: Standard trade agreements, tiered pricing, escalations  Item Types: Standard (fixed quantity), usage-based (variable), milestone-based (event-driven)  Renewals: Automatic contract renewals supported  Revenue Recognition Revenue can be deferred and recognized over time  Allocation rules and schedules ensure compliance  Integrated with general ledger for accurate financial reporting  Integration Subscription billing integrates with CRM, eCommerce, and customer service platforms  Supports external invoicing systems and electronic billing workflows  Stakeholders Billing Specialist: Manages schedules, invoicing, and renewals  Finance Controller: Oversees revenue recognition and compliance  Subscription Product Manager: Designs offerings and pricing models  Customer Service Representative: Handles billing inquiries and adjustments  Dynamics 365 Support Finance: Subscription Billing module for complex billing scenarios  Business Central: Recurring sales lines and invoice templates  Commerce: Continuity orders and omnichannel fulfillment  Project Operations: Subscription billing with deferrals and project integration

l3Processes.title: Recognize revenue
l3Processes.description: The Recognize Revenue business process defines how organizations account for earned revenue across standard sales and project-based scenarios. It ensures compliance with accounting standards such as ASC 606 and IFRS 15, supports financial transparency, and aligns revenue recognition with performance obligations. Core Scenarios Standard Sales Revenue Recognition Revenue is recognized when control of goods or services transfers to the customer. Recognition is based on fulfillment dates and performance obligations. Supports deferred revenue schedules and unbilled accounts receivable (A/R) for accrual-based accounting.  Project Revenue Recognition Managed through Dynamics 365 Project Operations. Time and Materials: Revenue recognized as billable hours and expenses are incurred. Fixed Price: Revenue recognized via milestones, percentage of completion, or straight-line schedules. Cost-Plus: Revenue recognized based on actual costs plus markup. Recognition can occur independently of billing, supporting accrual accounting.  Revenue Recognition Features Deferrals: Postpone revenue to match earned periods. Recognitions: Triggered when performance obligations are met. Audit Trails: Track adjustments, reversals, and recognition history. Integration: Syncs with external systems and general ledger for financial reporting.  Process Flow Identify Revenue Source: Sales order, project billing, subscription. Apply Recognition Rules: Based on contract terms, fulfillment, or billing milestones. Post Revenue Entries: Update financial records and general ledger. Adjust and Reconcile: Handle reversals, corrections, and audit compliance.  Stakeholders Finance Controller: Oversees recognition policies and compliance. Project Accountant: Manages project-based revenue alignment. Billing Specialist: Ensures correct invoicing and revenue schedules. IT Integration Lead: Supports data integration with reporting tools.  Dynamics 365 Support Finance & Business Central: Core revenue recognition and deferral capabilities. Project Operations: Project-based billing and milestone tracking. Electronic Invoicing: Integration with external systems for automated processing.

l3Processes.title: Process customer payments
l3Processes.description: The Process Customer Payments business process defines how organizations record and manage payments received from customers across various channels and payment types. It supports both manual and automated workflows and integrates with external systems to ensure accurate financial reconciliation and reporting. Supported Payment Types Cash: Accepted at POS or manually recorded in journals. Check: Entered manually or imported via lockbox. Credit/Debit Cards: Captured through integrated payment processors or POS terminals. Gift Cards: Redeemed at POS or online. Loyalty Cards/Points: Applied as payment at POS or eCommerce checkout. Bank Transfers: Recorded manually or imported from external systems.  Payment Entry Methods Manual Journal Entry: Payments recorded in the customer payment journal or cash receipt journal, typically for checks, cash, or bank transfers. Sales Order Payment Entry: Payments applied directly to open sales orders, especially in retail or service scenarios. POS and In-Person Payments: Captured at the point of sale using cash, card, gift cards, or loyalty points. Online Payments: Processed through integrated eCommerce platforms or payment gateways. Call Center Payments: Entered by agents using assisted channels. Lockbox Import: Payments received by a bank lockbox are imported and automatically matched to open invoices using remittance data. Integrated Payments: Imported from external systems such as CRM, service platforms, or third-party processors using APIs or data entities. Check Scanning: Checks scanned and processed automatically using OCR and bank integration. Service Order Payments: Payments collected for service-related transactions, either in advance or at completion.  Process Flow Receive Payment: Triggered by customer action or external system. Validate Payment: Confirm payment method, amount, and remittance data. Record Payment: Enter manually or import via integration. Apply Payment: Match to open invoices or hold as unapplied balance. Reconcile and Report: Update financial records and generate reports.  Stakeholders Accounts Receivable Specialist: Manages payment entry, application, and reconciliation. POS Associate: Captures in-person payments and handles loyalty/gift card redemptions. Finance Controller: Oversees compliance, reporting, and reconciliation. IT Integration Lead: Supports payment gateway and lockbox integrations.  Dynamics 365 Support Finance & Business Central: Core payment processing, journal entry, and reconciliation. Commerce: POS and eCommerce payment capture, loyalty integration. External Integrations: APIs and data entities for lockbox, CRM, and payment gateway connectivity.

l3Processes.title: Process customer prepayments
l3Processes.description:
The business process defines how organizations record, apply, and manage customer prepayments—advance payments made before goods or services are delivered. This process is distinct from standard customer payments, which are typically applied after invoicing. Prepayments are common in industries requiring deposits, down payments, or full advance payments prior to fulfillment. Supported Scenarios Sales Order Prepayments
A prepayment invoice is generated and posted before the order is fulfilled. The payment is applied to the sales order and deducted from the final invoice.  Manual Journal Entry
Prepayments can be recorded in a customer payment journal and held as an unapplied balance until matched to an invoice.  POS and eCommerce
In Dynamics 365 Commerce, prepayments are collected at the time of order placement for items not immediately fulfilled (e.g., special orders or pre-orders).  Project Prepayments
In project-based scenarios, prepayments are recorded against a project contract and applied to future billing milestones.   Entry Channels Point of Sale (POS) Online checkout Call center-assisted entry Manual entry via journals Integration with external systems (e.g., lockbox, check scanning)  Dynamics 365 Support Finance & Business Central
Supports prepayment invoicing, journal entry, and application to orders or projects.  Commerce
Enables prepayment capture at POS and online checkout for special or high-value orders.  Project Operations
Allows prepayments to be linked to project contracts and applied to milestone billing.

l3Processes.title: Process customer refunds
l3Processes.description:
The Process Customer Refunds business process defines how organizations manage and execute refunds across various channels and payment types. It ensures financial accuracy, customer satisfaction, and compliance with refund policies. Supported Refund Scenarios Sales Order Refunds
Refunds issued for returned or canceled orders, typically tied to original invoices or return orders.  Free Text Invoice Refunds
Refunds for miscellaneous charges not linked to a sales order, often processed manually.  Service Order Refunds
Refunds for undelivered or unsatisfactory services, including partial or full service cancellations.  POS Refunds
In-person refunds processed at the point of sale, including cash, card, gift card, and loyalty point redemptions.  Online and Call Center Refunds
Refunds initiated through eCommerce platforms or customer service representatives.  Journal Entry Refunds
Manual refunds recorded in the customer payment journal or cash receipt journal.  Lockbox and Check Scanning Refunds
Refunds processed through bank lockbox imports or scanned checks using OCR and integrated systems.   Process Flow Receive Refund Request
Initiated by customer service, POS associate, or automated system.  Validate Request
Confirm eligibility based on return policies, payment method, and transaction history.  Process Refund
Create refund transaction, apply to customer account, or issue payment.  Post Refund
Update financial records and reconcile accounts.  Confirm and Revise (if needed)
Review refund accuracy and make adjustments if necessary.   Stakeholders Accounts Receivable Specialist
Processes refund transactions, reconciles accounts, and ensures documentation.  Customer Service Representative
Initiates refunds based on customer interactions and return approvals.  Store Manager
Approves in-person refunds and ensures compliance with store policies.  Finance Controller
Oversees refund policies, financial controls, and audit compliance.  IT Integration Lead
Manages refund processing through integrated payment gateways and external systems.   Dynamics 365 Support Finance & Business Central
Core refund processing, journal entry, and reconciliation.  Commerce
POS and eCommerce refund capture, loyalty integration.  External Integrations
APIs and data entities for lockbox, CRM, and payment gateway connectivity.

l3Processes.title: Settle customer transactions
l3Processes.description: This business process defines how organizations match payments, credit memos, and other financial entries to open invoices or debit transactions. Settlement ensures accurate customer account balances and supports financial reconciliation. Dynamics 365 Finance and Business Central support both automatic and manual settlement methods, as well as netting between customer and vendor accounts. Core Settlement Methods Automatic Settlement  Payments are matched to open invoices based on predefined rules.  Rules can prioritize oldest invoices, specific invoice types, or currency logic.  Ideal for high-volume environments or when importing payments from lockboxes or integrated systems.  Manual Settlement  Used when automatic matching is not possible or when user control is required.  Supports partial payments, overpayments, underpayments, and customer-specific instructions.  Users manually select open transactions in the settlement form or during journal entry.  Open Transaction Editing  Allows corrections after posting.  Users can unmark and reapply settlements to adjust how payments are matched.  Netting (Customer and Vendor Balances) Netting Overview  Netting offsets balances between customers and vendors when they are the same party.  Minimizes unnecessary payments and transaction fees.  Netting Agreement  Defines customer-vendor pairs and posting parameters.  Can be created manually or via data entities.  Includes journal name, netting account, and effective date range.  Manual Netting  Users select open invoices for both customer and vendor.  System calculates the minimal balance and posts a netting journal to settle both sides.  Automatic Netting  Triggered via batch jobs or process automation.  Uses netting rules to automate selection and posting.  Process Flow Identify Transactions – Open invoices, payments, credit memos. Apply Settlement Method – Automatic, manual, or netting. Post Settlement – Update ledger and subledger balances. Reconcile and Report – Ensure financial accuracy and audit trail.  Stakeholders Accounts Receivable Specialist – Manages settlement entries and reconciliations. Finance Controller – Oversees compliance and financial reporting. Cash Clerk – Executes netting and journal postings. IT Integration Lead – Supports lockbox, check scanning, and external system integrations.  Dynamics 365 Support Finance & Business Central – Core settlement, journal entry, and reconciliation. Netting Module – Customer/vendor balance netting, agreement setup, and automation. Process Automation Framework – Supports automatic netting and batch processing.

l3Processes.description: This business process defines how organizations match payments, credit memos, and other financial entries to open invoices or debit transactions. Settlement ensures accurate customer account balances and supports financial reconciliation. Dynamics 365 Support Finance & Business Central – Core settlement, journal entry, and reconciliation. Netting Module – Customer/vendor balance netting, agreement setup, and automation.
