Share selected suppliers with sourcing and contract teams   Dynamics 365 Support Supplier collaboration portal: Bid submission, questionnaires, document uploads  Vendor information management: Stores evaluation results, scorecards, and rationale  Workflow automation: Routes evaluations, approvals, and notifications  Purchasing policies: Enforce sourcing rules and vendor eligibility  Analytics (Power BI): Visualize bid comparisons, scoring trends, and sourcing outcomes  Deliverables Evaluation templates and scorecards  Site visit reports and stakeholder feedback forms  Bid comparison dashboards  Approved vendor list with rationale  Documentation of sourcing decisions and audit trail

l3Processes.title: Manage procurement spend
l3Processes.parentL2: Source and contract goods and services
l3Processes.description: Manages procurement spend across categories, business units, and suppliers. Includes monitoring budget adherence, handling overages, enforcing signing limits, and triggering workflow escalations. Supports proactive spend governance, policy enforcement, and financial accountability. Scope & Outcomes Tracks actual vs. forecasted spend Flags budget overages and policy violations Enforces signing limits and approval hierarchies Escalates spend exceptions via workflows Provides visibility into spend by category, supplier, region, and cost center  Outcomes include: Controlled procurement spend Reduced maverick buying Improved budget compliance Spend dashboards and audit trails  Spend Management Model Spend Dimensions Category: goods/services classification Supplier: strategic vs. tail vendors Region: geography-based spend tracking Business Unit / Cost Center: internal allocation Purchase Type: PO, non-PO, contract-based  Controls Budget thresholds Signing limits by role Escalation workflows Policy flags (e.g., preferred vendor enforcement)  Exceptions Budget overages Unauthorized purchases Non-compliant suppliers Emergency buys  Operating Model Portal-driven: Requisitioners submit purchase requests via supplier portal or ERP Automated workflows: Escalate based on spend thresholds, signing limits, or policy violations Manual oversight: Finance and procurement review flagged transactions  High-Level Process Define Spend Policies Set signing limits, budget thresholds, preferred vendor rules   Configure Spend Controls Enable approval workflows, policy flags, budget checks   Monitor Spend Activity Track actual vs. forecasted spend across dimensions   Flag Exceptions Identify overages, unauthorized purchases, non-compliant vendors   Escalate for Review Route flagged transactions to approvers based on rules   Approve or Reject Approvers validate, reject, or reassign based on policy   Report & Analyze Generate dashboards for spend trends, violations, savings   Audit & Improve Review spend patterns, refine thresholds, update policies    Roles & Responsibilities Procurement: Owns spend policies, reviews exceptions Finance/AP: Validates budget adherence, signing limits Business Stakeholders: Provide input on spend needs, exceptions Category Managers: Monitor category-level spend, enforce sourcing rules  Dynamics 365 Support Vendor Information Management: Links spend to supplier profiles Purchasing Policies: Enforce spend controls, preferred vendor usage Workflow Automation: Escalation routing, approvals Analytics (Power BI): Spend dashboards, exception tracking Budget Integration: Forecast vs. actual spend comparison  Deliverables Spend policy matrix Configured approval workflows Exception handling rules Spend dashboards by category, supplier, region Audit logs of approvals, escalations, violations

l3Processes.title: Contract suppliers for goods
l3Processes.parentL2: Source and contract goods and services
l3Processes.description: Formalizes agreements with suppliers for the provision of tangible goods. Contracts define pricing, delivery terms, quality standards, and compliance requirements. Supports structured purchase agreements and automated workflows in Dynamics 365 SCM, with document intelligence via Microsoft Syntex. Scope & Outcomes  Establishes binding contracts for physical goods  Defines item-level pricing, delivery schedules, and quality expectations  Enables automated PO generation and trade agreement enforcement  Outcomes include searchable contracts, validated terms, and supplier performance linkage  Contracting Model  Contract Types: Purchase agreements, master supply agreements  Attributes: Item codes, quantities, pricing tiers, delivery terms, quality clauses  Syntex Capabilities: AI-based metadata extraction, clause tagging, compliance classification  SCM Features: Trade agreement setup, approved vendor lists, PO automation  Operating Model  Portal-driven contract intake and updates  Syntex automates classification, tagging, and metadata enrichment  SCM binds contracts to vendor records and enforces terms during purchasing  High-Level Process  Define contract scope and item specifications  Draft using templates and clause libraries  Review and approve via procurement/legal workflows  Upload to SharePoint with Syntex tagging  Link to vendor record and trade agreement in SCM  Monitor contract usage and performance  Renew or amend as needed  Dynamics 365 Support  Vendor Information Management  Purchase Agreements in SCM  Microsoft Syntex for contract processing  Power BI dashboards for contract usage and supplier performance

l3Processes.title: Contract suppliers for services
l3Processes.parentL2: Source and contract goods and services
l3Processes.description: Establishes contracts with service providers for consulting, maintenance, logistics, or other non-tangible deliverables. Contracts focus on scope of work, milestones, SLAs, and billing terms. Uses Microsoft Syntex for document intelligence and SCM for service contract tracking. Scope & Outcomes  Formalizes service delivery expectations and payment terms  Captures SLAs, resource commitments, and compliance clauses  Supports milestone-based billing and performance monitoring  Outcomes include searchable service contracts, validated SLAs, and audit trails  Contracting Model  Contract Types: Statements of Work (SOW), service agreements, retainer contracts  Attributes: Scope of work, timelines, SLAs, billing schedules, resource profiles  Syntex Capabilities: Clause extraction, SLA tagging, compliance classification  SCM Features: Service contract tracking, milestone billing, vendor performance linkage  Operating Model  Portal-driven contract submission and updates  Syntex enriches metadata and tags SLA clauses  SCM tracks service delivery and billing milestones  High-Level Process  Define service scope and deliverables  Draft contract with SLA and billing terms  Review and approve via legal/procurement workflows  Upload to SharePoint with Syntex tagging  Link to vendor record and service tracking in SCM  Monitor service delivery and milestone completion  Renew or amend based on performance  Dynamics 365 Support  Vendor Information Management  Service contract tracking in SCM  Microsoft Syntex for SLA tagging and metadata enrichment  Power BI dashboards for service performance and billing

l3Processes.title: Raise purchase requisitions
l3Processes.description:
Initiates the procurement cycle by capturing internal demand for goods or services. Purchase requisitions (PRs) can be created manually, through punch-out catalogs, or integrated from external systems. Supports both item-based and category-based requisition lines, enabling flexible sourcing aligned with business needs. Scope & Outcomes
Covers creation, submission, and approval of purchase requisitions.
Supports manual entry, catalog-based selection, and automated integrations.
Outcomes include approved requisitions ready for conversion to purchase orders, visibility into demand, and alignment with budget and sourcing policies. Requisition Types  Item-based lines: Specific SKUs or inventory items with quantity, unit price, and delivery details.  Category-based lines: Non-catalog goods or services grouped by procurement category, often used for indirect spend.  Punch-out requisitions: Real-time integration with supplier catalogs for dynamic item selection and pricing.  Integrated requisitions: Automatically generated from external systems (e.g., project management, maintenance, or planning tools).  Entry Methods  Manual creation via procurement workspace or employee self-service.  Punch-out catalog selection with automatic line population.  Import from external systems using APIs or batch uploads.  Key Attributes Captured  Requester details, cost center, business unit  Item/category, quantity, unit price, delivery date  Supplier (optional), justification, attachments  Budget check, approval routing, policy flags  Operating Model  Portal-driven requisition entry for employees and procurement teams  Workflow automation for approvals based on thresholds, roles, and policies  Integration with budget control and financial dimensions  Requisition consolidation for bulk purchasing or blanket orders  Technology Support  Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management: Requisition creation, approval workflows, budget validation, and PO conversion  Dynamics 365 Business Central: Simplified requisition entry, approval routing, and vendor linkage  Punch-out integration: Connects to supplier-hosted catalogs for real-time item selection  Workflow automation: Routes requisitions for approval based on spend limits and category rules  Roles & Responsibilities  Requesters: Initiate requisitions, provide specifications, confirm receipt  Procurement Managers: Review requisitions, validate sourcing strategy, approve or redirect  Finance/AP: Validate budget availability, ensure auditability  Category Managers: Oversee category-based requisitions, enforce preferred supplier usage  Deliverables  Approved purchase requisitions  Consolidated requisition reports  Budget compliance logs  Requisition-to-PO conversion records

l3Processes.title: Issue purchase orders
l3Processes.description:
Formalizes procurement commitments by issuing purchase orders (POs) to suppliers. POs are generated from approved purchase requisitions, purchase agreements, RFQs, or directly for project-based, intercompany, or drop shipment scenarios. Supports integration with external systems such as Field Service or Asset Management for spare parts and service-linked purchases. Scope & Outcomes
Covers creation, validation, and transmission of POs across various procurement scenarios.
Outcomes include: Accurate PO records linked to sourcing documents  Supplier commitment to pricing, delivery, and terms  Visibility into procurement obligations across business units and systems  Support for direct delivery, intercompany trade, and service-linked purchases  PO Creation Scenarios From Purchase Requisition: Converts approved requisitions into POs with validated budget and sourcing details  From Purchase Agreement: Uses pre-negotiated pricing and terms for recurring purchases  From RFQ: Selects winning bid and generates PO with agreed terms  Project-Based Purchases: Links PO to project ID, task, or milestone for cost tracking  Intercompany Trade: Issues PO to internal legal entity with mirrored sales order  Drop Shipment / Direct Delivery: PO sent to supplier for delivery directly to customer or field location  Spare Parts / Maintenance: POs triggered by Field Service or Asset Management systems for parts replenishment or service execution  Key Attributes Captured Supplier ID, legal entity, delivery address  Item or category, quantity, unit price, currency  Delivery date, payment terms, tax group  Project ID, cost center, business unit  PO type (standard, blanket, intercompany, drop ship)  Linked documents (requisition, agreement, RFQ)  Operating Model Portal-driven: Procurement teams or requesters initiate PO creation via workspace  Automated: System-generated POs from requisitions, agreements, or external triggers  Integrated: APIs and connectors link PO creation to Field Service, Asset Management, or external planning systems  Workflow-enabled: Approval routing based on spend thresholds, category, or business unit  Technology Support Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management: PO creation, approval workflows, intercompany trade, drop shipment, and project linkage  Dynamics 365 Business Central: Simplified PO issuance, invoice matching, and vendor management  Field Service / Asset Management: Triggers PO creation for parts and services  Workflow Automation: Approval routing, notifications, and audit trails  Power BI: PO analytics, spend tracking, supplier performance  Roles & Responsibilities Requesters: Confirm PO details, receive goods/services  Procurement Managers: Create, review, and approve POs  Finance / AP: Validate pricing, match invoices, ensure auditability  Category Managers: Oversee PO compliance with sourcing strategy  Project Managers / Field Technicians: Initiate service-linked POs, confirm delivery  Deliverables Issued purchase orders with linked sourcing documents  PO status dashboards (open, confirmed, received, invoiced)  Audit logs of approvals and changes  Spend reports by category, supplier, and project

l3Processes.title: Manage open purchases
l3Processes.description:
Manages post-issuance lifecycle of purchase orders (POs), including updates, cancellations, delivery adjustments, and follow-ups. Supports change management workflows for quantity, pricing, delivery dates, and cross-docking. Ensures procurement agility, supplier coordination, and compliance with approval policies. Scope & Outcomes
Covers all modifications to open POs across procurement scenarios.
Outcomes include: Accurate, up-to-date PO records  Controlled change approvals  Timely supplier communication  Visibility into PO status and fulfillment  Change Management Scenarios  Quantity Adjustments: Increase or decrease ordered quantities based on demand or supplier constraints  Pricing Updates: Reflect negotiated changes, discounts, or corrections  Delivery Information: Modify delivery dates, locations, or methods (e.g., cross-docking)  Partial or Full Cancellation: Cancel entire PO or specific lines due to budget changes, supplier issues, or sourcing shifts  Follow-ups: Track PO status, confirm delivery, escalate delays  Supported PO Types  Standard POs  Intercompany POs  Drop shipment / direct delivery  Project-based POs  Blanket order releases  Operating Model  Portal-driven: Procurement teams and requesters initiate changes via workspace  Workflow-enabled: Approval routing for sensitive changes (e.g., pricing, quantity)  System-integrated: Updates reflected across linked systems (inventory, finance, project management)  Supplier collaboration: Notifications and confirmations for PO changes  Technology Support  Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management
PO change tracking  Delivery schedule updates  Cross-docking configuration  Workflow approvals    Dynamics 365 Business Central
PO editing  Cancellation handling  Invoice matching    Field Service / Asset Management
PO updates for service-linked parts and maintenance    Workflow Automation
Escalation routing, notifications, audit trails    Roles & Responsibilities  Procurement Managers: Initiate and approve PO changes, coordinate with suppliers  Requesters: Request updates, confirm delivery  Finance / AP: Validate pricing changes, ensure auditability  Warehouse / Receiving: Confirm delivery, initiate returns or adjustments  Project Managers / Field Technicians: Trigger updates for project or service-linked POs  Deliverables  Updated PO records with change history  Cancellation logs and audit trails  Delivery schedule reports  Supplier communication records  PO performance dashboard

l3Processes.title: Issue blanket purchase orders
l3Processes.description:
Establishes long-term purchasing arrangements with suppliers for recurring goods or services. Blanket orders define pricing, quantities, and delivery schedules over a specified period, streamlining procurement and reducing administrative overhead. In F&O, these are managed as purchase agreements and can be linked to requisitions, purchase orders, and trade agreements. Scope & Outcomes
Supports strategic sourcing by locking in terms with preferred suppliers. Enables automated PO creation against agreed terms, improves spend forecasting, and enforces compliance with negotiated pricing and delivery conditions.
Outcomes include: Validated purchase agreements with supplier commitments  Automated PO generation based on releases  Spend tracking against agreement thresholds  Reduced manual entry and improved procurement efficiency   Types of Purchase Agreements  Value Commitment: Specifies a total monetary value to be purchased over time.  Quantity Commitment: Defines specific quantities of items to be procured.  Product Category Commitment: Applies to a category of items rather than specific SKUs.  Blanket Orders with Release Schedules: Used for recurring deliveries with predefined intervals or triggers.  Operating Model
Procurement teams create purchase agreements in F&O, linking them to vendor records and trade agreements. Agreements can be referenced in requisitions and POs, enabling automated enforcement of pricing and terms. Release orders are generated manually or automatically based on demand, forecasts, or consumption. Technology Support  Dynamics 365 Finance and Operations: Manages purchase agreements, release orders, and trade agreement enforcement.  Supplier Collaboration Portal: Enables suppliers to view agreement terms, confirm releases, and submit updates.  Workflow Automation: Routes agreements for approval, monitors thresholds, and triggers alerts.  Power BI: Provides dashboards for agreement utilization, supplier performance, and spend tracking.  Roles & Responsibilities  Procurement Managers: Define agreement terms, negotiate with suppliers, and monitor usage.  Finance/AP: Validate pricing, ensure auditability, and manage payment terms.  Category Managers: Oversee category-level agreements and supplier compliance.  Requesters: Reference agreements in requisitions and confirm deliveries.  Deliverables  Executed purchase agreements with supplier terms  Release order records linked to agreements  Spend utilization reports and compliance dashboards  Audit logs of agreement changes and approvals

l3Processes.title: Return goods to suppliers
l3Processes.description:
Manages the return of goods to suppliers due to quality issues, overages, incorrect deliveries, or other exceptions. Supports full and partial returns using debit/credit notes, negative lines, and purchase return orders. Also accommodates specialized scenarios where the original purchase order is linked to fixed assets, projects, or service operations. Ensures accurate financial reconciliation, inventory adjustments, and supplier coordination. Scope & Outcomes
Covers initiation, approval, and execution of returns across procurement scenarios.
Outcomes include: Validated return transactions and financial adjustments  Updated inventory and asset records  Supplier notifications and credit processing  Audit trail of return reasons and actions   Return Scenarios  Standard Returns: Full or partial returns of inventory items due to defects, overages, or incorrect shipments  Negative PO Lines: Used to reverse quantities or pricing on existing purchase orders  Debit/Credit Notes: Financial documents issued to reconcile supplier accounts for returned goods  Purchase Return Orders: Formal return documents linked to original PO for traceability  Fixed Asset Returns: Items procured as assets returned due to damage or misfit; triggers asset reversal workflows  Project-Based Returns: Goods linked to project procurement returned due to scope changes or quality issues  Service-Linked Returns: Spare parts or consumables returned from Field Service or Asset Maintenance modules   Operating Model  Portal-driven initiation by requesters or receiving teams  Workflow-enabled approvals based on return reason, value, and supplier policies  System-integrated updates to inventory, finance, and asset/project modules  Supplier collaboration for confirmation, credit issuance, and replacement coordination   Technology Support  Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management:
Purchase return order creation  Inventory adjustments and financial reconciliation  Integration with fixed asset and project modules    Dynamics 365 Business Central:
Credit memo generation  Vendor account updates  Return handling for service-linked purchases    Field Service / Asset Management:
Triggers for part returns and service-related reversals    Workflow Automation:
Approval routing, notifications, audit trails     Roles & Responsibilities  Requesters / Receiving Teams: Identify return needs, initiate return requests  Procurement Managers: Approve returns, coordinate with suppliers  Finance / AP: Issue debit/credit notes, reconcile vendor accounts  Warehouse / Inventory: Process physical returns, update stock levels  Asset / Project Managers: Validate returns linked to assets or projects  Service Technicians: Trigger returns for failed parts or service items   Deliverables  Approved purchase return orders  Debit/credit notes and financial adjustments  Updated inventory and asset records  Supplier communication logs  Return reason reports and audit trails

l3Processes.title: Consolidate requisitions
l3Processes.description: Combines multiple purchase requisitions into a single consolidated requisition or purchase order to streamline procurement, reduce administrative overhead, and optimize supplier engagement. This process is especially useful when multiple departments or cost centers request similar items or services within a close timeframe. Scope & OutcomesSupports consolidation of requisitions based on item, category, supplier, delivery date, or business unit.Outcomes include: Fewer purchase orders issued Improved negotiation leverage with suppliers Reduced processing time and manual effort Better alignment with blanket orders and purchase agreements  Consolidation Scenarios Multiple requisitions for the same item from different requesters or departments Category-based requisitions for indirect spend (e.g., office supplies, IT equipment) Requisitions linked to planned purchases or forecasted demand Consolidation across projects or cost centers for shared services or assets  Operating ModelProcurement teams use the Demand Consolidation workspace in F&O to identify and merge requisitions based on predefined rules.System suggests consolidation opportunities based on item match, supplier, delivery window, and budget availability.Approved consolidated requisitions are converted into purchase orders or linked to existing purchase agreements. Technology Support Dynamics 365 Finance and OperationsDemand Consolidation workspace Purchase requisition management Budget control and approval workflows Integration with purchase agreements and trade agreements   Workflow automation for approvals and notifications Power BI dashboards for tracking consolidation efficiency and spend impact  Roles & Responsibilities Requesters: Submit requisitions with accurate item/category details Procurement Managers: Review consolidation suggestions, approve merged requisitions, issue POs Finance/AP: Validate budget availability and audit compliance Category Managers: Oversee consolidation rules and supplier alignment  Deliverables Consolidated requisition records Linked purchase orders Spend optimization reports Audit logs of consolidation actions and approvals

l3Processes.title: Analyze supply purchase plan
l3Processes.description:
Analyzes supply and purchase plans to ensure timely procurement aligned with demand forecasts, inventory levels, and project needs. This process supports master planning activities by reviewing planned purchase orders, delayed or short receipts, and responding to action messages. It enables proactive decision-making to adjust supply plans, confirm procurement actions, and maintain operational continuity. Scope & Outcomes
Covers review and adjustment of planned purchase orders, confirmation of supply actions, and resolution of supply exceptions.
Outcomes include: Validated planned purchase orders Adjusted supply schedules based on delays or shortages Executed action messages (e.g., expedite, postpone, cancel) Improved supply chain responsiveness and procurement accuracy  Planning Scenarios Planned Purchase Orders: Generated from demand forecasts, MRP, or project schedules; reviewed for confirmation or adjustment. Delayed Receipts: Identifies overdue deliveries; triggers follow-up or rescheduling. Short Receipts: Flags partial deliveries; initiates corrective actions or supplier communication. Action Messages: System-generated recommendations to expedite, delay, cancel, or change orders based on updated planning data. Project-Based Supply: Ensures alignment of supply plans with project timelines and resource needs.  Operating Model Procurement and planning teams review supply plans in master planning workspace. Action messages are processed to adjust procurement actions. Planned orders are confirmed or modified based on supply chain constraints and business priorities. Integration with inventory, production, and project modules ensures holistic planning.  Technology Support Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management
Master planning engine for generating planned orders  Action message processing for supply adjustments  Integration with inventory, production, and project modules    Business Central
Simplified planning and purchase order generation  Visibility into supply schedules and procurement actions    Power BI
Dashboards for supply plan performance, delayed receipts, and action message execution    Roles & Responsibilities Procurement Managers: Confirm planned orders, execute action messages, coordinate with suppliers. Planners: Analyze supply plans, adjust schedules, validate demand forecasts. Warehouse / Receiving: Report short or delayed receipts, support corrective actions. Project Managers: Align supply plans with project milestones and resource needs.  Deliverables Confirmed planned purchase orders  Action message execution logs  Supply plan dashboards (delays, shortages, adjustments)  Supplier follow-up records and resolution notes

l3Processes.title: Process supplier invoices

apqc.description: Audit invoices and key data in AP system

l3Processes.description:
A vendor invoice is generated upon receiving products or services as per a
purchase order. It consists of a header and item or service lines, marking the
last step in the purchase process. Some invoices may not correspond to purchase
orders, like ongoing service bills. There are various methods to enter vendor
invoices, including, vendor invoice approval journal, vendor invoice journal for
non-purchase order invoices, and using the vendor invoice pool for expense
accrual.
Additionally, you can create vendor invoices based on confirmed purchase orders
using the Open vendor invoices and Pending vendor invoices pages.
The vendor invoices business process area should be defined and incorporated
into the overall implementation. This phase typically occurs at the beginning of
a project's lifecycle and involves establishing project goals, defining project
scope, and developing a detailed project plan. The setup and configuration of
your products and base pricing along with the general procurement process should
be in place before the vendor invoice process.

l3Processes.title: Dispute invoices

apqc.description: Audit invoices and key data in AP system

l3Processes.description:
Invoice dispute management in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows
users to flag, track, and resolve discrepancies between supplier invoices and
purchase orders or receipts. The system supports workflow-driven dispute
resolution, enabling AP teams to communicate with vendors, document issues, and
manage dispute statuses. Disputes can be categorized (e.g., pricing, quantity,
terms), and resolution steps are logged for audit purposes. Integration with
vendor collaboration portals facilitates direct communication and document
exchange, while configurable notifications and escalations ensure timely
resolution.

apqc.description: Audit invoices and key data in AP system

l3Processes.title: Issue and settle supplier payments
l3Processes.description:
Issuing and settling supplier payments in Dynamics 365 finance and operations
apps encompasses payment proposal generation, approval, and execution via
checks, ACH, wire, or other methods. The system supports payment batching,
positive pay file generation, bank integration, and payment status tracking.
Approval workflows ensure segregation of duties, and payment runs can be
scheduled or triggered on demand. The platform provides audit trails, exception
handling (e.g., failed ACH), and supports recurring and one-time payments.

l3Processes.title: Manage promissory notes
l3Processes.description:
Managing promissory notes in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
tracking, issuing, and settling formal payment commitments to suppliers. The
system supports creation of promissory notes, linking to invoices, scheduling
payments, and monitoring maturity dates. Approval workflows ensure compliance,
and integration with banking systems enables automated settlement. Audit trails
document all actions, and reporting provides visibility into outstanding notes
and payment schedules.

l3Processes.title: Cancel supplier payments
l3Processes.description:
Cancelling supplier payments in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows
users to void or reverse payments due to errors, disputes, or other reasons. The
system supports cancellation of checks, ACH, and wire payments, with automated
workflows for approval and audit documentation. Cancelled payments are tracked,
and associated invoices are reopened for further action. Integration with
banking platforms ensures proper handling of voided transactions.

l3Processes.title: Correct supplier payments
l3Processes.description:
Correcting supplier payments in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
involves adjusting payment amounts, reallocating payments, or reissuing payments
due to errors or changes. The system supports correction workflows, audit
trails, and integration with banking platforms for reissuance. Corrections can
be linked to original transactions, and approval workflows ensure compliance.

l3Processes.title: Process supplier rebates and incentives
l3Processes.description:
Processing supplier rebates and incentives in Dynamics 365 finance and
operations apps enables organizations to track, claim, and apply rebates or
incentives offered by vendors. The system supports rebate agreement setup,
automated calculation based on purchases, and application to invoices or
credits. Approval workflows ensure compliance, and reporting provides visibility
into earned and claimed rebates.

l3Processes.title: Measure and analyze procurement spend
l3Processes.description:
Measure and Analyze Procurement Spend in Dynamics 365 finance and operations
apps enables organizations to track, categorize, and report on all procurement
expenditures. The system aggregates spend data across vendors, categories, and
business units, providing dashboards and analytics to identify cost-saving
opportunities, monitor compliance, and support strategic sourcing decisions.
Standard features include spend analysis reports, vendor spend breakdowns, and
integration with Power BI for advanced analytics.

l3Processes.title: Manage supplier performance
l3Processes.description:
See also Processes list under #7797
[https://dev.azure.com/BDODigitalDynamicsDemo/Project%20Template/_work%20items/edit/7797/]

l3Processes.title: Analyze rebates and incentives
l3Processes.description:
Analyze Rebates and Incentives in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
enables organizations to track, calculate, and report on supplier rebates,
discounts, and incentive programs. The system automates accruals, validates
eligibility, and ensures accurate financial recognition. Users can configure
rebate agreements, monitor performance against targets, and generate reports for
audit and compliance.

l3Processes.title: Establish sales commission and incentive structures
Analyze sourcing effectiveness
l3Processes.description:
Analyze Sourcing Effectiveness in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
provides analytics on sourcing strategies, supplier selection, and procurement
outcomes. The system tracks sourcing events, bid responses, contract awards, and
savings achieved. Users can compare sourcing cycles, evaluate supplier
competitiveness, and identify opportunities for improvement. Integration with
procurement and supplier management modules ensures end-to-end visibility.

l3Processes.title: Analyze account payable
l3Processes.description:
Analyze Accounts Payable in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps provides
comprehensive visibility into AP transactions, aging, payment cycles, and vendor
balances. The system supports automated invoice matching, approval workflows,
and payment processing (ACH, check, wire). Users can generate aging reports,
monitor payment status, and reconcile accounts. Integration with procurement and
financial modules ensures accurate and timely AP management.

l3Processes.title: Manage procurement risks
l3Processes.description:
Manage Procurement Risks in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enables
organizations to identify, assess, and mitigate risks in the procurement
process. The system supports risk scoring, compliance checks, and audit trails.
Users can configure risk categories, monitor supplier risk profiles, and
automate alerts for high-risk transactions. Integration with supplier management
and compliance modules ensures proactive risk management.

l3Processes.title: Develop program charter
l3Processes.description:
The govern projects business process area encompasses the procedures, policies,
and structures that are used to manage recurring activities that deliver a
particular product or service. Effective management of these processes is
crucial for maintaining efficiency, improving productivity, and driving growth.
In this context, Dynamics 365 plays a pivotal role. As a comprehensive business
solution, it provides robust tools for project governance, so that businesses
can streamline their processes, enhance collaboration, and make data-driven
decisions.
The process of defining, scoping, and implementing this business process area in
an overall implementation with business apps is a strategic endeavor. Ideally,
it should occur during the initial stages of system planning. In this way, you
gain a holistic view of the organization's processes and increase the likelihood
that the solution is tailored to meet the organization's unique needs.
Important: Project governance is an iterative process. As the organization
evolves, so should its use of Dynamics 365. Conduct regular reviews and updates
to ensure that the implementation continues to provide value and meet the
changing needs of the business.

l3Processes.title: Develop project management strategy
l3Processes.description:
The "Develop Project Management Strategy" process in Dynamics 365 finance and
operations apps defines the methodologies, tools, and standards to be used for
managing projects throughout their lifecycle. This includes selecting project
management frameworks (e.g., waterfall, agile), establishing project controls,
risk management approaches, resource planning, and reporting standards. Dynamics
365 F\&O supports this by allowing organizations to configure project templates,
define work breakdown structures (WBS), set up project stages and gates, and
enforce standardized processes through workflow and automation. The system also
enables the capture of lessons learned and continuous improvement feedback.

l3Processes.title: Develop project governance strategy
l3Processes.description:
The "Develop Project Governance Strategy" process in Dynamics 365 finance and
operations apps establishes the framework for oversight, decision-making, and
accountability throughout the project lifecycle. This includes defining
governance bodies (e.g., steering committees), roles and responsibilities,
escalation paths, and key performance indicators (KPIs). Dynamics 365 finance
and operations apps supports governance by enabling workflow-driven approvals,
audit trails, role-based access controls, and automated notifications for key
governance events (such as budget overruns or milestone completions). The system
also facilitates the documentation and tracking of governance meetings,
decisions, and action items.

l3Processes.title: Develop project approval processes
l3Processes.description:
The "Develop Project Approval Processes" in Dynamics 365 finance and operations
apps defines the structured steps required to authorize projects, budgets, and
major changes. This includes initial project requests, business case
submissions, budget approvals, and change order management. Dynamics 365 finance
and operations apps enables organizations to configure multi-level approval
workflows, enforce segregation of duties, and maintain a complete audit trail of
all approvals and rejections. The system supports conditional routing based on
project type, value, or risk, and integrates with financial controls to prevent
unauthorized spending.

l3Processes.title: Estimate project contracts

apqc.description: Develop and manage sales proposals, bids, and quotes

l3Processes.description:
Project contracts serve as a legally binding agreement between a project provider and a client in the project sales process. It outlines the scope, deliverables, timelines, costs, and terms of the project. This document ensures both parties' expectations are aligned, mitigates potential conflicts, and provides a framework for successful project execution. It acts as a safeguard, promoting transparency and accountability throughout the project's lifecycle.
 Effective project contract management faces multifaceted challenges. Navigating complex legal frameworks, ensuring compliance with evolving regulations, and managing diverse stakeholder expectations strains seamless execution. Ambiguities in contract terms and obligations often lead to disputes, demanding meticulous communication and documentation. Integrating digital tools for efficient tracking while fostering clear vendor-client collaboration remains vital. Addressing these challenges fosters successful project delivery, minimizing risks and optimizing outcomes.
 Modern trends in project contract management include a shift towards digitalization and automation, with the integration of contract lifecycle management (CLM) software. The adoption of AI-powered tools for contract analysis and risk assessment, increased emphasis on data security and privacy compliance within contracts, growing integration of blockchain for transparent and tamper-proof contract records, and the utilization of collaborative cloud platforms for real-time updates and communication. These trends aim to enhance efficiency, accuracy, and collaboration while mitigating legal and operational risks in project contract management.
 In conclusion, the project contract stands as the cornerstone of the project sales process, encapsulating terms, deliverables, timelines, and expectations. It safeguards interests, ensuring a clear understanding between parties. Its meticulous formulation cultivates trust, minimizes disputes, and paves the way for successful project execution, fostering enduring client relationships.

l3Processes.title: Negotiate project contracts

apqc.description: Develop and manage sales proposals, bids, and quotes

l3Processes.description:
The “Negotiate project contracts” process in Dynamics 365 F\&O facilitates
structured negotiation of contract terms, pricing, deliverables, and risk
allocations with customers. The system supports version control of contract
drafts, tracks negotiation history, and enables collaboration among internal
stakeholders (legal, finance, operations). Dynamics 365 F\&O allows for the
simulation of different contract scenarios, impact analysis on profitability,
and automated routing of contract drafts for internal review and approval.
Integration with document management and e-signature platforms streamlines the
negotiation-to-signature process.

l3Processes.title: Finalize project contracts

apqc.description: Develop and manage sales proposals, bids, and quotes

l3Processes.description:
The “Finalize project contracts” process in Dynamics 365 finance and operations
apps encompasses the formal acceptance and execution of negotiated contracts.
This includes capturing final terms, pricing, deliverables, and compliance
requirements. Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps supports automated
contract approval workflows, digital signatures, and the generation of contract
records linked to projects, customers, and financial dimensions. The system
ensures that only approved contracts proceed to project setup and billing,
maintaining compliance and auditability.

l3Processes.title: Govern projects
l3Processes.description:
The manage project delivery business process area is key to tracking the work
that is delivered on a project. It helps ensure that the final outcomes are
provided to stakeholders in a diligent and timely manner. During this phase, all
the tasks, activities, and milestones that are outlined in the project plan are
performed and completed. Depending on the nature of the project, work might
include various activities, such as the examples in the following list:
 * Design
 * Development
 * Testing
 * Documentation
 * Quality assurance
We recommend that you use a structured approach to track, record, and manage the
effort that team members and resources invest in these activities. In this way,
you gain valuable insights into how the project is progressing and how project
objectives are being met.
This business process area also covers the evaluation of project performance,
estimation of task completion times, and identification of potential
bottlenecks. In addition, it facilitates the assessment of resource allocation,
and therefore helps optimize workforce utilization and enhance project
efficiency. Through the systematic capture of project time, organizations
enhance their ability to deliver projects on schedule, allocate resources
effectively, and boost overall productivity.
Ideally, the structure of the manage project delivery business process is
defined during the early stages of project implementation. By outlining the
manage project delivery process as project parameters, timelines, and resource
allocation are being established, you ensure that accurate data collection
begins from the project's inception. Although the process might naturally evolve
as the project progresses, it's important to lay its foundation before the
project work begins.

l3Processes.title: Maintain project contracts

apqc.description: Develop and manage sales proposals, bids, and quotes

l3Processes.description:
The “Maintain project contracts” process in Dynamics 365 finance and operations
apps enables ongoing management of contract records, including amendments,
renewals, and compliance tracking. The system supports version control, audit
trails, and automated notifications for key contract milestones (e.g., renewal
dates, compliance checks). Users can initiate and track contract changes,
ensuring all modifications are reviewed and approved before implementation.
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps also provides reporting on contract
performance, obligations, and risks.

apqc.description: Develop and manage sales proposals, bids, and quotes

l3Processes.title: Define project stakeholders

apqc.description: Develop and manage sales proposals, bids, and quotes

l3Processes.description:
The “Define project stakeholders” process in Dynamics 365 finance and operations
apps involves identifying and recording all internal and external parties with a
vested interest in the project contract. This includes customers,
subcontractors, regulatory bodies, and internal stakeholders. Dynamics 365
finance and operations apps supports stakeholder mapping, role assignment, and
automated notifications for key contract events. Stakeholder information is
linked to contract and project records, supporting communication, compliance,
and reporting.

l3Processes.title: Define project scope
l3Processes.description:
Resources and the schedule are critical during the project lifecycle. The
ability to track these two types of information helps ensure that your project
is in a healthy state. To get started, define a baseline at the beginning of
your project. Then, during the project's lifecycle, refine that baseline through
estimation to complete by adjusting the resources and schedule.
As a project manager, you should first collect requirements, define the project
scope, and plan the project, including all required costs and generated revenue.
Once you have all these elements, you can create and define the first budget
baseline to follow. During the project lifecycle, you should periodically
reassess the resources and schedule to ensure they reflect changes, and validate
that your project is profitable according to the baseline.
This business process should be defined and incorporated into the overall
implementation during the project planning phase, which is typically the first
step of your project.

l3Processes.title: Manage project budget
l3Processes.description:
Managing project budgets in Dynamics 365 F\&O involves establishing, monitoring,
and controlling the financial plan for a project. The system supports budget
creation at multiple levels (overall, phase, task), budget revisions, and
real-time tracking of actuals vs. budget. Dynamics 365 finance and operations
apps provides tools for cost control, variance analysis, and integration with
procurement and expense management. Budget workflows ensure that changes are
reviewed and approved according to governance policies.

l3Processes.title: Plan projects resources
l3Processes.description:
Resource planning in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enables
organizations to identify, allocate, and schedule the necessary labor,
equipment, and materials required to execute a project. The system supports
resource forecasting, skills matching, and availability checks, allowing project
managers to optimize resource utilization. Resource requirements can be defined
at the project or task level, and Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
provides visibility into resource assignments, utilization rates, and potential
conflicts. Integration with HR and asset management modules ensures that both
human and physical resources are considered in the planning process.

l3Processes.title: Plan project tasks
l3Processes.description:
Planning project tasks in Dynamics 365 F\&O involves breaking down the project
scope into manageable work packages or tasks, sequencing them, and assigning
dependencies. The system allows users to define work breakdown structures (WBS),
set task durations, assign resources, and establish milestones. Task planning
supports both top-down and bottom-up approaches and integrates with scheduling
tools (e.g., Microsoft Project, P6) for advanced planning needs. Task templates
and standard WBS structures can be leveraged for repeatable project types.

l3Processes.title: Assign project resources
l3Processes.description:
Assigning project resources in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
linking specific personnel, equipment, or materials to project tasks or phases.
The system supports assignment based on availability, skills, and location, and
provides visibility into resource workloads and conflicts. Assignments can be
made manually or through automated rules, and notifications can be triggered for
resource allocation changes. Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps also
supports tracking of actual resource usage against planned assignments for
performance analysis.

l3Processes.title: Forecast project resources
l3Processes.description:
Forecasting project resources in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
enables organizations to predict future resource needs based on project
schedules, task plans, and historical data. The system supports scenario
analysis, resource leveling, and what-if modeling to optimize resource
allocation and minimize bottlenecks. Forecasts can be generated at various
levels (project, task, resource type) and are integrated with financial
forecasting for comprehensive project planning.

l3Processes.title: Control project scope
l3Processes.description:
Controlling project scope in Dynamics 365 F\&O ensures that all project
deliverables, requirements, and boundaries are clearly defined, tracked, and
managed. The system supports scope change requests, approvals, and
documentation, with audit trails and integration to project schedules and
budgets. This helps prevent scope creep and ensures alignment with client and
organizational objectives.

l3Processes.title: Track project expenses
l3Processes.description:
Tracking project expenses in Dynamics 365 F\&O enables organizations to capture,
categorize, and monitor all costs associated with a project, including labor,
materials, travel, and subcontractor expenses. The system supports expense
entry, approval workflows, integration with procurement and accounts payable,
and real-time reporting on expense status and variances.

l3Processes.title: Track project time
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 F\&O provides comprehensive time tracking for project resources,
allowing employees to enter and allocate time to specific projects or tasks. The
system supports approval workflows, integration with payroll and resource
management, and reporting on time utilization and variances.

l3Processes.title: Track project fees
l3Processes.description:
Tracking project fees in Dynamics 365 F\&O involves managing all billable and
non-billable charges associated with a project, including fixed fees, milestone
payments, and variable charges. The system supports fee schedules, billing
rules, and integration with invoicing and revenue recognition.

l3Processes.title: Purchase project materials
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 F\&O enables organizations to manage the procurement of materials
required for project delivery. The system supports requisitioning, purchase
order creation, approval workflows, supplier management, and integration with
inventory and AP.

l3Processes.title: Use and track project materials
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 F\&O provides tools for tracking the usage and consumption of
project materials, including inventory management, material issuance, and
consumption reporting. Integration with procurement and project costing ensures
accurate tracking of material costs.

l3Processes.title: Produce project materials
l3Processes.description:
For organizations that manufacture or assemble materials for projects, Dynamics
365 F\&O supports production order management, BOM tracking, and integration
with inventory and costing. This enables accurate tracking of production costs
and material availability.

l3Processes.title: Subcontract project components
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 F\&O enables organizations to manage subcontracted work, including
subcontractor selection, contract management, progress tracking, and integration
with procurement and AP. The system supports approval workflows, milestone
tracking, and compliance management.

l3Processes.title: Manage project communications
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 F\&O, integrated with Microsoft 365, supports project communication
management through Teams, Outlook, and SharePoint. This includes meeting
scheduling, action item tracking, and centralized communication logs, ensuring
all project stakeholders are informed and aligned.

l3Processes.title: Manage project knowledge and documentation
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 F\&O, with SharePoint integration, provides a centralized
repository for all project documentation and knowledge assets. This includes
version control, document approvals, and secure access management, supporting
compliance and knowledge sharing.

l3Processes.title: Correct project transactions
l3Processes.description:
The manage project financials business process area focuses on managing the
financial data that is associated with project-related activities. It involves
generating, processing, and reviewing invoices according to contractual
arrangements. It also involves tracking payments, managing project budgets,
adjusting project transactions, and recognizing project revenue. The manage
project financials business process ensures the accuracy of the financial data
that is linked to the project. In this way, it also ensures a healthy cash flow
for your organization.
Manage project financials is an essential step in the project to profit
end-to-end business process flow. Maintaining a structured and well-defined
approach to project financial management is critical for preventing financial
risks and payment delays. It also helps ensure control and successful completion
of project tasks in your organization.
The manage project financials business process should be outlined and
incorporated into the overall implementation during the planning phase. The
planning phase typically occurs at the project's launch. It involves setting
project goals, defining project scope, and developing a detailed project plan.

l3Processes.title: Convert projects to fixed assets
l3Processes.description:
The “Convert projects to fixed assets” process in Dynamics 365 finance and
operations apps allows organizations to capitalize completed project costs as
fixed assets. This process supports the transfer of accumulated project
expenditures (such as construction, equipment installation, or internal
development) into the fixed asset register. Dynamics 365 finance and operations
apps automates asset creation, allocation of costs, depreciation schedule setup,
and compliance with accounting standards. The process ensures that only eligible
costs are capitalized, with full traceability from project transactions to asset
records.

l3Processes.title: Invoice project milestones
l3Processes.description:
The “Invoice project milestones” process in Dynamics 365 finance and operations
apps enables organizations to generate customer invoices based on the
achievement of predefined project milestones. Milestone billing supports
contractual arrangements where payments are tied to deliverables, phases, or
completion percentages. Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps automates
milestone tracking, invoice generation, approval workflows, and integration with
revenue recognition. The process ensures that milestone achievement is
validated, supporting documentation is attached, and invoices are generated in
accordance with contract terms.

l3Processes.title: Invoice project transactions
l3Processes.description:
The “Invoice project transactions” process in Dynamics 365 finance and
operations apps supports the generation of customer invoices based on actual
project transactions, such as time, materials, expenses, and subcontractor
costs. Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps provides flexible billing rules,
markup calculations, grouping, and approval workflows. The process ensures that
all billable transactions are validated, supporting documentation is attached,
and invoices are generated in compliance with contract terms. Integration with
accounts receivable and revenue recognition ensures end-to-end financial
control.

l3Processes.title: Recognize project revenue
l3Processes.description:
The “Recognize project revenue” process in Dynamics 365 finance and operations
apps enables organizations to record revenue in accordance with project
progress, contract terms, and accounting standards (e.g., percentage of
completion, completed contract, milestone-based). Dynamics 365 finance and
operations apps automates revenue recognition calculations, supports multiple
recognition methods, and provides audit trails and approval workflows. The
process ensures that revenue is recognized accurately and timely, with full
traceability to project transactions and billing events.

l3Processes.title: Monitor project status
l3Processes.description:
The monitor and analyze project performance business process area focuses on
systematically monitoring, assessing, and evaluating various project elements,
including the following list:
 * How much of the project scope is already delivered?
 * What remains?
 * What are the project costs and revenue?
 * Are there any risks?
 * Which issues prevent the project from progressing as planned and meeting its
   objectives?
By monitoring project performance and conducting in-depth analysis, project
managers and stakeholders can gain valuable insights, make informed decisions,
and take any corrective action that is required.
The process of monitoring and analyzing project performance should be
established at the onset of the project. As soon as the project objectives,
scope, and resources are defined, it's imperative to set up a monitoring and
analysis mechanism. In this way, you ensure that a system for gauging progress,
measuring performance, and implementing course corrections is in place from the
earliest stages. Early incorporation of this business process area also ensures
that data collection is consistent throughout the project, so that insights for
analysis are more accurate.

l3Processes.title: Analyze project metrics
l3Processes.description:
Consuming consigned (non-owned inventory) on consignment orders- marking items
as non-inventory

l3Processes.title: Monitor project risks
l3Processes.description:
Monitoring project risks in Dynamics 365 Finance enables organizations to
identify, assess, and track risks throughout the project lifecycle. The system
supports risk registers, automated risk scoring, mitigation planning, and
escalation workflows. Project teams can document risk events, assign ownership,
and monitor the effectiveness of mitigation actions. The process ensures that
risks are proactively managed and that stakeholders are informed of potential
impacts to project objectives.

l3Processes.title: Manage project quality
l3Processes.description:
Managing project quality in Dynamics 365 Finance provides organizations with
tools to define, monitor, and enforce quality standards throughout the project
lifecycle. The system supports quality plans, inspection checklists,
non-conformance tracking, and corrective action workflows. Project teams can
document quality events, assign responsibility, and monitor resolution. This
process ensures that deliverables meet contractual and regulatory requirements,
and that lessons learned are captured for future improvement.

l3Processes.title: Measure project progress
l3Processes.description:
Measuring project progress in Dynamics 365 Finance enables organizations to
track the completion of project tasks, milestones, and deliverables against the
project plan. The system supports progress entry, milestone tracking, and
automated calculation of percent complete. Project managers can compare actual
progress to planned schedules, identify variances, and forecast completion
dates. This process underpins accurate project reporting, revenue recognition,
and resource planning.

l3Processes.title: Maintain contacts and accounts
l3Processes.description:
Strong customer relationship management requires collaboration across Sales,
Service, Marketing, and Operations to maximize customer lifetime value.
Effective customer relationship management starts when Sales classifies and
documents new customer accounts. Prospects can be nurtured so that they become
clients. Sales is also responsible for structuring customer hierarchies to
represent organizational relationships. Account managers and cross-functional
teams are assigned to manage key accounts and develop growth strategies.
As customers begin engaging, Customer Service provides ongoing support across
channels such as email, chat, phone, and in person. Service agents have access
to the full customer history and can therefore deliver personalized experiences.
Marketing creates segmented customer journeys to guide customers through
educational touchpoints. Operations fulfills orders and returns.
Throughout the customer lifecycle, teams track satisfaction metrics and loyalty
through surveys and interaction analysis. This data helps identify upsell
opportunities and prevent churn. Dynamics 365 empowers teams to work together
seamlessly and gain unified visibility into all customer interactions. Proactive
relationship management is vital for maximizing lifetime value, profitability,
and traceability.

l3Processes.title: Nurture opportunities and finalize the sale

apqc.description: Receive Request For Proposal (RFP)/Request For Quote (RFQ)

l3Processes.title: Define sales quotations

apqc.description: Receive Request For Proposal (RFP)/Request For Quote (RFQ)

l3Processes.description:
Setup requires:
 * Admin access in Dynamics 365 Sales.
 * A valid Copilot Studio license.
 * Updated Sales Hub UI.
 * Proper security roles and permissions.

l3Processes.title: Conduct upsell, cross sell or repeat sale prompt
Nurture trust relationship regularly with customer
Provide insights into sales strategies and performance
Develop company structure
l3Processes.description:
In a Dynamics 365 implementation, definition of the financial structure and
definition of organizational policies are crucial steps that significantly
affect the success of the implementation and the overall efficiency of the
business processes. In Dynamics 365 Finance, the chart of accounts, financial
dimensions, and many General ledger parameters and configurations are used in
downstream processes. Because Dynamics 365 Finance is a double-entry system that
integrates directly with Dynamics 365 Commerce, Project Operations, and Supply
Chain Management, all entries that are posted to a subledger are also posted to
the general ledger.
By defining the financial structure early and clearly in your implementation,
you help ensure accurate financial reporting, compliance, data consistency, and
cost allocations. The financial structure in a Dynamics 365 Finance
implementation includes the chart of accounts, cost centers, profit centers,
financial reporting structures, tax codes, and more.
Organizational accounting policies include things such as the legal entity or
company structure, elimination and consolidation rules, and intercompany
accounting configuration, including centralized payments. They also define how
you handle currency conversion and revaluation of your financial data throughout
the system. In addition, if you use the Fixed assets or Asset leasing module, it
covers the asset-related company policies. If you use the Cash and bank
management module, it covers the banking policies and configurations. It's
important to design your processes so that they are streamlined and efficient,
to help reduce manual intervention and minimize errors. When you define your
organizational accounting policies, you should consider standardizing them
across companies and departments. By implementing consistent business processes
across the organization, you help ensure uniformity and reduce confusion among
employees. This standardization is especially important when different
geographies, departments, or companies are involved. Although Dynamics 365
Finance supports flexible configuration across legal entities, the more
processes you can standardize, the smoother your implementation is.
Many other areas rely on or are affected by the decisions that are made in the
define financial structure and organizational accounting policies area.
Therefore, the business processes in this area should be defined as early as
possible in the overall implementation project.

l3Processes.title: Develop financial period strategy
l3Processes.description:
Developing a financial period strategy in Dynamics 365 finance and operations
apps involves defining fiscal calendars, financial periods, and period statuses
to support accurate financial closing, reporting, and compliance. This includes
configuring period opening/closing routines, managing period-end activities, and
ensuring that all modules (GL, AP, AR, etc.) are synchronized for period
management.

l3Processes.title: Define posting policies
l3Processes.description:
Defining posting policies in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
establishes the rules and controls for posting financial transactions to the
general ledger. This includes configuring approval workflows, segregation of
duties, posting restrictions, and audit trails. Posting policies ensure that
only authorized transactions are recorded, supporting compliance and internal
controls.

l3Processes.title: Develop chart of accounts strategy
l3Processes.description:
Developing a chart of accounts (COA) strategy in Dynamics 365 finance and
operations apps involves designing the account structure, segments, and
hierarchies to support financial reporting, analysis, and compliance. The COA
must accommodate business requirements, regulatory needs, and facilitate
consolidation across entities. Dynamics 365 supports flexible COA design,
including main accounts, dimensions, and account structures.

l3Processes.title: Develop budgeting strategy

apqc.description: Develope and maintain budget polices and procedures

l3Processes.description:
Developing a budgeting strategy in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
includes configuring budget models, workflows, and controls to support planning,
forecasting, and variance analysis. Dynamics 365 provides tools for budget
creation, approval, monitoring, and integration with financial reporting. The
system supports multiple budget versions, scenarios, and hierarchical approvals.

l3Processes.title: Develop currency policies
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps Description:
Developing currency policies in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
involves configuring currency codes, exchange rates, and revaluation routines to
support multi-currency operations. The system enables automated currency
conversion, revaluation, and reporting, ensuring compliance with accounting
standards for foreign currency transactions.

l3Processes.title: Define banking policies
l3Processes.description:
Defining banking policies in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps includes
configuring bank accounts, payment methods, reconciliation routines, and
controls for cash management. The system supports automated bank statement
imports, reconciliation workflows, and integration with payment providers.

l3Processes.title: Define costing policies
l3Processes.description:
Defining costing policies in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
setting up cost accounting rules, cost centers, allocation methods, and controls
for tracking and analyzing costs. The system supports direct and indirect cost
allocation, variance analysis, and integration with project accounting.

l3Processes.title: Develop asset policies
l3Processes.description:
Developing asset policies in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps includes
configuring asset types, depreciation methods, asset lifecycle management, and
controls for asset acquisition, transfer, and disposal. The system supports
automated depreciation, asset tracking, and integration with financial
reporting.

l3Processes.title: Develop tax strategy
l3Processes.description:
Developing a tax strategy in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
configuring tax codes, jurisdictions, calculation rules, and compliance
reporting. The system supports automated tax calculation, reporting, and
integration with external tax engines to ensure compliance with local and
international tax regulations.

l3Processes.title: Allocate budgets
l3Processes.description:
Budget allocation in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
distributing approved budget amounts across various entities, such as
departments, projects, or cost centers. The system supports allocation rules,
formulas, and automated distribution based on predefined criteria. Allocations
can be tracked, adjusted, and reported on, ensuring transparency and
accountability in budget management.

l3Processes.title: Establish banking relationships
l3Processes.description:
Establishing banking relationships in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
involves setting up and maintaining records for financial institutions that the
organization interacts with. This includes configuring bank accounts, defining
authorized signatories, setting up electronic payment formats, and managing bank
account security. The system supports multiple bank accounts per legal entity,
allows for the assignment of payment methods, and integrates with electronic
banking standards for payment processing and reconciliation. The process ensures
that all banking details are securely stored and accessible for transactions,
reporting, and compliance purposes.

l3Processes.title: Reconcile bank accounts
l3Processes.description:
Bank account reconciliation in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps is a
core financial process that matches transactions recorded in the ERP against
those reported by the bank. The system provides automated and manual
reconciliation tools, supports importing electronic bank statements, and enables
matching of deposits, withdrawals, and fees. Users can identify discrepancies,
post adjustments, and ensure that the general ledger accurately reflects the
bank’s records. The reconciliation process is supported by audit trails,
approval workflows, and reporting capabilities to facilitate compliance and
financial integrity.

l3Processes.title: Forecast cash flow
l3Processes.description:
Cash flow forecasting in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enables
organizations to project future cash positions based on expected inflows and
outflows. The system aggregates data from accounts receivable, accounts payable,
payroll, and other sources to generate forecasts. Users can model scenarios,
adjust assumptions, and analyze liquidity needs. Dynamics 365 supports both
short-term and long-term forecasting, provides visualization tools, and allows
for integration with external planning systems. The process helps organizations
anticipate funding requirements and optimize cash utilization.

l3Processes.title: Track cash position
l3Processes.description:
Tracking cash position in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
real-time monitoring of available cash across all bank accounts and legal
entities. The system consolidates balances, provides dashboards for cash
visibility, and supports drill-down into transactions. Users can view cash by
currency, entity, and account, and set up alerts for low balances or unusual
activity. The process is tightly integrated with bank reconciliation, payment
processing, and cash flow forecasting, ensuring that decision-makers have
accurate and timely information.

l3Processes.title: Predict customer payments
l3Processes.description:
Predicting customer payments in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
leverages accounts receivable data, payment history, and customer behavior
analytics to estimate when payments will be received. The system can use
statistical models, payment terms, and aging analysis to forecast cash inflows.
Users can identify at-risk receivables, prioritize collection efforts, and
improve cash flow planning. Dynamics 365 supports integration with credit
management and external analytics tools to enhance prediction accuracy.

l3Processes.title: Manage funds
l3Processes.description:
Managing funds in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps encompasses the
movement, allocation, and optimization of cash resources across the
organization. The system supports intercompany transfers, investment management,
funding requests, and compliance with internal policies. Users can initiate fund
transfers, track approvals, and monitor fund utilization. Dynamics 365 provides
audit trails, reporting, and integration with treasury management systems to
ensure efficient and secure fund management.

l3Processes.title: Plan budgets
l3Processes.description:
Budget management is a cornerstone of financial planning and control, and holds
profound significance for businesses across industries. It empowers
organizations to allocate resources efficiently, make informed financial
decisions, and chart a course towards sustainable growth. In an era marked by
economic volatility and shifting market dynamics, a robust budget management
strategy underpins not only fiscal responsibility but also the ability to adapt
and thrive in changing circumstances. By leveraging Dynamics 365 to streamline
this critical business process, enterprises can harness the power of data-driven
insights, automation, and real-time visibility, ultimately optimizing their
financial performance and enhancing their competitive edge.
The budgeting features of Dynamics 365 Finance can be added at any point in your
project or post go-live; however, we recommend that you consider including the
basic budgeting features in your project at a minimum to harness the full
flexibility and data visibility into budgets versus actuals. If Dynamics 365
Finance is not your system of record for financials, we recommend that you
integrate your actual or the totals of your financial transactions into your
system of record for financials where you do manage your budget. As part of a
holistic ERP implementation, budget management should be integrated into your
organization's operations, enabling real-time insights, collaboration, and
data-driven decision-making.

l3Processes.title: Define preliminary budgets
l3Processes.description:
Defining preliminary budgets in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
involves creating initial budget versions that serve as drafts for review and
refinement. Users can input baseline figures, leverage historical data, and
apply business rules to generate preliminary budgets. These drafts can be routed
for feedback, adjusted based on stakeholder input, and compared against multiple
scenarios before moving to formal approval.

l3Processes.title: Reserve budgets
l3Processes.description:
The Reserve Budgets process in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows
organizations to earmark funds for specific purposes, projects, or departments.
Budget reservations ensure that allocated funds are protected from overspending
and can be tracked against commitments. The system supports reservation
workflows, approval hierarchies, and real-time visibility into reserved versus
available budget balances.

l3Processes.title: Maintain budgets
l3Processes.description:
Maintaining budgets in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps encompasses
ongoing updates, adjustments, and monitoring of budget performance. Users can
revise budgets in response to changing business conditions, track actuals
against budgeted amounts, and perform variance analysis. The system supports
audit trails, approval workflows for changes, and integration with reporting
tools for continuous oversight.

l3Processes.title: Approve budgets
l3Processes.description:
The Approve Budgets process in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
formalizes the review and authorization of budget plans. Approval workflows can
be configured to route budgets through multiple levels of management, ensuring
compliance with organizational policies. The system provides notifications,
status tracking, and audit logs to document the approval process.

l3Processes.title: Transfer budgets
l3Processes.description:
Transferring budgets in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows
organizations to reallocate funds between accounts, departments, or projects as
business needs evolve. The system supports transfer requests, approval
workflows, and real-time updates to budget balances. Transfers are tracked for
audit purposes, ensuring transparency and control.

l3Processes.title: Appropriate budgets
l3Processes.description:
Appropriating budgets in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps refers to the
formal assignment of funds for specific purposes, ensuring that expenditures are
authorized and aligned with organizational priorities. The system supports
appropriation workflows, documentation, and compliance checks, providing a clear
audit trail for all appropriated funds.

l3Processes.title: Record ledger entries
l3Processes.description:
Efficiently recording financial transactions is a cornerstone of organizational
success, serving as the bedrock for informed decision-making, compliance, and
financial stability. In the realm of Dynamics 365, this process gains an extra
layer of precision and agility, transforming the way businesses handle their
financial data. The significance of recording financial transactions lies in its
ability to provide a real-time, accurate snapshot of an organization's fiscal
health. Dynamics 365 Finance and Business Central enhance this importance by
streamlining the entire transaction recording process, offering robust tools and
features that contribute to better financial management.
Implementing Dynamics 365 Finance involves navigating a series of crucial
business processes to ensure seamless financial transaction recording. The
overall processes include defining policies, setting approval workflows,
managing ledger postings, recording entries, correcting vouchers, handling
accruals, managing deferrals, and processing allocations. These steps
collectively ensure accurate, real-time financial data. In a Dynamics 365
implementation, start with clear policies and early approval workflows,
progressively addressing ledger management, journal entries, and voucher
corrections. Strategic integration of accruals, deferrals, and allocations at
different project stages maximizes the system's potential, optimizing financial
transaction recording.
When embarking on a Dynamics 365 Finance implementation, addressing the
aforementioned processes strategically is paramount. Begin by defining clear
policies and procedures, setting the stage for a robust financial transaction
recording system. Early in the project, establish the approval process for
journals to ensure a streamlined workflow. As the implementation progresses,
focus on managing ledger postings, recording journal entries, and correcting
vouchers. Accruals, deferrals, and allocations should be methodically integrated
into the system to enhance the accuracy and completeness of financial data. By
approaching these processes thoughtfully at different stages of the project,
organizations can unlock the full potential of Dynamics 365 Finance in
revolutionizing their financial transaction recording practices.

l3Processes.title: Record intercompany transactions
l3Processes.description:
Intercompany transactions in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enable
organizations with multiple legal entities to process financial activities
between those entities. The platform automates reciprocal entries, ensures
proper elimination of intercompany balances during consolidation, and supports
configurable workflows for approval and posting. Intercompany agreements,
pricing, and tax implications are managed within the system.

l3Processes.title: Record cost accounting transactions
l3Processes.description:
Cost accounting in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enables
organizations to track, allocate, and analyze costs across various dimensions
