(departments, projects, products). The system supports cost journals,
allocations, and integration with operational modules. Cost centers and cost
objects are defined for granular tracking, and reporting tools provide insights
into cost behavior and profitability.

l3Processes.title: Revalue inventory
l3Processes.description:
Inventory revaluation in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows
organizations to adjust the value of inventory items based on market conditions,
cost changes, or accounting policies. The process supports periodic and ad-hoc
revaluations, with audit trails and approval workflows. Adjustments impact both
inventory and financial ledgers, ensuring accurate financial reporting.

l3Processes.title: Correct ledger vouchers
l3Processes.description:
Correction of ledger vouchers in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
involves identifying and amending erroneous entries in the general ledger. The
system provides tools for reversing, adjusting, or reclassifying vouchers, with
full audit trails and approval workflows. Corrections are subject to security
controls and can be linked to original transactions for traceability.

l3Processes.title: Manage accruals
l3Processes.description:
Accrual management in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enables
organizations to recognize expenses and revenues in the appropriate accounting
periods, even if cash has not been exchanged. The system automates accrual
calculations, posting, and reversal, with configurable schedules and approval
workflows. Accruals are integrated with subledgers and financial reporting.

l3Processes.title: Manage deferrals
l3Processes.description:
Deferral management in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps allows
organizations to postpone recognition of income or expenses to future periods.
The system supports automated deferral schedules, posting, and reversal, with
audit trails and approval workflows. Deferrals are integrated with revenue
recognition and expense management modules.

l3Processes.title: Process allocations
l3Processes.description:
Allocations in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enable organizations to
distribute costs or revenues across accounts, departments, or projects based on
predefined rules. The system supports both fixed and variable allocations, with
automated posting and audit trails. Allocation rules can be configured for
recurring or one-time distributions.

l3Processes.title: Record tax commitments
l3Processes.description:
Tax commitment recording in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
capturing tax liabilities arising from business transactions. The system
supports multiple tax types (sales, use, VAT, etc.), automates calculation and
posting, and provides compliance reporting. Tax codes, rates, and jurisdictions
are configurable, and workflows ensure review and approval.

l3Processes.title: Pay taxes
l3Processes.description:
Paying taxes in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves processing
payments for tax liabilities to relevant authorities. The system supports
payment scheduling, remittance, and reconciliation, with audit trails and
approval workflows. Payment methods and bank integrations are configurable,
ensuring secure and compliant transactions.

l3Processes.title: Finalize and post transactions
l3Processes.description:
In Dynamics 365 Finance, the "Finalize and post transactions" process involves
reviewing, validating, and posting financial transactions to the general ledger.
This ensures that all entries are accurate, complete, and comply with internal
controls. The process typically includes preparatory steps such as
documentation, approval workflows, and segregation of duties, followed by
posting transactions to the ledger. Once posted, transactions become part of the
official financial records and cannot be altered without appropriate audit
trails. Security roles and workflow configurations help enforce approval before
posting, supporting compliance and auditability.

l3Processes.title: Reconcile ledger and subledger
l3Processes.description:
The reconciliation process in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps ensures
that balances in the general ledger align with those in subledgers (e.g.,
accounts payable, accounts receivable). This process involves extracting reports
from both sources, matching transactions, and resolving discrepancies. Automated
tools and reconciliation modules can streamline certification, while manual
reconciliation remains available for complex cases. Integration with third-party
reconciliation tools is supported for enhanced functionality.

l3Processes.title: Revalue currency
l3Processes.description:
Currency revaluation in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps is used to
adjust foreign currency balances to reflect current exchange rates at period
end. The system calculates unrealized gains and losses, posts adjustment
entries, and ensures compliance with accounting standards. This process is
typically automated, with configuration options for exchange rate sources and
posting accounts.

l3Processes.title: Perform ledger settlements
l3Processes.description:
Ledger settlements in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involve matching
debit and credit transactions within the general ledger to clear offsetting
entries and reduce account noise. This process helps maintain clean account
balances and simplifies reconciliation. The system provides forms and tools for
transaction matching, with audit trails for all settlements.

l3Processes.title: Consolidate and eliminate financials
l3Processes.description:
Consolidation and elimination in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enable
organizations to combine financial results from multiple legal entities,
eliminating intercompany transactions to produce consolidated financial
statements. The process supports multi-entity structures, automated
eliminations, and compliance with reporting standards. Integration with planning
and budgeting modules is available.

l3Processes.title: Prepare financial statements
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps provides tools for preparing financial
statements such as balance sheets, income statements, and cash flow statements.
The Financial Reporter module enables users to design, generate, and distribute
statements, supporting compliance and audit requirements. Statements can be
exported for external reporting and integrated with other systems.

l3Processes.title: Close periods
l3Processes.description:
Closing periods in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves locking
financial modules after all transactions are posted and reconciled. This
prevents further changes and ensures data integrity. The process includes
validation steps, trial balance tie-outs, and sign-off workflows. Periods can be
reopened with appropriate permissions, and audit trails are maintained.

l3Processes.title: Audit financial transactions
l3Processes.description:
Effectively reporting and analyzing financials and cash flow is at the heart of
informed decision-making for organizations. In this dynamic landscape, Dynamics
365 offers a comprehensive suite of tools and functionalities to streamline this
critical process. From configuring cash flow forecasting to running financial
statements, the platform empowers finance professionals to delve deep into
financial data, derive meaningful insights, and make data-driven decisions. This
journey encompasses a series of strategic steps, including configuring cash flow
forecasting, refining prediction models, analyzing cash position, predicting
customer payments, scrutinizing financial trends, detecting anomalies, analyzing
budget to actuals, designing financial statements, running reports, and
distributing financial insights. This holistic approach ensures that
organizations not only have a clear understanding of their current financial
health but also gain valuable foresight to navigate future financial landscapes
effectively.
Reporting and analyzing the financials and cash flow within Dynamics 365 is a
strategic process. Beginning with the configuration of cash flow forecasting,
organizations can refine and improve prediction models to anticipate future
financial scenarios. The platform facilitates real-time analysis of the cash
position, enabling proactive liquidity management. Utilizing advanced prediction
tools, Dynamics 365 helps forecast customer payments, optimize cash inflows, and
support effective credit management. The system's analytical capabilities come
into play to identify trends in financial data, while anomaly detection features
help promptly address irregularities. With a focus on budget-to-actual analysis,
Dynamics 365 enables comparisons that yield insights for strategic adjustments.
The platform's versatility extends to the design and generation of comprehensive
financial statements, providing accurate and timely reports. Throughout this
process, Dynamics 365 empowers finance professionals to view, analyze, and
distribute financial statements, ensuring a comprehensive approach to financial
reporting and analysis that goes beyond compliance to enable proactive
decision-making.
In this dynamic process, Dynamics 365 acts as a catalyst, transforming financial
data into actionable insights. From configuring cash flow forecasting to
dissecting individual transactions, the platform equips organizations to
navigate the complexities of financial reporting and analysis with precision and
agility. This journey not only ensures compliance with reporting standards but
also empowers finance professionals to proactively shape the financial
trajectory of their organizations. The reporting and analyzing configurations
and business processes are typically left until later phases of a Dynamics 365
implementation to allow other configurations and data to be stabilized to ensure
the reporting and testing is accurate.

l3Processes.title: Analyze costs
l3Processes.description:
Cost analysis in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps leverages integrated
financial and operational data to provide insights into direct and indirect
costs. Users can analyze costs by project, department, or account, using
built-in reporting tools and Power BI dashboards. The system supports variance
analysis, cost allocation, and reconciliation between job cost and general
ledger accounts. Automated workflows can be set up for cost review and approval,
and the platform enables proactive monitoring through real-time analytics.

l3Processes.title: Monitor budget to actuals
l3Processes.description:
Monitoring budget to actuals in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
involves comparing planned budgets against actual financial performance. The
system supports multiple budget versions, forecasts, and real-time variance
analysis. Users can set up budget approval workflows, track changes, and
generate reports to identify deviations. Integration with Power BI enables
advanced analytics and visualization, while configurable alerts notify
stakeholders of significant variances.

l3Processes.title: Analyze financial statements
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps provides tools for preparing and
analyzing financial statements, including balance sheets, income statements, and
cash flow statements. The Financial Reporter tool enables users to design,
generate, and distribute statements, with drill-down capabilities for detailed
analysis. The system supports multi-entity consolidation, automated report
scheduling, and compliance with external audit standards.

l3Processes.title: Audit financial results
l3Processes.description:
Auditing financial results in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps involves
reviewing finalized financial statements and ensuring compliance with internal
policies and external regulations. The platform provides audit trails, approval
workflows, and reporting tools to support both internal and external audits.
Users can track changes, document findings, and generate audit reports for
management and auditors.

l3Processes.title: Monitor financial ratios and metrics
l3Processes.description:
Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps enables monitoring of key financial
ratios and metrics through configurable dashboards and reports. Users can track
liquidity, profitability, efficiency, and leverage ratios, with real-time data
updates and trend analysis. Power BI integration allows for advanced
visualization and benchmarking against industry standards.

l3Processes.title: Monitor regulatory compliance
l3Processes.description:
Regulatory compliance monitoring in Dynamics 365 finance and operations apps
involves tracking adherence to financial regulations, tax laws, and industry
standards. The system provides compliance checklists, automated alerts, and
audit trails to ensure all requirements are met. Users can configure compliance
workflows, generate regulatory reports, and integrate with external compliance
systems.

l3Processes.title: Implement cloud solutions
l3Processes.description:
Recommended practices:  Transform your business processes with SaaS applications and features that add value right away and let you improve continuously.  Don't recreate your legacy on-premises user experiences in the cloud.
Make sure everyone involved, from the executive sponsor to the developers, knows the business impact of the solution.  Use DevOps and CI/CD pipelines to automate your build, testing, and deployment.  Design your solution within the service boundaries and licensing capacity, and know how to expand and scale it if needed.  Design your solution to meet the nonfunctional requirements, such as network latency, user environment, and devices.  If you move an on-premises solution to the cloud, make sure your data model, design, and quality are good enough for the cloud.  Don't bring over poor design or data that could slow down your cloud adoption.  Know the value and impact of each extension you make to the application. For example, how does it affect data-driven decision making, efficiency, automation, and user adoption?  Follow the guidelines for when and how to customize and extend the out-of-the-box apps.  Only use the documented extension techniques.  Don't change or repurpose the out-of-the-box tables and data models. You could lose the ability to use new features and capabilities in the future.  Make sure you have the expertise you need to support and evolve your solution after deployment. You can use a partner (system integrator) or your own teams.  Stay connected with the community to keep up with the latest innovation and influence the product direction. You can use channels, events, blogs, and other mediums.

l3Processes.title: Drive app value
l3Processes.description: Recommended practices:  Consider the various change streams that will affect the project and adjust your budget and timelines accordingly.  Create an effective minimum viable product (MVP) scope that goes beyond the bare minimum to speed up value realization.  Adopt the latest capabilities to deliver continuous innovation to the business.

l3Processes.title: Implement process governance
l3Processes.description: Recommended practices:  Make sure you put the business process view of your organization at the center of your project definition.  Clearly describe the key business processes and the personas involved in them that are in scope for your project so that everyone understands them.  Make sure you include business model analysis, process engineering, and standardization strategies in your project definition. This way, you can establish a strong process baseline before you start the implementation.  Collect your business processes in a structured and hierarchical process catalog during the requirements phase.  Use business processes for each phase of your project to achieve better outcomes—all phase activities are better planned, executed, and measured.  Look for opportunities to improve, streamline, and consolidate your processes as part of the implementation to meet your digital transformation goals and drive user adoption.  Make sure you complete your business process definition and include all activities and subprocesses.  Use the latest software as a service (SaaS) technology to increase the efficiency and effectiveness of your process optimization.  Make sure you prepare for the future when you map your business process to your solution by incorporating configurability by design.  Use a fit-to-standard approach and follow the principle of adopting the standard processes wherever possible and adapting them only where necessary.

l3Processes.title: Define environment strategy
l3Processes.description: Recommended practices:  Consider the future growth of your solution and how it will affect your environment and tenant needs.  Support your application lifecycle management (ALM) processes and automation with your environment and tenant strategy.  Consider the short- and long-term impact of your environment and tenant strategy on licensing, compliance, application design, performance, scalability, maintainability, and ALM of your solution.  Assess the impact of adding more apps on your data estate. Avoid too much fragmentation and reuse existing integrations and data flows as much as possible.

l3Processes.title: Implement project governance approach
l3Processes.description: Recommended practices:  Intentional and customized communication to stakeholders

l3Processes.title: Manage organizational change
l3Processes.description:
Recommended practices:
Explain limitations of demos or proofs of concept (POCs)
Take note of challenges identified during presales
Create awareness of the project and coming changes
Use familiar data and day-in-the-life processes
Set realistic performance expectations
Break down common technology barriers
Break down people and process barriers
Take the chance out of change

l3Processes.title: Manage data
l3Processes.description:
Recommended practices:
Create, maintain, update, and test a configuration plan throughout the project. The plan should include all the required configuration data that you import to support the go-live phase.
Maximize the data throughput during migration by following best practices.
Optimize for network latency by staging in the cloud and batching requests.
Store only the necessary data in the app for the key processes that interact with it. Choose the right type of data store based on the usage.
Assess your data quality realistically and estimate the efforts required to perform the cleanup.
Ensure that the apps have the validations and controls to enforce data quality and that you have processes to measure and report on it.
Maintain high-quality data by following the principles in this article and by having leadership drive the habit of managing data on an ongoing basis.

l3Processes.title: Implement application lifecycle management (ALM) strategies
l3Processes.description:
Recommended practices:  Set up your ALM strategy at the start of your project. Align your ALM strategy with your environments, source control, implementation methodology, and automation strategy.
Make sure your ALM strategy is efficient, reliable, robust, and automated. Your ALM strategy should let you deploy your configurations and customizations smoothly across different environments, including any peripheral solution components.
Use a source control-centric approach for your ALM strategy. Source control helps you create new environments with the latest configurations without having to replicate existing environments.
Consider your implementation team's structure. Your team structure should allow for effective collaboration and give developers exclusive control where needed.
Follow best practices for ALM and for each app. Download the white paper Solution Lifecycle Management: Dynamics 365 to learn more about ALM best practices and recommendations for each Dynamics 365 app.

l3Processes.title: Define usability strategy
l3Processes.description:
Recommended practices:
 * Understand the app-specific security constructs and model your security based
   on the native access control mechanisms rather than customizing the build.
 * Understand that hiding information from the view doesn’t necessarily remove
   access—there are alternate mechanisms to access and extract information.
 * Understand the impact of losing the security context when the data is
   exported.

l3Processes.title: Define business intelligence, reporting, and analytics strategy
Define integration strategy
l3Processes.description:
Recommended practices:
Make sure the integration supports the organization's short-term and long-term goals.
Make sure that everyone involved knows the purpose and scope of the integration.
Help the organization understand the difference between cloud and on-premises platforms and where they connect.
Make sure that the integration platform or middleware can monitor, audit, notify, and alert as needed.
Make sure that the integration platform or middleware has the expected level of security, availability, and disaster recovery.
Make sure that all parts of the integration platform or middleware can use application lifecycle management (ALM) and version control tools.
Make sure that each integration design fits with the overall integration architecture.
Avoid sending or storing data that isn't needed in the solution.
Design integrations that use reliable, asynchronous messages to exchange data.
Choose a pattern that matches the expected amount, frequency, and service protection limitations of data exchange.
Determine realistic estimates of how much it will cost to run the services, platforms, and storage involved and how it will change over time.
Manage any changes that are related to integrations in a way that supports the business goals.

l3Processes.title: Define performance strategy
l3Processes.description:
Recommended practices:
Follow the service boundaries when you design, develop, and test for performance.
Include performance as part of the requirement conversation. Document specific goals that reflect the customer's needs and expectations.
Track your performance goals through future change requests. Don't let them slip away as you add new features or fix bugs.
Understand that user perceptions of performance are the result of many factors, such as network latency, device performance, browser add-ins, firewalls, and proxies. Test your solution in the actual user environment and location.
Make sure your performance testing considers realistic loads and usage scenarios. Don't rely on synthetic data or ideal conditions.

l3Processes.title: Prepare to go live
l3Processes.description:
Recommended practices:
Complete all activities in sequence.
Meet the exit criteria.
Consider the following items:
The business processes that the solution supports
The applications and solutions that are used in the solution
Whether the solution contains customizations and ISV solutions
The type and volume of integrations
The number of go-lives or rollouts
The number of users at go-live and in future rollouts"
Assess change readiness early in the project and reassess throughout.
Deliver effective training to users on how to use the new solution.
Engage users with the solution and get their input and feedback.
Communicate the value of the new solution with business sponsorship.
Create a community of super-users and provide resources for user support.

l3Processes.title: Define business continuity objectives
l3Processes.description:
Business continuity planning stands as a cornerstone in the realm of
organizational resilience, ensuring the seamless operation of critical business
functions in the face of unforeseen disruptions. At its core, business
continuity planning encompasses a comprehensive framework designed to identify
potential risks, devise preventive measures, and establish protocols for swift
recovery. In the dynamic landscape of today's business environment, where
digital transformation is the norm, a robust business continuity plan becomes
not just a best practice but a strategic imperative.
When it comes to cloud solutions, such as Dynamics 365, the stakes are higher,
and the need for a well-defined business continuity plan becomes even more
pronounced. Cloud solutions are the backbone of modern businesses and power
essential processes and workflows. Any downtime or data loss can have
far-reaching consequences, affecting not only operational efficiency but also
customer trust. All of that makes it paramount to understand that business
continuity means everything during an implementation project, particularly in
the implementation of cloud solutions such as Dynamics 365. Business continuity
is essential for safeguarding the integrity of critical business operations.
In the realm of Dynamics 365, a successful business continuity plan is more than
a safety net; it's a strategic investment. Navigating the nuances of this
powerful platform requires a tailored approach to risk mitigation and recovery.
As Dynamics 365 becomes an integral part of your project, considerations for
data backup, system monitoring, and failover mechanisms become pivotal. While
Microsoft protects and manages your SaaS components that are managed Microsoft
tenant, it's still important to consider other components that connect with or
are used alongside your Dynamics 365 components.
In this article, we'll explore the types of tools and options that organizations
should weigh when crafting a business continuity plan specific to Dynamics 365
implementations. From Azure-based solutions to integration with third-party
tools, we delve into the key considerations that elevate your project's
resilience and ensure the uninterrupted flow of business processes.
The processes we have defined in our business process catalog for business
continuity planning encompasses a strategic framework with defined processes to
ensure organizational resilience. Initiatives begin with the establishment of
clear objectives, followed by a comprehensive Business Impact Analysis to assess
potential disruptions and prioritize recovery efforts. The identification of
risks, development of a detailed plan, and its subsequent implementation form
the core of the strategy. Regular testing and validation refine the plan's
effectiveness, while ongoing maintenance ensures its relevance through updates
reflecting changes in technology, processes, and risks. Concurrently, critical
systems and data are identified and prioritized.
A Disaster Risk Assessment informs recovery objectives, leading to the
formulation and implementation of a comprehensive Disaster Recovery Plan.
Testing, validation, and continuous updates enhance the plan's adaptability to
evolving threats and technologies, ultimately ensuring an organization's
preparedness and resilience in the face of unforeseen disruptions.
Integrating business continuity planning into a Dynamics 365 implementation
project is crucial for fostering a resilient operational environment. Ideally,
consideration for business continuity planning should commence during the
initial stages of project planning. As project objectives are defined and
critical business processes identified, it becomes opportune to evaluate
potential risks and plan for disruptions. Throughout the implementation
lifecycle, continuous assessment and refinement of the business continuity plan
should occur alongside the development and deployment of Dynamics 365 solutions.
By weaving business continuity planning into the fabric of the implementation
process from the outset, organizations can proactively address risks, safeguard
critical systems, and ensure a robust framework for continuity, thereby
fortifying the success and sustainability of their Dynamics 365 project.

l3Processes.title: Test and validate the business continuity plan
Maintain and update the business continuity plan
Test and validate the disaster recovery plan
Mitigate and update the disaster recovery plan
Take inventory on hardware and devices
Configure and monitor system generated numbers
Configure and maintain electronically generated documents
Configure and manage offline mode for apps
Configure and manage mobile apps and devices
Configure and management office apps and add-ins
Configure and manage reporting and analytics
Configure and manage Microsoft Teams integrations
Configure and manage geofencing and geolocation settings
Develop training strategy
l3Processes.description: Recommended practices:  Have training objectives that are SMART (specific, measurable, achievable, realistic, and timely).  Make them relevant to your organization's goal of user adoption, and make them fit the organization's overall change management goals.  Have a process for incrementally updating the training plan as necessary to reflect scope, changes, risks, and dependencies, to ensure adoption and engagement.  Consider what to include as far as system processes and business processes, so that the training provides the best possible foundation to system users.  Ensure that the training schedule accounts for key project milestones, resource requirements and availability, dates, and tasks, and that it's aligned with the overall project plan.  Consider several types of content, the time to create it, and the effort to deliver and maintain it.  Cover how you store the content, and the skill set of the authors and trainers.  Ensure that trainers and super users are trained well in advance of system user training. Ideally, this training should occur before user acceptance testing (UAT).  Ensure that the training plan appropriately addresses accessibility.  Identify a process to provide for continuous training in alignment with Microsoft updates and changes to the solution, and also with changes in roles and responsibilities.

l3Processes.title: Identify training needs
l3Processes.description:
Recommended practices: 
 * Have training objectives that are SMART (specific, measurable, achievable,
   realistic, and timely). 
 * Make them relevant to your organization's goal of user adoption, and make
   them fit the organization's overall change management goals. 
 * Have a process for incrementally updating the training plan as necessary to
   reflect scope, changes, risks, and dependencies, to ensure adoption and
   engagement. 
 * Consider what to include as far as system processes and business processes,
   so that the training provides the best possible foundation to system users. 
 * Ensure that the training schedule accounts for key project milestones,
   resource requirements and availability, dates, and tasks, and that it's
   aligned with the overall project plan. 
 * Consider several types of content, the time to create it, and the effort to
   deliver and maintain it. 
 * Cover how you store the content, and the skill set of the authors and
   trainers. 
 * Ensure that trainers and super users are trained well in advance of system
   user training. Ideally, this training should occur before user acceptance
   testing (UAT). 
 * Ensure that the training plan appropriately addresses accessibility. 
 * Identify a process to provide for continuous training in alignment with
   Microsoft updates and changes to the solution, and also with changes in roles
   and responsibilities.

l3Processes.title: Clean up and view log storage
Handle background job errors and exceptions
Analyze background job performance and history
Clean up and archive background jobs
Migrate to new versions of software
Manage the initial synchronization of data
Manage the recurring synchronization of data
Identify applicable regulations and compliance requirements
Develop support transition strategy
l3Processes.description:
Recommended practices:
Consider how supporting a cloud and SaaS application affects your support model and whether you need a Dynamics 365 Center of Excellence (CoE).
Have a clear strategy to manage the critical period after going live (hypercare) with specific criteria for starting and ending it.
